Project {
  database_type: 'Databricks Unity Catalog'
  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Genomics Biotech - Version 1'
}

Table sequencing.sequencing_run {
  sequencing_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) run record in the Databricks Silver layer. Primary key for the sequencing_run entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — this entity represents a discrete NGS sequencing execution event with a full operational lifecycle.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Sequencing runs performed as service for customer accounts in contract research/service lab operations. Essential for billing, SLA tracking, regulatory compliance, and customer-specific data segregati']
  accreditation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.accreditation. Business justification: Sequencing runs performed under CLIA/CAP-accredited workflows must be traceable to the customer\'s active accreditation record for regulatory audit, inspection readiness, and clinical reporting complia']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: IVD/clinical sequencing runs must be traceable to the regulatory approval (e.g., 510(k), CE-IVD) authorizing their use. GxP-regulated labs require this for audit trails and compliance reporting. \'regu']
  assay_development_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.assay_development. Business justification: Sequencing runs executed as assay development validation runs must be traceable to the assay_development record. Assay development teams track run performance against target coverage_depth and technol']
  assay_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.assay_version. Business justification: CLIA labs must document which assay version governed each sequencing run. The assay_version defines minimum_coverage_depth, minimum_phred_score, and sequencing_platform acceptance criteria applied to ']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Reagent kit batch traceability for sequencing runs is required for supplier recall management and lot-level QC investigations. sequencing_run has reagent_kit_lot_id (reagent domain) but no FK to the p']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.capa. Business justification: Systematic sequencing run failures (repeated QC failures, instrument issues, process deviations) trigger CAPAs. Labs must link runs to CAPAs for effectiveness checks, trend analysis, and regulatory co']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Technical contact for run-specific communications: QC notifications, result delivery, troubleshooting. Critical for customer-facing service operations and regulatory traceability of data delivery.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Sequencing runs frequently trigger quality deviations (failed QC, instrument errors, contamination events). GxP labs must link runs to deviations for investigation, root cause analysis, and regulatory']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: Sequencing runs generate data for specific experiments. Tracking this link enables experiment-level data aggregation, cost allocation to R&D experiments, and traceability from raw sequencing output to']
  installed_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.installed_instrument. Business justification: A sequencing run executes on a specific customer-installed instrument. This link is essential for instrument utilization reporting, warranty/service tracking, and SLA compliance — a genomics ops team ']
  lab_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.lab_site. Business justification: Multi-site genomics labs require run-to-site traceability for proficiency testing, regulatory submissions (CLIA/CAP site-specific reporting), and inter-site QC comparisons. A genomics operations exper']
  license_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.license_entitlement. Business justification: Each sequencing run consumes software license entitlements (BaseSpace compute, DRAGEN analysis, data_processing_limit_gb). License compliance reporting and usage-based billing require runs to referenc']
  manufacturing_production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Direct traceability from sequencing run to manufactured reagent batch enables rapid quality investigations. When Q30 scores drop or error rates spike, QC teams must immediately identify affected manuf']
  manufacturing_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_order. Business justification: Vertically-integrated genomics companies manufacture custom reagent kits in-house. Sequencing runs must trace to manufacturing work orders for regulatory compliance (21 CFR Part 11, ISO 13485), qualit']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.model. Business justification: Sequencing runs are executed on a specific instrument model determining chemistry, read length capability, flow cell compatibility, and regulatory classification. GxP audit trails, throughput reportin']
  performing_laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.performing_laboratory. Business justification: Each sequencing run is physically performed in a CLIA-certified performing laboratory. CAP/CLIA compliance requires every run to be traceable to the performing lab for inspection, proficiency testing,']
  primary_sequencing_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing instrument (e.g., NovaSeq, NextSeq, HiSeq) on which this run was executed. Used to trace run performance back to a specific physical instrument asset.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Sequencing runs consume reagent kits purchased via PO. Link enables cost accounting per run, supplier performance tracking, and GMP traceability required for regulated assays. Critical for COGS calcul']
  reagent_kit_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.catalog. Business justification: Reagent kit compatibility validation and recall management require linking each sequencing run to the reagent.catalog entry to access storage requirements, GMP compliance status, and shelf-life specs.']
  reagent_kit_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Sequencing runs consume specific reagent kit catalog items. Essential for cost-per-run analysis, inventory management, regulatory lot traceability, and procurement planning. Labs must track which comm']
  reagent_kit_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reagent kit lot (LOT number) consumed during this sequencing run. Enables traceability from run output back to reagent manufacturing lot for quality investigations and COA verification.']
  reagent_kit_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Inventory depletion and COGS reporting require the specific SKU (pack size, regional config) consumed per run, not just the catalog item. Procurement reorder triggers and run-level cost accounting in ']
  regulatory_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.regulatory_classification. Business justification: IVD vs RUO classification of a sequencing run determines GxP requirements, data handling, and regulatory reporting obligations. The plain column regulatory_use_classification is denormalized from prod']
  sales_order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.header. Business justification: A sequencing run executed as a service must reference the sales order that commissioned it. This is fundamental traceability in genomics service labs — linking lab execution to commercial order for bi']
  sequencing_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_protocol. Business justification: Sequencing runs must follow approved, validated sequencing protocols that define run configuration, QC thresholds, and regulatory compliance requirements. sequencing_run currently has run_mode, run_ty']
  service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_agreement. Business justification: Runs performed under specific service agreements defining SLA terms, turnaround time, pricing tier, regulatory classification. Essential for contract compliance verification and billing.']
  service_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.service_offering. Business justification: Sequencing-as-a-service runs must reference the service offering for billing, SLA compliance, and deliverables tracking. Contract research organizations and genomics service labs link each run to the ']
  software_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.software_release. Business justification: Regulatory submissions (FDA, CLIA) and reproducibility require tracing which basecalling/RTA software version executed each run. GxP-regulated sequencing environments mandate software version traceabi']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Run QC pass/fail decisions are made against product specifications (min Q30, min yield, coverage depth thresholds). In CLIA/CAP-regulated labs, the specification version the run was validated against ']
  technical_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.technical_requirement. Business justification: Runs executed to fulfill customer-specified technical requirements (coverage depth, read length, platform). Links run parameters to customer engagement specifications for QC validation and acceptance ']
  test_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_catalog. Business justification: A sequencing run is executed to fulfill a specific clinical test catalog entry. CLIA labs must document which test (CPT code, TAT requirement, assay type) each run was performed for, enabling billing,']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Sequencing runs are used in validation studies (assay validation, instrument qualification, method verification, software validation). Labs must link validation runs to study records for regulatory su']
  actual_sample_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of samples successfully demultiplexed and yielding usable FASTQ data from this run. May differ from planned_sample_count due to index mismatches, low-quality libraries, or run failures. Key metric for run efficiency and yield reporting.']
  basespace_run_code varchar [note: 'The unique run identifier assigned by Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub for this sequencing run. Used to link the internal enterprise record to the BaseSpace cloud platform for FASTQ retrieval, run monitoring, and bioinformatics pipeline initiation.']
  cluster_density decimal(10,2) [note: 'The density of sequencing clusters generated on the flow cell surface, measured in thousands of clusters per mm². A critical run quality parameter — too low indicates under-loading, too high causes cluster overlap and quality degradation. Reported by the instrument and BaseSpace.']
  cluster_pf_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of clusters that pass the Illumina chastity filter (PF = Passing Filter), indicating the proportion of reads with sufficient signal purity for reliable base calling. A key run-level QC metric; typically expected to be >75-80% for a passing run.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sequencing run record was first created in the enterprise data system (LIMS or BaseSpace ingestion). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED timestamp for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity. Distinct from run_start_timestamp which reflects the physical run start. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_retention_category varchar [note: 'Data retention classification for this sequencing run record and its associated FASTQ/BAM/VCF outputs, determining the minimum retention period per applicable regulations. \'standard\' = 2 years (RUO); \'extended\' = 10+ years (clinical/GxP); \'permanent\' = indefinite (regulatory submission data); \'restricted_deletion\' = deletion requires regulatory approval.']
  demultiplexing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the FASTQ demultiplexing process for this run, which separates pooled (multiplexed) sample reads by their index barcodes into individual sample FASTQ files. Tracks the post-run data processing step that precedes bioinformatics pipeline execution.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sequencing run completed (final cycle finished or run terminated). Used with run_start_timestamp to calculate run duration and TAT. Null if run is still in progress or queued. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  error_rate_pct decimal(5,3) [note: 'The base-level sequencing error rate for this run expressed as a percentage, calculated using PhiX control spike-in alignment. Lower values indicate higher sequencing accuracy. Used for run QC acceptance/rejection decisions and instrument performance trending.']
  fastq_output_path varchar [note: 'The storage path or URI (e.g., S3 bucket path, BaseSpace project URL, or HPC filesystem path) where the FASTQ output files for this run are stored. Confidential as it reveals internal data infrastructure topology. Used by bioinformatics pipelines to locate raw sequencing data.']
  flow_cell_type varchar [note: 'The type/format of flow cell used in this run (e.g., SP, S1, S2, S4 for NovaSeq; P1, P2, P3 for NovaSeq X; nano, micro for MiSeq). Determines lane count, cluster capacity, and maximum output yield. Critical for run planning and cost accounting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SP|S1|S2|S4|P1|P2|P3|nano|micro|high|mid — promote to reference product]']
  folder_name varchar [note: 'The instrument-generated run folder name following Illumina naming convention (e.g., \'240115_A00001_0001_AHXXXXXX\'). Contains date, instrument serial, run number, and flow cell ID. Used for data traceability between instrument output and enterprise data systems.']
  index_read_length varchar [note: 'Number of base pairs sequenced for the index (barcode) reads used in multiplexing/demultiplexing. Typically 6, 8, or 10 bp per index. Determines the number of samples that can be multiplexed on a single flow cell and the accuracy of demultiplexing.']
  is_paired_end boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this run was configured as paired-end (True) or single-read (False) sequencing. Paired-end sequencing generates reads from both ends of a DNA fragment, improving alignment accuracy and variant detection. Critical parameter for bioinformatics pipeline configuration.']
  lane_count varchar [note: 'Number of lanes on the flow cell used in this sequencing run (e.g., 1 for MiSeq, 2 for NextSeq, 4 for NovaSeq SP/S1/S2, 8 for NovaSeq S4). Used for per-lane QC analysis, sample distribution planning, and run capacity calculations.']
  library_prep_kit varchar [note: 'Name or catalog identifier of the library preparation kit used to prepare samples for this sequencing run (e.g., \'Illumina DNA Prep\', \'TruSeq Stranded mRNA\', \'Nextera DNA Flex\'). Affects sequencing performance, adapter trimming parameters, and bioinformatics pipeline configuration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or QC reviewer notes about this sequencing run, including observations about run performance, deviations from SOP, troubleshooting actions taken, or contextual information relevant to data interpretation. Supports GxP deviation documentation.']
  output_yield_gb decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total sequencing data output generated by this run measured in gigabases (Gb). Represents the QUANTITATIVE_RESULT for this non-monetary TRANSACTION_HEADER. Used for run performance benchmarking, storage capacity planning, and comparison against instrument specifications.']
  phix_spike_in_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of PhiX bacteriophage control library spiked into the sequencing run for real-time quality monitoring and error rate calibration. Typically 1-5% for standard runs. Used to calculate error_rate_pct and validate instrument performance.']
  planned_sample_count varchar [note: 'Number of samples planned to be multiplexed and sequenced in this run as defined in the sample sheet at run setup. Used for capacity planning, reagent consumption estimation, and comparison against actual demultiplexed sample count.']
  q30_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of bases with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (Q30), indicating ≥99.9% base call accuracy. The primary industry-standard run quality metric. Typically required to be >75-80% for a run to pass QC. Directly impacts variant calling accuracy and clinical reporting confidence.']
  qc_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the QC review of this sequencing run was completed and the run_qc_status was formally assigned. Part of the GxP electronic audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'The overall QC pass/fail determination for this sequencing run based on evaluation of run metrics (Q30%, cluster density, error rate, yield). \'pass\' = all metrics within specification; \'fail\' = one or more critical metrics out of specification; \'pending_review\' = awaiting manual QC review; \'conditional_pass\' = passed with noted deviations. Determines whether downstream FASTQ data is released for analysis.']
  read_length varchar [note: 'The number of base pairs sequenced per read in this run (e.g., 75, 100, 150, 250 bp). A key sequencing parameter that determines data quality, coverage, and application suitability. Configured at run setup and recorded for QC and reproducibility.']
  run_mode varchar [note: 'The NGS application type or sequencing strategy for this run. Determines the library preparation approach, bioinformatics pipeline, and downstream analysis. WGS = Whole Genome Sequencing; WES = Whole Exome Sequencing; targeted = targeted panel sequencing; amplicon = amplicon-based sequencing; RNA-seq = transcriptome sequencing; metagenomics = environmental/microbial sequencing.']
  run_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the sequencing run, typically following a lab naming convention (e.g., \'RUN-2024-001-NovaSeq\'). Used for identification in LIMS, BaseSpace, and operational dashboards. Maps to the Run Name field in Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the sequencing run, assigned by the LIMS or laboratory operations system. Used for cross-system referencing, customer reporting, and regulatory traceability. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sequencing run. Drives operational workflows, downstream pipeline triggering, and SLA monitoring. \'queued\' = scheduled but not started; \'in_progress\' = actively sequencing; \'completed\' = run finished successfully; \'failed\' = run terminated with errors; \'aborted\' = manually stopped; \'paused\' = temporarily halted. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the run by its intended use context. \'clinical\' = IVD/LDT clinical diagnostic run subject to CLIA/CAP; \'research\' = RUO research run; \'validation\' = assay validation run; \'QC\' = quality control run; \'development\' = R&D development run. Determines applicable regulatory framework and data handling requirements.']
  sample_sheet_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Illumina sample sheet file used to configure this sequencing run, specifying sample IDs, index sequences, and demultiplexing parameters. Used for run reproducibility, troubleshooting, and audit trail. Maps to Illumina SampleSheet.csv versioning.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sequencing run was initiated on the instrument (first cycle commenced). Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity. Used for TAT (Turnaround Time) calculation, SLA monitoring, and run scheduling. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  target_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'The intended mean sequencing coverage depth (number of times each base is sequenced) specified at run design time (e.g., 30x for WGS, 100x for WES, 500x for targeted panels). Used to validate whether the run met its coverage objectives and for clinical reporting thresholds.']
  total_cycles varchar [note: 'Total number of sequencing chemistry cycles executed in this run. For paired-end runs, this includes both read 1, index reads, and read 2 cycles (e.g., 2×150 bp = 300 cycles + index cycles). Used for run cost accounting, reagent consumption tracking, and run configuration validation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sequencing run record was last modified in the enterprise data system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED timestamp for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Core master record for each NGS sequencing run executed on an instrument. Captures run identity, instrument assignment, flow cell, run mode (WGS, WES, targeted), read length, paired-end configuration, run status (queued, in-progress, completed, failed), start/end timestamps, operator, reagent kit lot, BaseSpace run ID, total cycles, and run-level QC pass/fail outcome. This is the central operational entity of the sequencing domain — every downstream FASTQ, QC metric, and library result traces back to a sequencing run.'
}

Table sequencing.flow_cell {
  flow_cell_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each flow cell master record in the Genomics Biotech lakehouse. Primary key for the flow_cell data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Flow cells used in IVD-cleared assays must be traceable to the regulatory approval authorizing their use. This is a real supply/quality traceability requirement for clinical genomics labs. \'regulatory']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sequencing instrument on which this flow cell was or is currently loaded. Links to the instrument domain for instrument qualification records, maintenance history, and run association. Populated when status transitions to \'loaded\'.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Flow cells are batch-managed consumables. Link enables lot traceability, expiry tracking, recall management, and quality investigations correlating run failures to specific batches. Critical for GMP c']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Flow cells are catalog items with part_number, requiring linkage to reagent catalog for procurement, inventory management, and compatibility verification with instruments. New FK needed; removes denor']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Flow cells are consumable products with SKUs, pricing, and regulatory classifications. Critical for procurement, cost accounting, supply chain management, and compatibility validation with instruments']
  coa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.coa. Business justification: Flow cells require Certificate of Analysis verification for GxP-compliant sequencing runs, quality control, and regulatory audits. supplier_lot_certificate_number is a reference string, not a denormal']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Flow cells with cold_chain_breach_flag or QC failures require deviation records in GxP genomics labs. Direct FK enables traceability from a specific flow cell to the deviation raised against it — a st']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Flow cells are reagent consumables requiring lot-level traceability for recall management, expiry enforcement, and QC release status. The existing flow_cell FKs cover catalog and COA but not the speci']
  manufacturing_production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Vertically-integrated genomics companies manufacturing flow cells in-house require full batch traceability for regulatory compliance (ISO 13485, FDA QSR). Flow cell performance issues (low cluster den']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Flow cells are materials in supply chain. Link enables inventory management, MRP planning, demand forecasting, and integration with ERP material master for procurement, costing, and warehouse manageme']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Flow cells failing incoming QC inspection (qc_inspection_status present) require formal nonconformance records for material disposition under ISO 13485 / GxP material control. A genomics lab QA expert']
  qc_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Flow cells undergo incoming QC inspection (qc_inspection_status, qc_inspection_date on flow_cell). The formal QC result record must be traceable from the flow cell for ISO 13485 incoming material insp']
  regulatory_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.regulatory_classification. Business justification: Flow cells used in IVD applications require regulatory classification traceability for FDA/CE compliance. The plain column regulatory_use_classification is denormalized from product.regulatory_classif']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sequencing run associated with this flow cell. Links to the sequencing_run data product for run parameters, quality metrics, and FASTQ output tracking. A flow cell may be associated with at most one sequencing run in its lifecycle.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Flow cell inventory management requires SKU-level tracking (lane count variant, read length configuration). Procurement reorder points and lot-level COGS accounting depend on the specific SKU consumed']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Flow cell QC inspection compares actual performance against product specifications. Columns cluster_density_spec_mm2, max_output_gb, read_length_spec are denormalized from product.specification. Linki']
  basespace_flow_cell_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned to this flow cell within the Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub system. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the internal lakehouse record and the BaseSpace sequencing data management platform. Used for data lineage and run data retrieval.']
  cold_chain_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion or cold chain breach was detected during shipping or storage of this flow cell (True = breach detected). Triggers quarantine workflow and quality investigation in LabVantage LIMS and Veeva Vault. A breached flow cell must not be used until cleared by QA.']
  consumed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sequencing run was completed and the flow cell was fully consumed or removed from the instrument. Marks the end of the flow cell\'s active lifecycle. Used for TAT reporting, instrument utilization metrics, and inventory depletion tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the flow cell master record was first created in the Genomics Biotech lakehouse, typically at the point of goods receipt registration. Supports audit trail requirements under GxP, 21 CFR Part 11, and SOX internal controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit_cost field (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency financial reporting and cost normalization across global procurement activities.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date on which the consumed, failed, or expired flow cell was disposed of per the applicable disposal method. Required for environmental compliance records and GMP documentation of consumable lifecycle closure.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used for disposal of the consumed or failed flow cell. Governs environmental health and safety (EHS) compliance for waste management. Options include standard laboratory waste, biohazardous waste, chemical waste, or return to supplier for failed/defective units.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Manufacturer-specified expiration date after which the flow cell must not be used for sequencing. Critical for GMP compliance, inventory management, and preventing use of expired consumables that could compromise sequencing quality. Monitored in SAP S/4HANA QM and LabVantage LIMS for automated expiry alerts.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Genomics Biotech facility (laboratory or sequencing center) where this flow cell is stored and used. Supports multi-site inventory management, site-level capacity planning, and regulatory site-specific compliance reporting.']
  flow_cell_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the physical flow cell consumable. Tracks the operational state from receipt through consumption or disposal. \'in_stock\' = received and available; \'reserved\' = allocated to a planned run; \'loaded\' = currently on instrument; \'consumed\' = run completed; \'failed\' = quality failure; \'expired\' = past expiry date; \'quarantined\' = held for investigation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_stock|reserved|loaded|consumed|failed|expired|quarantined — promote to reference product]']
  flow_cell_type varchar [note: 'Instrument-specific flow cell format designation indicating throughput tier and lane configuration (e.g., S1, S2, S4, SP for NovaSeq 6000; MID, HIGH for NextSeq 550; P1, P2, P3 for NovaSeq X). Determines maximum output (Gb), number of lanes, and cluster density specifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: S1|S2|S4|SP|MID|HIGH|P1|P2|P3 — promote to reference product]']
  instrument_platform varchar [note: 'The sequencing instrument platform for which this flow cell is designed and compatible (e.g., NovaSeq 6000, NovaSeq X, NextSeq 2000, MiSeq). Governs compatibility rules for loading, reagent kits, and run parameters. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NovaSeq 6000|NovaSeq X|NextSeq 550|NextSeq 2000|MiSeq|HiSeq 4000|iSeq 100 — promote to reference product]']
  intended_application varchar [note: 'Primary sequencing application for which this flow cell is designated (e.g., WGS — Whole Genome Sequencing, WES — Whole Exome Sequencing, targeted sequencing, RNA-seq, single-cell). Drives run parameter configuration and quality acceptance criteria. Also distinguishes Research Use Only (RUO) from In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) use contexts for regulatory compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_sequencing|amplicon|RNA_seq|single_cell|RUO|IVD — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_single_use boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the flow cell is designed for single use only (True) or supports multiple sequencing runs (False). Governs reuse policies, disposal procedures, and run scheduling logic. Most modern Illumina flow cells are single-use; this flag supports future platform compatibility.']
  lane_count varchar [note: 'Number of independent sequencing lanes on the flow cell (e.g., 1, 2, or 4 lanes for NovaSeq S1/S2/S4). Each lane can be loaded with a separate library pool, enabling multiplexing strategies. Drives capacity planning for sample batching and run scheduling.']
  lims_barcode varchar [note: 'Barcode label scanned into LabVantage LIMS at receipt to register the flow cell in the laboratory inventory system. Used for physical tracking, chain-of-custody documentation, and automated workflow triggering in the LIMS. May differ from the manufacturer serial number.']
  loaded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the flow cell was physically loaded onto the sequencing instrument, marking the transition from \'reserved\' to \'loaded\' status. Recorded in Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub and LabVantage LIMS. Used for run scheduling, instrument utilization analysis, and TAT (Turnaround Time) calculations.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the flow cell was manufactured by the supplier, as printed on the product label or Certificate of Analysis (COA). Used for shelf-life calculations, lot traceability, and quality investigations. Sourced from the COA accompanying the shipment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for laboratory personnel to record observations, anomalies, deviations, or special handling instructions associated with this flow cell. Examples include cold chain deviation details, physical damage observations, or QA hold reasons. Captured in LabVantage LIMS.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA purchase order number under which this flow cell was procured. Provides procurement lineage linking the physical consumable to the financial transaction, supplier contract, and goods receipt documentation. Supports COGS tracking and financial reconciliation.']
  qc_inspection_date date [note: 'Date on which the incoming QC inspection was performed for this flow cell. Used for compliance audit trails, SOP adherence tracking, and calculating time-from-receipt-to-clearance metrics.']
  qc_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the incoming quality control inspection performed upon receipt of the flow cell. \'pending\' = inspection not yet completed; \'passed\' = meets acceptance criteria; \'failed\' = does not meet criteria, quarantined; \'waived\' = inspection waived per approved SOP (e.g., for pre-qualified lots). Managed in SAP S/4HANA QM and LabVantage LIMS.']
  reagent_kit_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number of the sequencing reagent kit used in conjunction with this flow cell. Enables paired traceability between the flow cell consumable and its associated reagent kit for quality investigations, recall management, and GMP compliance. Sourced from LabVantage LIMS run setup records.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the flow cell was physically received at the Genomics Biotech facility and logged into inventory. Recorded in SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt and LabVantage LIMS. Used to calculate storage duration, validate shelf life, and support FIFO inventory management.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number printed on the physical flow cell cartridge. Used as the primary external identifier for traceability across LabVantage LIMS, Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub, and SAP S/4HANA inventory management. Enables one-to-one mapping between the physical consumable and its digital record.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the physical storage location (e.g., freezer unit, shelf, rack position) where the flow cell is currently stored within the facility. Managed in SAP S/4HANA MM Warehouse Management and LabVantage LIMS. Enables rapid physical retrieval and inventory audits.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius as specified by the manufacturer. Paired with storage_temperature_min_c to define the acceptable cold chain range. Monitored against environmental monitoring system data to ensure GMP-compliant storage conditions.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Minimum required storage temperature in degrees Celsius as specified by the manufacturer. Used to validate cold chain compliance during receipt inspection and ongoing storage monitoring. Deviations from this range trigger quarantine and quality investigation workflows.']
  supplier_lot_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (COA) issued by the supplier for this specific lot. The COA documents manufacturer QC test results confirming the flow cell meets specification. Stored in Veeva Vault for regulatory document management and GMP compliance.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external supplier or manufacturer who supplied this flow cell (e.g., Illumina, Inc.). Used for procurement lineage, supplier qualification tracking, and quality management. Linked to the reagent and consumable domain for full procurement traceability.']
  unit_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Actual procurement cost per flow cell unit in the operating currency (USD). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM invoice verification. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) allocation, run cost accounting, and financial reporting. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity of supplier pricing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the flow cell master record. Tracks status transitions, QC result updates, and any modifications to the record. Required for data lineage, GxP audit trails, and Silver Layer change tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical flow cell consumable loaded onto a sequencing instrument. Tracks flow cell serial number, part number, lot number, flow cell type (e.g., S1, S2, S4, SP for NovaSeq; MID, HIGH for NextSeq), number of lanes, cluster density specification, maximum output (Gb), expiry date, storage conditions, receipt date, and current status (in-stock, loaded, consumed, failed). Links to reagent domain for procurement lineage and to sequencing_run for usage tracking.'
}

Table sequencing.library {
  library_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the sequencing library record within the Genomics Biotech data platform. Primary key for the library master record. MASTER_RESOURCE role — serves as the SSOT for library identity within the sequencing domain.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Libraries prepared for customer samples in service/contract research. Essential for sample ownership tracking, billing, data access control, consent management, and regulatory compliance (PHI/GDPR).']
  accreditation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.accreditation. Business justification: Libraries prepared for clinical/diagnostic use must be traceable to the customer\'s CLIA/CAP accreditation for regulatory audit and IVD compliance. Clinical genomics labs require this traceability at t']
  aliquot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.aliquot. Business justification: A sequencing library is prepared from a specific aliquot. GMP chain-of-custody, freeze-thaw tracking, and QC failure investigations require knowing exactly which aliquot was used. Genomics LIMS expert']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: Libraries used in IVD/clinical workflows must be traceable to the regulatory approval authorizing the assay. Required for 21 CFR Part 820 device history records and ISO 13485 traceability. Genomics la']
  assay_development_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.assay_development. Business justification: Libraries prepared for assay development validation must link to the assay_development record. Assay development programs track library type, insert size, and prep kit requirements — all defined in as']
  assay_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.assay_version. Business justification: A library is prepared according to an assay version\'s library_preparation_protocol and quality thresholds. CLIA labs must document which assay version governed library preparation for regulatory audit']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Sample submitter/technical contact for library-specific communications: QC results, resequencing decisions, consent verification. Critical for customer-facing service labs and regulatory traceability.']
  container_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical plate or rack in which the library is stored. Used for laboratory inventory management, retrieval workflows, and storage location tracking within the LIMS.']
  extraction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.extraction. Business justification: Libraries are prepared from extracted nucleic acid. Extraction quality metrics (RIN, yield, purity) are direct inputs to library QC. Regulatory traceability (CAP/CLIA) and method validation require li']
  index_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.index_set. Business justification: Libraries are assigned index adapters from controlled index sets for multiplexing. library currently has adapter_sequence_i7, adapter_sequence_i5, and udi_plate_id but no FK to index_set. Adding index']
  library_prep_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.library_prep_run. Business justification: Each library is produced by a specific library preparation run. This is a standard parent-child relationship where library_prep_run is the batch execution entity and library is the output product. Cur']
  molecular_design_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.molecular_design. Business justification: Targeted sequencing libraries (capture panels, amplicon libraries) are built from specific molecular designs defining target loci and probe sequences. Linking library to molecular_design enables trace']
  multiplexing_pool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the multiplexing pool to which this library has been assigned for co-loading onto a flow cell. Enables tracking of pooled libraries, demultiplexing yield analysis, and per-library coverage attribution within a pooled run.']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Libraries failing QC (qc_status, qc_failure_reason on library) require nonconformance records for material disposition decisions in GxP genomics. Library already links to oos_investigation and qc_resu']
  oos_investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.oos_investigation. Business justification: Libraries with out-of-specification QC results (concentration out of range, wrong fragment size, high adapter dimer) trigger OOS investigations. Labs link investigations to specific libraries for root']
  original_library_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original library record when this library is a resequencing attempt (resequencing_flag = True). Enables lineage tracking across preparation attempts and supports failure analysis workflows. Null for original preparations.']
  prep_kit_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Library prep kits are commercial catalog items. Labs track which kit SKU was used for cost allocation, protocol validation, regulatory compliance, and troubleshooting quality issues. Essential for GxP']
  prep_kit_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Library prep kit SKU traceability is required for lot-level COGS accounting and inventory depletion. In regulated genomics labs, the specific kit SKU (pack size, configuration) used per library must b']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the research or clinical project under which this library was prepared. Supports project-level tracking, cost allocation, and reporting across the R&D and clinical genomics domains.']
  qc_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Libraries undergo QC testing (concentration via Qubit/qPCR, fragment size via Bioanalyzer/TapeStation, adapter dimer assessment). Each library has multiple QC results tracked for batch release, troubl']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample from which this library was prepared. Links the library to its upstream sample management record, enabling traceability from raw specimen through sequencing output.']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Library QC pass/fail is evaluated against product specifications (insert size range, PCR cycle count, reaction volume). For OOS investigations and CLIA compliance, the specification version the librar']
  technical_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.technical_requirement. Business justification: Libraries prepared to meet customer-specified technical requirements (coverage, insert size, prep method). Links library design to customer engagement specifications for acceptance criteria validation']
  test_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_order. Business justification: A library is prepared to fulfill a specific clinical test order. CLIA audit trails require direct traceability from library preparation to the clinical test order, enabling consent verification, TAT t']
  basespace_library_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned to this library within Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub. Enables cross-system traceability between the internal LIMS record and the BaseSpace sequencing data management platform for FASTQ retrieval and run association.']
  concentration_nm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured molar concentration of the library in nanomolar (nM) units. Used for accurate loading onto flow cells and pooling calculations during multiplexing. Sourced from quantification instrument output.']
  consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether valid informed consent has been obtained for the use of the source sample in sequencing and data sharing (True = consent confirmed; False = consent not confirmed or withdrawn). Required for HIPAA, GDPR, and NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the library record was first created in the system of record. Supports audit trail requirements, TAT calculations, and data lineage tracking. Aligns with FDA 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.']
  data_sharing_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether genomic data derived from this library is subject to data sharing restrictions (True = restricted; False = unrestricted). Restrictions may arise from consent limitations, IRB conditions, contractual obligations, or GDPR/HIPAA data subject rights. Governs downstream data product access controls.']
  input_dna_mass_ng decimal(10,3) [note: 'Mass of input nucleic acid (DNA or RNA) used for library preparation, measured in nanograms (ng). A key QC parameter — insufficient input mass is a leading cause of library preparation failure and low complexity.']
  insert_size_bp varchar [note: 'Target or measured average insert size of the library fragments in base pairs (bp). A key MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE (MASTER_RESOURCE category) for library quality assessment. Impacts read alignment efficiency, coverage uniformity, and variant calling accuracy. Typically 150–500 bp for WGS/WES.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the library\'s intended use. Determines applicable quality, documentation, and reporting requirements. Values: RUO — Research Use Only; IVD — In Vitro Diagnostic; LDT — Laboratory Developed Test; clinical_trial — use in regulated clinical study; research — internal R&D use.']
  is_paired_end boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the library is designed for paired-end (True) or single-read (False) sequencing. Paired-end sequencing generates two reads per fragment, improving alignment accuracy and structural variant detection. Drives instrument run configuration.']
  is_pcr_free boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the library was prepared using a PCR-free protocol (True) or a PCR-amplified protocol (False). PCR-free libraries have lower duplicate rates and reduced GC bias, preferred for high-accuracy WGS applications.']
  library_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the sequencing library, used in LIMS, BaseSpace, and laboratory documentation. Follows the internal naming convention LIB-{alphanumeric}. This is the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER (MASTER_RESOURCE category) used across operational systems.']
  library_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name assigned to the sequencing library, typically incorporating sample identifier, preparation date, and assay type. Used as the IDENTITY_LABEL (MASTER_RESOURCE category) for display in LIMS dashboards, BaseSpace projects, and laboratory reports.']
  library_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sequencing library by assay strategy. Drives downstream bioinformatics pipeline selection, coverage expectations, and reporting templates. CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE (MASTER_RESOURCE category). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|amplicon|RNA-seq|targeted_panel|methylation|ChIP-seq|ATAC-seq|single_cell — promote to reference product]']
  lims_library_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier for this library record within LabVantage LIMS. Supports bidirectional reconciliation between the Silver Layer data product and the operational LIMS system of record for audit and data lineage purposes.']
  nucleic_acid_type varchar [note: 'Type of nucleic acid used as input for library preparation. Drives protocol selection, QC thresholds, and bioinformatics pipeline configuration. Values: gDNA — genomic DNA; cfDNA — cell-free DNA (liquid biopsy); RNA — total or mRNA; cDNA — complementary DNA; FFPE_DNA — formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded DNA; amplicon_DNA — pre-amplified target DNA.']
  pcr_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Number of PCR amplification cycles applied during library preparation. Impacts PCR duplicate rate, GC bias, and library complexity. PCR-free libraries have a value of 0. Tracked for QC benchmarking and protocol optimization.']
  pooling_proportion_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage contribution of this library to the total multiplexing pool by molar concentration. Used to balance per-library coverage in multiplexed sequencing runs and to investigate coverage imbalance post-sequencing.']
  prep_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the library preparation protocol was completed. Used for lot traceability, reagent expiry correlation, and turnaround time (TAT) calculations. Aligns with GxP batch record requirements.']
  qc_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the reason for QC failure when qc_status is \'fail\' or \'conditional_pass\'. Supports root-cause analysis, protocol improvement, and regulatory deviation reporting. Null when qc_status is \'pass\' or \'pending\'.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Current quality control disposition of the library. Determines whether the library is eligible for sequencing run loading. LIFECYCLE_STATUS (MASTER_RESOURCE category). Values: pass — meets all QC thresholds; fail — does not meet thresholds; pending — QC not yet completed; conditional_pass — meets minimum thresholds with caveats; requeue — failed and scheduled for re-preparation.']
  quantification_method varchar [note: 'Analytical method used to measure library concentration and/or size distribution. Affects confidence in concentration accuracy and downstream loading calculations. Common methods: qPCR (most accurate for sequenceable molecules), Bioanalyzer/TapeStation (size + concentration), Qubit (fluorometric mass concentration).']
  resequencing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this library was prepared as a resequencing attempt for a previously failed or insufficient-coverage library (True = resequencing; False = original preparation). Supports TAT tracking, cost analysis, and failure rate reporting.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Required or actual storage temperature for the library in degrees Celsius (e.g., -20°C for long-term, 4°C for short-term). Supports cold chain compliance, stability monitoring, and deviation investigation.']
  storage_well_position varchar [note: 'Well position (e.g., A01, H12) within the storage plate where the library aliquot is located. Combined with storage_plate_id, provides precise physical location for laboratory retrieval and inventory audits.']
  strand_specificity varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the library preserves strand-of-origin information for RNA-seq or other strand-aware assays. Values: unstranded — no strand information retained; forward — read 1 maps to sense strand; reverse — read 1 maps to antisense strand. Required for correct transcript quantification in RNA-seq pipelines.']
  target_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'Intended sequencing coverage depth (read depth) for this library, expressed as fold-coverage (e.g., 30x for WGS, 100x for WES, 500x for targeted panels). Drives sequencing run planning, flow cell loading calculations, and QC pass/fail thresholds.']
  target_read_length varchar [note: 'Intended sequencing read length in base pairs (e.g., 150 bp for paired-end short-read WGS). Determines sequencing run configuration, instrument chemistry selection, and bioinformatics pipeline parameters.']
  udi_plate_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the unique dual index (UDI) plate from which the index pair was assigned. Enables traceability of index assignments across batches and supports investigation of index hopping or cross-contamination events.']
  udi_well_position varchar [note: 'Well position (e.g., A01, B12) on the UDI plate from which the index pair was drawn. Together with udi_plate_id, uniquely identifies the index assignment for demultiplexing and audit purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the library record. Supports change tracking, audit trail completeness, and Silver Layer incremental load processing. Aligns with FDA 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.']
  volume_remaining_ul decimal(8,2) [note: 'Current volume of library material remaining in storage, measured in microliters (µL). Tracks consumable inventory for re-sequencing eligibility and storage management. Updated after each aliquot event.']

  Note: 'Master record representing a prepared sequencing library — the biochemically processed form of a biological sample ready for loading onto a flow cell. Captures library identity, source sample reference (FK to sample domain), preparation protocol and kit, insert size (bp), concentration (nM), quantification method (qPCR, Bioanalyzer, Qubit), library type (WGS, WES, amplicon, RNA-seq, ChIP-seq, targeted panel, methylation), adapter sequences, unique dual index (UDI) assignment, strand specificity, preparation date, operator, QC status (pass/fail/pending), volume remaining (µL), and storage location (plate/well). SSOT for library identity within the sequencing domain — the central entity linking sample preparation to sequencing output and the primary unit of work for coverage tracking.'
}

Table sequencing.library_prep_run {
  library_prep_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the library preparation run record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — captures a discrete batch execution event with a full lifecycle from initiation through QC disposition.']

  accreditation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.accreditation. Business justification: Library prep runs executed under GxP or CLIA-accredited conditions must link to the customer\'s accreditation record for regulatory traceability, audit trail completeness, and quality system compliance']
  aliquot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.aliquot. Business justification: GMP chain-of-custody and QC failure root cause analysis require tracing a library prep run to the specific aliquot consumed. Genomics LIMS experts universally expect this link for lot genealogy, mater']

  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.audit. Business justification: Library prep runs are audited for GxP compliance, protocol adherence, documentation completeness, and reagent traceability. Auditors link findings to specific prep runs for corrective action tracking.']

Essential for batch cost tracking, lot-to-lot performance analysis, regulatory traceability, and quality investigations when prep runs fail QC.']

The use_type column (RUO/IVD) is a denormalized signa']
  research_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the standard operating procedure (SOP) or library preparation protocol document used for this run. Links to the document control system (MasterControl/Veeva Vault) for version-controlled protocol traceability.']
  sales_order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.header. Business justification: Library prep runs performed as a sequencing service must be traceable to the originating sales order for billing, TAT tracking, and service delivery reporting. Role-prefix \'sales_order_\' is required t']
  service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_agreement. Business justification: Library prep services governed by service agreements defining turnaround time, QC standards, pricing. Critical for SLA compliance verification and contract-specific protocol adherence.']
  service_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.service_offering.   software_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.software_release. Business justification: Automated library prep instruments run specific software versions.   specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Library prep runs are executed against product specifications defining acceptable fragment size, PCR cycles, and yield. For GxP batch release and OOS investigations, the specification version governin']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Library prep kit supplier tracking. Enables supplier performance evaluation based on prep run outcomes (QC failures, adapter dimer rates), quality investigations, and supplier scorecarding for procure']
  technical_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.technical_requirement. Business justification: Library prep runs are configured to fulfill specific customer technical requirements (insert size, library type, PCR cycles, coverage targets). This link enables traceability from prep execution back ']
  test_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_catalog. Business justification: Library prep runs are executed as part of a specific clinical test workflow. The test_catalog entry defines library prep requirements (insert size, PCR cycles, fragmentation method). Linking enables e']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Library prep runs are used in validation studies (kit validation, protocol verification, robustness testing). Labs track which prep runs contributed to validation data for regulatory documentation and']
  adapter_dimer_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of adapter dimer contamination detected in the final library by electrophoresis (Bioanalyzer/TapeStation). High adapter dimer content reduces effective sequencing yield and is a key QC failure indicator. Typically must be below 5% for pass disposition.']
  adapter_ligation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to ligate sequencing adapters to the end-repaired DNA fragments. Tagmentation (Nextera) combines fragmentation and adapter ligation in a single step. Affects ligation efficiency, adapter dimer formation rate, and overall library yield.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the library preparation run record received formal QA/supervisor approval. Distinct from run completion timestamp. Required for GxP electronic record compliance and regulatory audit trail.']
  basespace_run_code varchar [note: 'Run identifier assigned by Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub for the downstream sequencing run associated with this library preparation batch. Enables end-to-end traceability from library preparation through raw data generation and FASTQ output.']
  batch_qc_outcome varchar [note: 'Overall quality control disposition for the library preparation run batch. Determines whether the batch is approved for sequencing submission. A conditional pass may require additional review or re-quantification before proceeding.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this library preparation run record was first created in the source LIMS system. Supports GxP audit trail and data lineage requirements. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  deviation_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any deviation from the approved SOP observed during this library preparation run. Populated only when sop_deviation_flag is True. Supports GxP deviation management, CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) initiation, and regulatory audit readiness.']
  end_repair_performed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an end-repair and A-tailing step was performed on the fragmented DNA prior to adapter ligation. End repair is required for blunt-end ligation protocols but may be skipped in tagmentation-based workflows.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the library preparation run was completed or terminated. Used in conjunction with start_timestamp to compute total run duration and TAT metrics. Nullable if run is still in progress.']
  final_library_concentration_nm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured concentration of the final pooled library in nanomolar (nM), typically quantified by qPCR or fluorometric assay (e.g., Qubit). Used to calculate the dilution factor for optimal flow cell loading and to assess library yield.']
  final_pooling_volume_ul decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total volume in microliters (µL) of the final pooled library submitted for sequencing. Used to calculate loading concentration for the flow cell and to assess library yield sufficiency for sequencing.']
  fragmentation_method varchar [note: 'DNA/RNA fragmentation approach applied during library preparation. Enzymatic fragmentation uses restriction enzymes or transposases (e.g., Nextera); mechanical fragmentation uses sonication or acoustic shearing (e.g., Covaris). Affects insert size distribution and library quality metrics.']
  index_collision_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the selected multiplexing index combinations were verified to have sufficient Hamming distance to prevent index hopping or misassignment during demultiplexing. A failed check requires index reassignment before sequencing.']
  input_dna_amount_ng decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total mass of input DNA or RNA (in nanograms) used per sample in this library preparation run. Critical QC parameter — insufficient input material is a leading cause of library preparation failure and low-complexity libraries.']
  input_material_type varchar [note: 'Type of nucleic acid input material used for library preparation (e.g., genomic DNA (gDNA), cell-free DNA (cfDNA), RNA, formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) DNA). Determines applicable protocol, QC acceptance criteria, and downstream analytical interpretation.']
  library_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sequencing library type prepared in this run. Determines downstream bioinformatics pipeline selection, QC thresholds, and sequencing parameters. Examples: Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), targeted panel, RNA-seq. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted|RNA-seq|amplicon|ChIP-seq|ATAC-seq|other — promote to reference product]']
  lims_run_code varchar [note: 'Native run identifier assigned by the source LabVantage LIMS system. Preserved as a cross-reference key to enable bidirectional traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer and the operational LIMS without relying solely on the surrogate primary key.']
  mean_fragment_size_bp varchar [note: 'Measured mean fragment size in base pairs (bp) of the final library as determined by Bioanalyzer, TapeStation, or equivalent electrophoresis instrument. Compared against target_insert_size_bp to assess size selection success and protocol adherence.']
  multiplexing_index_set varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the index/barcode set used for sample multiplexing in this library preparation run (e.g., Illumina TruSeq CD Index Set A, IDT for Illumina 10 bp UDI). Enables demultiplexing of pooled samples post-sequencing and prevents index collision errors.']
  pcr_amplification_cycles varchar [note: 'Number of PCR amplification cycles applied during library preparation. Excessive PCR cycles introduce duplicate reads and GC bias, reducing library complexity. Zero cycles indicates a PCR-free protocol. Key parameter for library quality assessment and protocol compliance.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the library preparation SOP or protocol document applied during this run (e.g., v2.1.0). Critical for GxP compliance to confirm the correct, approved protocol version was followed and to detect protocol drift across runs.']
  qc_method varchar [note: 'Analytical method used to quantify and assess library quality (e.g., qPCR for accurate molar quantification, Qubit fluorometry for mass concentration, Bioanalyzer/TapeStation for fragment size distribution). Affects the reliability of concentration measurements and QC pass/fail decisions.']
  run_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the library preparation run, typically generated by the LIMS (e.g., LP-NGSA-240315). Used for cross-system traceability, SOP deviation tracking, and GxP audit references. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  run_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator observations, troubleshooting notes, or contextual comments recorded during or after the library preparation run. Supplements structured deviation documentation and supports root cause analysis.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the library preparation run. Drives workflow routing, QC gating, and downstream sequencing scheduling. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|completed|failed|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Total number of biological samples processed in this library preparation run batch. Used for capacity planning, throughput reporting, and cost-per-sample calculations. Represents the principal quantitative result of this transaction.']
  size_selection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to select the target insert size range from the library pool (e.g., SPRI bead-based cleanup, gel excision, Pippin Prep automated electrophoresis). Size selection removes adapter dimers and off-target fragments, improving sequencing efficiency.']
  sop_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any deviation from the approved standard operating procedure (SOP) was recorded during this library preparation run. A True value triggers mandatory deviation documentation and review per GxP requirements.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the library preparation run was physically initiated in the laboratory. Represents the principal real-world business event time. Used for turnaround time (TAT) calculation and GxP audit trails. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  target_insert_size_bp varchar [note: 'Intended DNA insert size in base pairs (bp) for the prepared library, as specified by the protocol. Insert size directly impacts sequencing read overlap, coverage uniformity, and variant calling accuracy. Typical ranges: 150–400 bp for short-read NGS.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this library preparation run record. Enables change detection, incremental data loading, and GxP audit trail compliance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  use_type varchar [note: 'Regulatory use classification of the library preparation run. Research Use Only (RUO) runs are not subject to IVD regulatory controls; In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) and Laboratory Developed Test (LDT) runs require CLIA/CAP compliance and stricter QC documentation. Drives applicable regulatory requirements.']
  workgroup_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory workgroup or team responsible for executing this library preparation run. Supports capacity planning, workload balancing, and team-level performance reporting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the execution of a library preparation workflow batch. Tracks prep run ID, protocol version, kit lot number, number of samples processed, start/end timestamps, operator, instrument used (e.g., liquid handler), fragmentation method (enzymatic, mechanical), end-repair and adapter ligation steps, PCR amplification cycles, final pooling volume, batch QC outcome, and any deviations from SOP. Enables traceability from raw sample to sequencing-ready library and supports GxP audit requirements.'
}

Table sequencing.pool {
  pool_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the multiplexed library pool record. Primary key for the pool entity in the sequencing domain.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Pooling services for customer samples in multiplexed sequencing. Tracks service delivery, billing, and customer-specific pooling strategies. Essential for multi-customer pool segregation and data acce']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing instrument (e.g., Illumina NovaSeq, NextSeq) onto which this pool is intended to be loaded.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Pool preparation uses specific reagent batches (dilution buffers, loading reagents) that must be traceable for QC failure root-cause analysis and supplier recall management. pool.lot_number is a denor']
  flow_cell_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flow cell onto which this pool will be or has been loaded for sequencing.']
  index_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.index_set. Business justification: Pools use specific index sets for multiplexing libraries. pool currently has index_type (string) but no FK to the index_set reference master. Adding index_set_id formalizes the relationship to the con']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Pooling operations use specific reagent lots (loading buffers, normalization reagents); lot traceability is required for QC investigations and recall management. The plain lot_number attribute is a de']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Pools failing QC (qc_overall_passed, qc_failure_reason on pool) require nonconformance records for material disposition in GxP genomics. Pool links to oos_investigation and qc_result but not nonconfor']
  oos_investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.oos_investigation. Business justification: Pools with OOS QC results (concentration deviation, fragment size out of range, index imbalance) trigger OOS investigations. Labs track which pool triggered each investigation for root cause analysis ']
  parent_pool_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original pool from which this re-pool was derived, when is_repool is True. Enables lineage tracking and root cause analysis for failed pools.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the research or clinical project under which this pool was created. Links the pool to project-level objectives, funding, and reporting requirements.']
  qc_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Pools undergo QC testing (concentration, fragment size, index balance, PhiX spike-in verification) before sequencing. QC results determine pool disposition, loading concentration adjustments, and repr']
  reagent_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.catalog. Business justification: Pooling reagent specification lookups (storage temperature, hazard classification, GMP compliance) require linking pool to reagent.catalog. The existing reagent_catalog_item_id targets product.catalog']
  reagent_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Pooling operations use reagents/normalization kits that are catalog items. Needed for cost accounting, protocol compliance, and tracking which commercial products were used for library normalization a']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Pool QC (loading concentration, fragment size, library count) is evaluated against product specifications. For sequencing run planning and QC investigations, the specification version governing pool a']
  technical_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.technical_requirement. Business justification: Pool configuration (loading concentration, multiplex count, target read depth, read length) is directly driven by customer technical requirements. This link enables traceability from pooling decisions']
  basespace_pool_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned to this pool within Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub. Used for cross-system traceability between the internal LIMS and the BaseSpace sequencing data management platform.']
  concentration_method varchar [note: 'Analytical method used to measure the pool concentration. Qubit and qPCR are preferred for accurate molar quantification; Bioanalyzer and TapeStation provide both concentration and fragment size data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pool record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory traceability.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the pool is considered expired and should not be loaded onto the sequencer. Determined by the stability window of the pooled libraries under specified storage conditions.']
  final_concentration_nm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured molar concentration of the pool in nanomolar (nM) after pooling, as determined by Qubit fluorometry or qPCR quantification. Used to calculate dilution for loading concentration.']
  flow_cell_lane varchar [note: 'Specific lane(s) on the flow cell to which this pool is assigned for loading. A pool may occupy a single lane or the entire flow cell depending on the instrument type and run design.']
  fragment_size_lower_bp varchar [note: 'Lower bound of the acceptable fragment size distribution range in base pairs (bp) for this pool, as defined by the QC specification. Fragments below this threshold indicate adapter contamination or over-fragmentation.']
  fragment_size_method varchar [note: 'Instrument or method used to assess the fragment size distribution of the pool (e.g., Agilent Bioanalyzer, Agilent TapeStation, Advanced Analytical Fragment Analyzer, or gel electrophoresis).']
  fragment_size_upper_bp varchar [note: 'Upper bound of the acceptable fragment size distribution range in base pairs (bp) for this pool. Fragments above this threshold may indicate incomplete fragmentation or genomic DNA contamination.']
  index_collision_risk varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level of index sequence collision (cross-contamination between demultiplexed samples) within this pool, based on Hamming distance analysis of the index set. Low risk indicates sufficient edit distance between all index pairs.']
  is_repool boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pool is a re-pool created to replace or supplement a previously failed or underperforming pool (True = re-pool, False = original pool). Used for tracking remediation events.']
  library_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individually indexed libraries combined into this pool. Determines the degree of multiplexing and directly impacts per-library read depth allocation.']
  lims_pool_code varchar [note: 'Native pool identifier from LabVantage LIMS, used for cross-system reconciliation between the enterprise data lakehouse and the operational LIMS source system.']
  loading_concentration_pm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Target loading concentration of the diluted pool in picomolar (pM) for the specific instrument and flow cell type. Directly impacts cluster density and sequencing quality.']
  mean_fragment_size_bp varchar [note: 'Mean insert fragment size of the pool in base pairs (bp) as measured by Bioanalyzer or TapeStation electrophoresis. Critical for assessing library quality and estimating molar concentration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for laboratory scientists to record observations, deviations, or special handling instructions associated with this pool that are not captured in structured fields.']
  phix_spike_in_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of PhiX Control library spiked into the pool prior to sequencing. PhiX is used as an internal sequencing quality control to monitor cluster density, error rate, and base calling performance.']
  pool_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the pool for tracking across LIMS, BaseSpace, and laboratory worksheets. Used as the business identifier on COAs and run manifests.']
  pool_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name assigned to the pool, typically encoding project, date, and cohort information for easy identification in run planning and reporting.']
  pool_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pool. Tracks progression from initial creation through QC verification, loading onto the sequencer, sequencing completion, or expiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|qc_in_progress|qc_passed|qc_failed|loaded|sequenced|expired — promote to reference product]']
  pooling_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the individual libraries were combined to form this pool. Used for traceability, stability window calculation, and laboratory scheduling.']
  pooling_strategy varchar [note: 'Method used to combine individual indexed libraries into the pool. Equimolar pools combine equal molar quantities; custom ratio pools use user-defined proportions; normalized pools use concentration normalization prior to combining; volume-based pools combine equal volumes.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) or laboratory protocol used to perform the pooling procedure. Ensures reproducibility and regulatory traceability.']
  qc_concentration_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pool passed the concentration QC threshold check (True = passed, False = failed). A pool must pass concentration QC before it is eligible for loading onto the sequencer.']
  qc_date date [note: 'Date on which QC measurements (concentration and fragment size) were performed on the pool. May differ from pooling date if QC is conducted on a subsequent day.']
  qc_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the reason(s) for QC failure when qc_overall_passed is False. Used for root cause analysis, CAPA initiation, and laboratory troubleshooting.']
  qc_fragment_size_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pool passed the fragment size distribution QC check (True = passed, False = failed). Ensures the pool\'s insert size profile is within acceptable bounds for the target sequencing application.']
