  qc_overall_passed boolean [note: 'Composite QC pass/fail flag indicating whether the pool has passed all required QC checks (concentration and fragment size) and is approved for sequencer loading. True = all QC checks passed.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Intended use classification of the sequencing run for which this pool is prepared. RUO (Research Use Only) pools are not subject to clinical regulatory requirements; IVD and LDT pools require CLIA/CAP compliance.']
  sequencing_application varchar [note: 'Genomic sequencing application type for which this pool was prepared. Determines bioinformatics pipeline routing, QC thresholds, and coverage requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wgs|wes|targeted_panel|amplicon|rna_seq|chip_seq|atac_seq|methyl_seq — promote to reference product]']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical storage location identifier (freezer, rack, box, position) where the pool is stored in the laboratory. Used for sample retrieval and inventory management within LIMS.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required storage temperature in degrees Celsius for maintaining pool stability and integrity. Typically -20°C for long-term storage or 4°C for short-term use.']
  target_read_depth varchar [note: 'Expected average sequencing read depth (coverage depth) per library within the pool, expressed as number of reads. Used for run planning, yield allocation, and per-sample coverage estimation.']
  target_read_length varchar [note: 'Intended sequencing read length in base pairs (bp) for this pool (e.g., 150 bp paired-end). Determines the sequencing run configuration and impacts data output volume.']
  total_volume_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total volume of the combined pool in microlitres (µL) after all libraries have been pooled. Used to assess whether sufficient material exists for loading and archival.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the pool record. Used for change tracking, audit trail compliance, and data synchronization between source systems and the lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Master record for a multiplexed pool of indexed libraries combined for co-sequencing on a single flow cell lane or entire flow cell. Captures pool identity, pooling strategy (equimolar, custom ratio, normalized), number of constituent libraries, total volume (µL), final concentration (nM), loading concentration for the target instrument and flow cell type, pooling date, operator, pool QC status (concentration verification via Qubit/qPCR, fragment size distribution via Bioanalyzer/TapeStation), expected per-library read depth targets, and pool expiry/stability window. Central to multiplexing operations — the pool is what physically gets loaded onto the sequencer and determines per-sample yield allocation.'
}

Table sequencing.pool_library {
  pool_library_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the pool-library association record. Primary key for this junction entity linking a sequencing library to a pool.']
  index_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.index_set. Business justification: pool_library tracks individual library assignments within pools with their specific index sequences. Currently has index_kit_name (string) and index sequences but no FK to index_set. Adding index_set_']
  library_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing library that has been assigned to this pool. Enables reconstruction of which library contributed reads in a pooled run.']
  pool_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing pool to which this library has been assigned. Supports demultiplexing traceability back to the originating pool.']
  actual_molar_fraction_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'The measured proportion (%) of this library\'s molar contribution in the final pool, determined by qPCR or fluorometric quantification. Deviations from expected fraction indicate pipetting or quantification errors.']
  assigned_read_count varchar [note: 'Total number of sequencing reads successfully demultiplexed and assigned to this library from the pool run. Populated post-sequencing from demultiplexing output (e.g., bcl2fastq/BCL Convert reports). Used to assess yield per library.']
  assigned_read_fraction_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of total pool reads assigned to this library after demultiplexing. Compared against expected molar fraction to assess pooling balance and identify over- or under-represented libraries.']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when this library was assigned to the pool in the LIMS or BaseSpace Sequence Hub. Serves as the primary business event timestamp for this junction record.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the library-to-pool assignment. Tracks whether the library is pending confirmation, actively loaded, failed QC, or removed from the pool.']
  basespace_dataset_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FASTQ dataset generated for this library in Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub following demultiplexing. Links the pool-library assignment to the resulting sequencing data artifact for downstream bioinformatics pipeline ingestion.']
  cluster_density decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cluster density (in thousands per mm²) on the flow cell lane attributable to this library\'s loading contribution. Excessively high or low cluster density impacts sequencing quality and read assignment accuracy.']
  demultiplex_mismatch_allowed varchar [note: 'Number of base mismatches permitted during demultiplexing for this library\'s index sequence. Typically 0 or 1. Higher values increase sensitivity but risk misassignment between similar indexes.']
  expected_molar_fraction_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'The theoretical proportion (%) of this library\'s molar contribution relative to the total pool. Used to balance sequencing depth across libraries in a multiplexed run. Sum across all libraries in a pool should equal 100%.']
  i5_index_sequence varchar [note: 'The i5 (read 2 side) barcode index nucleotide sequence assigned to this library for dual-index multiplexing. Required for dual-indexed library pools to prevent index hopping. Typically 6–10 bases.']
  i7_index_sequence varchar [note: 'The i7 (read 1 side) barcode index nucleotide sequence assigned to this library for multiplexing identification. Used during demultiplexing to assign reads back to the originating library. Typically 6–10 bases.']
  index_collision_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this library\'s index sequence conflicts (collides) with another library\'s index in the same pool, which would impair demultiplexing accuracy. True = collision detected; False = no collision. Populated by index compatibility checking tools.']
  index_distance varchar [note: 'Minimum Hamming distance between this library\'s index sequence and all other index sequences in the same pool. A distance of ≥2 is typically required for reliable demultiplexing with 1 mismatch tolerance. Low values indicate collision risk.']
  index_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or catalog designation of the index pair (e.g., \'IDT-10nt-UDI-0001\', \'TruSeq-D701-D501\'). Corresponds to the index kit nomenclature used during library preparation.']
  insert_size_bp varchar [note: 'Mean insert size in base pairs (bp) of this library as measured prior to pooling. Insert size affects read overlap, coverage uniformity, and compatibility with the sequencing run parameters.']
  lane_assignment varchar [note: 'The specific flow cell lane(s) to which this library-pool combination is assigned for sequencing (e.g., \'1\', \'1,2\', \'all\'). Relevant for patterned flow cells with lane-level loading (e.g., Illumina NovaSeq S4 flow cell).']
  lims_pool_library_ref varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for this pool-library association record as recorded in LabVantage LIMS. Enables cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational LIMS source of record.']
  loading_ratio decimal(8,5) [note: 'The ratio of this library\'s contribution relative to the total pool volume or molar amount at the time of flow cell loading. Used to normalize read depth expectations and assess pooling balance post-sequencing.']
  mean_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average Phred quality score across all bases in reads assigned to this library. Provides a summary measure of base call accuracy for this library\'s contribution to the pool run.']
  normalization_tool varchar [note: 'Name of the instrument or software tool used to quantify and normalize this library prior to pooling (e.g., \'Qubit 4 Fluorometer\', \'Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100\', \'Agilent TapeStation 4200\', \'KAPA Library Quantification qPCR\'). Supports reagent and instrument traceability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for laboratory operator observations, deviations, or contextual remarks specific to this library\'s pooling event. Supports GxP batch record documentation and investigation workflows.']
  phred_q30_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of bases in reads assigned to this library that meet or exceed Phred quality score Q30 (≥99.9% base call accuracy). Key NGS quality metric; typically ≥80% Q30 is required for clinical-grade sequencing.']
  pooling_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this library was physically added to the pool. Used for traceability, lot expiry checks, and TAT (Turnaround Time) calculations.']
  pooling_method varchar [note: 'Method used to determine the volume of this library added to the pool. Options include equimolar normalization, volume-based, mass-based, qPCR-normalized, or fluorometric quantification-based pooling.']
  regulatory_use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of this library\'s intended use context within the pool. RUO (Research Use Only), IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic), LDT (Laboratory Developed Test), or GxP-governed (GMP/GCP/GLP). Determines applicable compliance requirements for data handling and reporting.']
  removal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why this library was removed from the pool. Populated only when assignment_status is \'removed\' or \'failed\'. Supports root cause analysis and CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) workflows.']
  removed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this library was removed or disqualified from the pool, if applicable. Null if the library remains active in the pool. Supports lifecycle tracking and investigation of failed runs.']
  sample_sheet_position varchar [note: 'Row position of this library entry in the Illumina sample sheet (SampleSheet.csv) used to configure the sequencing run and demultiplexing. Ensures correct index-to-sample mapping during BCL conversion.']
  sequencing_application varchar [note: 'The NGS application type for which this library was prepared and pooled. Determines expected coverage depth, read length, and bioinformatics pipeline routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|amplicon|RNA_seq|ChIP_seq|ATAC_seq|methylation_seq|single_cell — promote to reference product]']
  spike_in_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this library entry represents a spike-in control (e.g., PhiX, synthetic spike-in) rather than a biological sample library. True = spike-in control; False = biological sample. Spike-ins are used for sequencing quality calibration and are excluded from biological analysis.']
  target_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'The intended sequencing coverage depth (×) for this library in the pool (e.g., 30× for WGS, 100× for WES, 500× for targeted panels). Used to calculate required read count and validate pooling balance.']
  undetermined_read_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this library contributed a disproportionate number of reads to the undetermined (unassigned) category during demultiplexing, suggesting index sequence issues or contamination. True = flagged; False = within acceptable threshold.']
  volume_added_ul decimal(8,3) [note: 'Actual volume in microliters (µL) of this library pipetted into the pool. Critical for molar balance calculations and audit of physical pooling operations.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking libraries to pools, capturing the multiplexing relationship with its own business data. Records the library-to-pool assignment, index sequence (i7/i5), expected molar fraction (%), actual volume contributed (µL), loading ratio, and any index collision flags. Essential for demultiplexing traceability — enables reconstruction of which library contributed which reads in a pooled run.'
}

Table sequencing.run_lane {
  run_lane_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a single lane record within a sequencing run on a multi-lane flow cell. Primary key for the run_lane entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE — represents one lane/line of a sequencing run (TRANSACTION_HEADER), carrying its own QC metrics, yield, and operational state.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing instrument (e.g., NovaSeq 6000, NextSeq 2000) on which this lane was sequenced. Supports instrument utilization tracking, maintenance scheduling, and cross-instrument performance benchmarking.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Run lane failures (lane_qc_status, lane_failure_reason on run_lane) require deviation records in GxP sequencing labs. run_lane has no existing quality FKs. A GxP genomics QA expert expects direct trac']
  flow_cell_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical flow cell consumable loaded on the instrument for this run. Links lane-level performance data to the specific flow cell lot and type (e.g., NovaSeq 6000 S4 flow cell). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: RESOURCE_REFERENCE.']
  library_pool_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the multiplexed library pool or individual library loaded onto this lane. Enables traceability from lane-level sequencing output back to the sample preparation and library construction records in the LIMS.']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sequencing run (TRANSACTION_HEADER) that this lane belongs to. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: HEADER_REFERENCE — the lane is a line-level record within the run.']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Run lane QC (lane_qc_status) is evaluated against product specifications for target cluster density and yield. Columns target_cluster_density_k_per_mm2 and target_yield_gb are denormalized from produc']
  basecalling_software varchar [note: 'Name and version of the base calling software used to convert raw intensity signals to nucleotide sequences (FASTQ) for this lane (e.g., Illumina RTA 3.4.4, DRAGEN). Critical for data provenance, reproducibility, and regulatory traceability of genomic data.']
  basecalling_software_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the base calling software used for this lane. Required for GxP audit trails and bioinformatics reproducibility; version changes may affect base quality scores and downstream variant calling results.']
  basespace_lane_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this lane as recorded in Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub, the primary system of record for sequencing data management. Enables direct linkage between the enterprise data lakehouse and the BaseSpace platform for data retrieval and audit.']
  cluster_density_k_per_mm2 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of clusters generated per square millimeter on this lane, expressed in thousands (K/mm²). A key sequencing performance metric; values outside the instrument-specific acceptable range indicate over- or under-clustering, leading to reduced data quality or yield.']
  clusters_passing_filter_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of clusters on this lane that pass the Illumina chastity filter, indicating the proportion of high-quality reads retained for downstream analysis. Industry benchmark is typically ≥75–80%. Low values indicate poor library quality, phasing issues, or flow cell defects.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this run lane record was first created in the system, typically upon run initiation or lane assignment. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED. Required for data lineage, GxP audit trail, and regulatory compliance.']
  demultiplexing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the demultiplexing process for this lane, which separates pooled sample reads by index barcode to generate per-sample FASTQ files. Tracks whether the bioinformatics demultiplexing step has been initiated, completed, or encountered errors.']
  error_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Sequencing error rate for this lane, expressed as a percentage, calculated from PhiX alignment. Reflects the per-base mismatch rate against the PhiX reference and is a key indicator of instrument and reagent performance. Typical acceptable threshold is <1%.']
  fastq_output_path varchar [note: 'File system or cloud storage path (e.g., AWS S3 URI or BaseSpace project path) where the demultiplexed FASTQ files for this lane are stored. Enables downstream bioinformatics pipelines to locate and consume the raw sequencing data output.']
  index_read_length_i1 varchar [note: 'Length of Index Read 1 (i7 index) in base pairs used for demultiplexing samples on this lane. Defines the barcode length for sample identification in multiplexed sequencing runs.']
  index_read_length_i2 varchar [note: 'Length of Index Read 2 (i5 index) in base pairs used for dual-index demultiplexing on this lane. Null for single-index runs. Dual indexing reduces index hopping and improves sample assignment accuracy in high-multiplex runs.']
  lane_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when sequencing completed on this lane. Together with lane_start_timestamp, enables calculation of lane-level sequencing duration and instrument cycle time for operational efficiency reporting.']
  lane_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the root cause when a lane fails QC (lane_qc_status = \'fail\'). Captures failure categories such as low cluster density, high error rate, low Q30, PhiX misalignment, or reagent lot issues. Used for root cause analysis, CAPA, and instrument troubleshooting.']
  lane_number varchar [note: 'Physical lane position number on the flow cell (e.g., 1–8 for NovaSeq 6000 S4 flow cell). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: LINE_SEQUENCE — the ordinal position of this lane within the flow cell/run.']
  lane_qc_status varchar [note: 'Overall Quality Control (QC) disposition for this lane based on cluster density, clusters passing filter, PhiX alignment, and yield thresholds. Drives downstream bioinformatics pipeline gating and sample release decisions. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pass|fail|warning|pending|under_review|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  lane_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when sequencing commenced on this lane. Used for Turnaround Time (TAT) calculation, instrument utilization reporting, and scheduling of downstream bioinformatics processing. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP.']
  lane_yield_gb decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total sequencing output (data yield) generated from this lane, expressed in gigabases (Gb). Primary throughput metric for lane-level performance assessment and SLA compliance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: LINE_QUANTITY.']
  lims_lane_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier for this lane record as assigned by the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS), such as LabVantage. Enables cross-system traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer analytical record and the operational LIMS source of record.']
  loading_concentration_pm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Concentration of the library or pool loaded onto this lane, expressed in picomolar (pM). Critical operational parameter that directly influences cluster density and sequencing quality. Deviations from the target loading concentration are a primary root cause of lane failures.']
  mean_phred_q30_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of bases on this lane with a Phred quality score ≥30 (Q30), indicating a base call accuracy of 99.9% or higher. Industry-standard quality threshold for NGS data; Illumina specifies Q30 ≥75–80% as a minimum acceptance criterion for most applications.']
  mean_phred_score decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average Phred base quality score across all bases sequenced on this lane. Provides a single summary quality metric complementing the Q30 percentage for lane-level quality trending and instrument performance monitoring.']
  num_samples_multiplexed varchar [note: 'Count of individual samples multiplexed (pooled) and loaded onto this lane. Determines the expected per-sample read depth after demultiplexing and is a key parameter for capacity planning and SLA compliance per sample.']
  phix_alignment_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of reads on this lane that aligned to the PhiX reference genome. Used to validate the PhiX spike-in and assess lane-level sequencing error rate. Significant deviation from the expected PhiX spike-in percentage indicates a lane-level quality issue.']
  phix_spike_in_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of PhiX Control v3 library spiked into this lane for sequencing quality monitoring and calibration. PhiX is an Illumina-standard internal control used to assess error rate, base quality, and instrument performance per lane.']
  qc_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the QC review of this lane was completed and the QC disposition (pass/fail/warning) was recorded. Required for GxP audit trail and regulatory submission traceability.']
  qc_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Reference to the Quality Control (QC) scientist or analyst who reviewed and approved or rejected the lane QC results. Required for GxP electronic signature compliance and audit trail integrity under FDA 21 CFR Part 11.']
  read_length_r1 varchar [note: 'Length of Read 1 in base pairs (bp) for this lane, as configured in the sequencing run setup. Determines the sequencing mode (e.g., 150 bp paired-end) and directly impacts the genomic applications supported (WGS, WES, targeted panels).']
  read_length_r2 varchar [note: 'Length of Read 2 in base pairs (bp) for paired-end sequencing on this lane. Null for single-read runs. Together with read_length_r1, defines the paired-end read configuration (e.g., 2×150 bp) used for library insert size estimation and alignment accuracy.']
  reads_passing_filter varchar [note: 'Number of reads on this lane that passed the Illumina chastity filter and are available for downstream bioinformatics processing. Directly determines the effective sequencing depth available for variant calling and genomic analysis.']
  requeue_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this lane has been requeued for resequencing due to QC failure or insufficient yield. Tracks repeat failure patterns for root cause analysis and supports escalation decisions when a lane repeatedly fails.']
  requeue_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane requires resequencing (requeue) due to QC failure or insufficient yield. Triggers downstream sample management and scheduling workflows in the LIMS to prioritize reruns and maintain customer SLA commitments.']
  run_mode varchar [note: 'Sequencing run mode for this lane: paired-end (reads generated from both ends of the DNA fragment) or single-read (reads generated from one end only). Determines FASTQ file structure and alignment strategy for downstream bioinformatics processing.']
  sequencing_application varchar [note: 'Genomic sequencing application type assigned to this lane (e.g., Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), targeted panel, RNA-seq). Determines QC thresholds, coverage requirements, and downstream bioinformatics pipeline routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|amplicon|RNA_seq|methylation|other — promote to reference product if additional application types are required]']
  total_reads varchar [note: 'Total number of sequencing reads generated from this lane before quality filtering. Provides a raw throughput count used alongside yield (Gb) for per-lane performance benchmarking and capacity planning.']
  undetermined_reads_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of reads on this lane that could not be assigned to a sample index during demultiplexing. High undetermined read rates indicate index contamination, index hopping, or sample sheet errors. Typically acceptable threshold is <5–10%.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this run lane record was last modified, such as when QC metrics were populated post-run or when the QC status was updated. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE category: RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED. Required for data lineage and GxP audit trail.']

  Note: 'Operational record representing a single lane within a sequencing run on a multi-lane flow cell. Captures lane number, assigned pool or library, loading concentration, cluster density (K/mm²), clusters passing filter (%), PhiX spike-in percentage, lane-level QC status, and lane yield (Gb). Enables per-lane performance monitoring and troubleshooting of lane-specific failures on instruments such as NovaSeq 6000 with S-series flow cells.'
}

Table sequencing.run_qc_metric {
  run_qc_metric_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each run QC metric record. Serves as the primary key for this transactional entity capturing quantitative quality measurements at run, lane, or library granularity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — stores discrete QC measurement events with a clear lifecycle and multiple metric dimensions sourced from Illumina InterOp, BaseSpace, bcl2fastq, Picard, and samtools.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: QC metrics are reported to customers and must be tied to accounts for customer-facing QC reports, SLA compliance tracking, and billing disputes. A genomics service team generates per-account QC summar']
  assay_development_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.assay_development. Business justification: Run QC metrics (error_rate, q30_percent, on_target_rate) are the primary evidence used in assay development validation reports. Direct linkage to assay_development enables automated aggregation of QC ']
  assay_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.assay_version. Business justification: QC metrics are evaluated against assay-version-specific thresholds (minimum_phred_score, minimum_coverage_depth). Linking run_qc_metric to assay_version enables version-specific QC trending and suppor']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing instrument (e.g., NovaSeq, NextSeq, HiSeq) that produced the run. Enables instrument-level performance trending, preventive maintenance correlation, and GxP instrument qualification tracking.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Run QC metric failures (error_rate_percent, q30_percent below spec) trigger deviation records in GxP sequencing labs. run_qc_metric has no existing deviation FK. A GxP genomics QA expert expects direc']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: QC metrics are evaluated in experiment context to determine if data quality meets experimental design requirements (coverage depth, Q30 thresholds). Drives go/no-go decisions for downstream analysis, ']
  extraction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.extraction. Business justification: Correlating sequencing QC outcomes (Q30, error rate, coverage) with upstream extraction quality is standard in genomics QC trending and method validation reports. This link enables root cause analysis']
  flow_cell_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flow cell consumable used in the sequencing run. Supports lot-level quality tracking, reagent performance correlation, and consumable failure root-cause analysis.']
  library_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing library (prepared sample) for which per-library coverage metrics are recorded. Nullable — populated only when metric_granularity_level is \'library\'. Enables per-library coverage adequacy assessment for WGS, WES, and targeted assays.']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Run QC metrics falling below specification (qc_status on run_qc_metric) can trigger nonconformance records for run output disposition in GxP sequencing. run_qc_metric links to oos_investigation but no']
  oos_investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.oos_investigation. Business justification: Run QC metrics that fail specifications (Q30 below threshold, cluster density out of range, high error rate) trigger OOS investigations. Labs link investigations to specific QC metrics for root cause ']
  performing_laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.performing_laboratory. Business justification: QC metrics are generated at a specific performing laboratory. For CAP proficiency testing, inter-lab QC comparisons, and CLIA compliance reporting, QC metrics must be traceable to the performing lab t']
  post_market_surveillance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.post_market_surveillance. Business justification: Sequencing run QC metrics (error rates, yield, Q30) are a primary data source for post-market surveillance reports on IVD sequencing products, as required by EU MDR Article 83 and FDA PMS guidance. Do']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Quality traceability: when sequencing run QC metrics fail, the root-cause investigation traces directly to the reagent production batch. This link enables the QC failure → production batch → CAPA work']
  qc_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Run QC metrics (Q30, cluster density, error rate, PhiX alignment) ARE QC results in the quality system. Labs must link sequencing QC to the broader QC result framework for trending, OOS investigations']
  regulatory_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.regulatory_classification. Business justification: QC metrics for IVD applications require regulatory classification traceability to determine reporting obligations and acceptance criteria. The plain column use_classification is denormalized from prod']
  run_lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.run_lane. Business justification: QC metrics can be captured at multiple granularity levels including lane level (metric_granularity_level attribute). When metrics are lane-specific, they should formally link to run_lane. Currently ha']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample associated with the library being assessed. Nullable — populated when metric_granularity_level is \'library\'. Supports sample-level quality traceability and clinical reporting.']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sequencing run for which this QC metric record was generated. Links the metric to the instrument run event, enabling run-level pass/fail determination and instrument performance trending.']
  software_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.software_release. Business justification: QC metrics are computed by specific software versions (alignment tools, QC pipelines). In regulated environments, the software version that computed each metric must be traceable for audit trails and ']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: QC metrics are evaluated against product specifications to determine pass/fail status. For OOS investigations and regulatory audits, the specification version used as the acceptance criterion must be ']
  support_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.support_case. Business justification: QC failures trigger customer support cases. Links metrics to cases for root cause analysis, resolution tracking, and customer communication. Critical for service lab incident management and SLA compli']
  test_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_catalog. Business justification: QC metrics are evaluated against test-catalog-specific acceptance criteria (minimum Q30, minimum coverage depth, error rate thresholds). Linking run_qc_metric to test_catalog enables per-assay QC tren']
  alignment_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of reads that successfully aligned to the reference genome. Low alignment rates may indicate sample contamination, wrong species, or library preparation failures. Sourced from samtools flagstat or DRAGEN alignment metrics.']
  assay_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sequencing assay type performed: Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), targeted panel, amplicon, RNA-seq, or other. Determines applicable coverage thresholds and QC pass/fail criteria for the run or library.']
  cluster_density_k_per_mm2 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of clusters per square millimeter on the flow cell surface, expressed in thousands (K/mm²). Optimal cluster density is critical for sequencing quality; too high causes overcrowding and quality degradation, too low reduces yield. Sourced from Illumina InterOp.']
  clusters_passing_filter_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of clusters that pass Illumina\'s chastity filter, indicating the proportion of high-quality reads retained after filtering. A key run-level QC metric; typically ≥75–85% is expected for acceptable run quality. Sourced from Illumina InterOp.']
  coverage_uniformity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of target bases covered at ≥20% of the mean coverage depth, measuring how evenly reads are distributed across the target region. High uniformity indicates consistent library preparation and sequencing performance. Sourced from Picard or samtools.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this QC metric record was first created in the system, typically upon automated ingestion from the source QC tool (InterOp, BaseSpace, Picard, or samtools). Supports audit trail requirements under GxP and FDA 21 CFR Part 11.']
  duplication_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of reads identified as PCR or optical duplicates, which are removed prior to variant calling. High duplication rates reduce effective coverage and indicate over-amplification during library preparation. Sourced from Picard MarkDuplicates.']
  error_rate_percent decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage of base calls that do not match the reference sequence, typically measured using PhiX control spike-in. Reflects sequencing chemistry and instrument performance. Lower values indicate higher accuracy. Sourced from Illumina InterOp.']
  gc_bias_index decimal(6,4) [note: 'Quantitative measure of GC content bias in sequencing coverage, indicating the degree to which GC-rich or GC-poor regions are over- or under-represented relative to expected coverage. Values near 0 indicate minimal bias. Sourced from Picard CollectGcBiasMetrics.']
  insert_size_std_dev_bp decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard deviation of the insert size distribution in base pairs. High standard deviation indicates heterogeneous fragmentation, which can affect alignment accuracy and coverage uniformity. Sourced from Picard CollectInsertSizeMetrics.']
  mean_coverage_depth decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average number of times each base in the target region (genome, exome, or panel) is covered by sequencing reads, expressed as fold-coverage (X). Primary metric for assessing whether sufficient sequencing depth was achieved for reliable variant calling. Populated for library-level records. Sourced from Picard or samtools.']
  mean_insert_size_bp decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average size of the DNA fragment inserted between sequencing adapters, in base pairs. Reflects library preparation quality; deviations from expected insert size indicate degradation, over-fragmentation, or adapter contamination. Sourced from Picard CollectInsertSizeMetrics.']
  mean_phred_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average Phred quality score across all bases in the run or lane, representing overall base call accuracy. A Phred score of 30 corresponds to 99.9% accuracy. Used for instrument performance benchmarking and run-level QC trending.']
  median_coverage_depth decimal(10,2) [note: 'Median fold-coverage across the target region, less sensitive to outliers than mean coverage. Used alongside mean_coverage_depth to assess coverage uniformity and detect regions of extreme over- or under-representation. Sourced from Picard or samtools.']
  metric_granularity_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the level of aggregation at which this QC metric record applies: \'run\' for instrument-wide metrics, \'lane\' for per-lane flow cell metrics, or \'library\' for per-library/per-sample coverage metrics. Determines which metric fields are populated and how the record is used in QC workflows.']
  metric_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the upstream tool or system from which this QC metric record was derived: Illumina InterOp files, BaseSpace, bcl2fastq demultiplexing stats, Picard alignment tools, samtools, DRAGEN, or other. Supports data lineage tracking and metric reproducibility audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: InterOp|BaseSpace|bcl2fastq|Picard|samtools|DRAGEN|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  on_target_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of aligned reads that map to the intended target region (exome capture or targeted panel). Applicable for WES and targeted sequencing assays. Low on-target rates indicate poor library enrichment or capture efficiency. Sourced from Picard CollectHsMetrics.']
  pct_bases_at_100x decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraction of target bases covered at a minimum depth of 100X, expressed as a percentage. Used for high-depth targeted sequencing panels, somatic variant calling, and liquid biopsy applications requiring high sensitivity at low allele frequencies. Sourced from Picard or samtools.']
  pct_bases_at_10x decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraction of target bases covered at a minimum depth of 10X, expressed as a percentage. Used as a coverage adequacy threshold for low-depth applications and as a QC gate for variant calling sensitivity. Sourced from Picard CollectHsMetrics or samtools depth.']
  pct_bases_at_20x decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraction of target bases covered at a minimum depth of 20X, expressed as a percentage. Standard coverage threshold for WES clinical assays; used to assess whether sufficient depth was achieved for reliable heterozygous variant detection. Sourced from Picard or samtools.']
  pct_bases_at_30x decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraction of target bases covered at a minimum depth of 30X, expressed as a percentage. Common coverage threshold for WGS clinical applications; used to confirm adequate depth for high-confidence variant calling across the genome. Sourced from Picard or samtools.']
  pf_reads varchar [note: 'Number of reads passing Illumina\'s chastity filter (PF reads), representing high-quality reads used in downstream alignment and variant calling. Distinct from total_reads which includes all raw clusters. Sourced from Illumina InterOp or bcl2fastq.']
  phix_alignment_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of reads that align to the PhiX bacteriophage control genome, which is spiked into sequencing runs as an internal quality control standard. Used to validate sequencing chemistry performance and calibrate error rate measurements.']
  phix_error_rate_percent decimal(6,4) [note: 'Sequencing error rate calculated from PhiX control reads, providing an unbiased measure of instrument and chemistry accuracy independent of sample biology. Sourced from Illumina InterOp. Used for instrument performance trending and GxP compliance.']
  q30_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of bases with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (Q30), indicating ≥99.9% base call accuracy. Industry-standard instrument performance metric; Illumina typically requires ≥75–80% Q30 for run acceptance. Sourced from Illumina InterOp or BaseSpace.']
  qc_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of the reason(s) for QC failure or warning status, such as \'Q30 below threshold\', \'high duplication rate\', or \'low on-target rate\'. Populated when qc_status is \'fail\' or \'warning\'. Supports root-cause analysis and corrective action tracking.']
  qc_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the scientist or QA analyst who performed the manual QC review and sign-off for this metric record. Required for GxP audit trail and electronic signature compliance under FDA 21 CFR Part 11.']
  qc_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the QC review and sign-off was completed for this metric record. Supports GxP audit trail, turnaround time (TAT) tracking, and regulatory submission documentation.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Overall QC determination for this metric record: \'pass\' indicates all applicable thresholds met, \'fail\' indicates one or more critical thresholds breached, \'warning\' indicates marginal performance requiring review, \'pending_review\' indicates awaiting manual QC sign-off, \'invalidated\' indicates the run or library was voided. Central to GxP compliance reporting and run release decisions.']
  read_length_bp varchar [note: 'Length of sequencing reads in base pairs (bp) for this run or library (e.g., 150 bp for paired-end 150). Determines the resolution of variant calling and structural variant detection. Sourced from run configuration in Illumina BaseSpace.']
  read_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the sequencing run used single-end or paired-end read chemistry. Paired-end reads provide improved alignment accuracy and structural variant detection. Affects coverage calculation and bioinformatics pipeline configuration.']
  run_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the sequencing run was initiated. Used as the principal business event date for instrument performance trending, throughput reporting, and time-series QC analysis.']
  total_reads varchar [note: 'Total number of sequencing reads generated for the run, lane, or library before quality filtering. Used to assess raw sequencing throughput and as the denominator for percentage-based QC metrics such as clusters passing filter and undetermined index rate.']
  undetermined_index_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of reads that could not be assigned to a sample index during demultiplexing, indicating index hopping, contamination, or library preparation issues. High undetermined rates may trigger run failure or sample reprocessing. Sourced from bcl2fastq demultiplexing stats.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this QC metric record was last modified, such as when a QC status was updated following manual review or when a metric value was corrected. Required for GxP audit trail and data integrity compliance.']
  yield_gb decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total sequencing output in gigabases (Gb) for the run, lane, or library. Represents the volume of sequence data generated and is used to assess whether sufficient data was produced to meet coverage requirements for the assay type. Sourced from Illumina BaseSpace or bcl2fastq.']

  Note: 'Transactional record storing quantitative quality metrics for sequencing runs at multiple granularity levels: run-level, lane-level, and per-library. Instrument-level metrics include Q30%, mean Phred score, error rate, cluster density, clusters passing filter, yield (Gb), PhiX alignment and error rate, and undetermined index rate. Per-library coverage metrics include mean/median coverage depth (X), coverage uniformity (%), fraction of bases at ≥10X/≥20X/≥30X/≥100X thresholds, on-target rate (% for WES/targeted), duplication rate (%), insert size statistics, and GC bias. Each metric row is attributed to a run, optionally a lane, and optionally a library — enabling both run-level pass/fail determination and per-library coverage adequacy assessment. Sourced from Illumina InterOp files, BaseSpace, bcl2fastq stats, and alignment tools (Picard, samtools). Supports instrument performance trending, assay-specific coverage thresholds, and GxP compliance reporting.'
}

Table sequencing.demux_result {
  demux_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each demultiplexing result record. Primary key for the demux_result data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — captures the discrete business event of demultiplexing a pooled sequencing run into per-sample FASTQ outputs.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Demultiplexing results are delivered to customers and must be tied to accounts for data delivery tracking, consent management, and billing. Customer data portals and delivery pipelines require direct ']
  clinical_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.clinical_specimen. Business justification: Demux results are produced for a specific clinical specimen. While demux_result links to sample_specimen_id, the clinical_specimen carries accession number and chain of custody. Direct link enables CL']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: Demultiplexing results are evaluated in experiment context to determine if per-sample read counts meet experimental design (e.g., minimum reads per sample for statistical power). Drives sample reseque']
  library_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing library (prepared sample) whose reads were assigned during demultiplexing. Provides the authoritative link between the demux result and the source library.']
  original_demux_result_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original demux_result record when this record represents a reprocessing event (reprocessing_flag = True). Enables full lineage tracing from reprocessed results back to the original demultiplexing output. Null when reprocessing_flag = False.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the research or clinical project under which this sequencing and demultiplexing activity was performed. Enables project-level aggregation of demux results for reporting, billing, and data governance purposes.']
  qc_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Demultiplexing results represent a formal QC checkpoint (qc_pass_flag, qc_failure_reason on demux_result). In CLIA/CAP regulated genomics, the demux QC outcome must be traceable to a formal qc_result ']
  run_lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.run_lane. Business justification: Demultiplexing results are generated per lane within a sequencing run. demux_result currently has run_id (to sequencing_run) and lane_number (integer), but no formal FK to the run_lane master record. ']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing run from which this demultiplexing result was produced. Links the demux output back to the originating instrument run and flow cell.']
  service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_agreement. Business justification: Data delivery format, retention period, turnaround time, and release conditions for demux results are governed by the customer\'s service agreement. Direct linkage enables automated compliance checking']
  software_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.software_release. Business justification: Demultiplexing results are produced by specific software versions. Columns demux_software and demux_software_version are denormalized from product.software_release. For data provenance, regulatory sub']
  test_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_order. Business justification: Demultiplexing results are produced per-sample as part of a clinical test order workflow. Linking demux_result to test_order enables direct traceability from raw demux output to the clinical order for']
  assigned_read_count varchar [note: 'Total number of sequencing reads assigned to this library after demultiplexing. Primary quantitative outcome of the demux process. Used to assess library representation and coverage depth adequacy for downstream analysis.']
  assigned_read_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of total reads in the lane assigned to this library (assigned_read_count / total lane reads × 100). Indicates library balance within the pool. Values significantly deviating from expected multiplexing ratio trigger QC review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this demux result record was first captured in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and GxP compliance.']
  data_release_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FASTQ files produced by this demux result have been formally released for downstream bioinformatics processing or delivery to the customer/requester. True = released; False = held (pending QC review or approval). Supports data governance and release management workflows.']
  demux_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the demultiplexing process completed for this library within the sequencing run. Principal business event timestamp. Used for turnaround time (TAT) calculation and SLA monitoring.']
  demux_run_identifier varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the demultiplexing execution job, as assigned by the demux software (e.g., bcl2fastq job ID or DRAGEN BCL Convert run name). Used for cross-referencing with Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub and bioinformatics pipeline logs.']
  demux_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the demultiplexing process was initiated for this run. Used in conjunction with demux_completion_timestamp to calculate demux processing duration for performance monitoring.']
  demux_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demultiplexing job for this library-run combination. \'completed\' indicates successful assignment; \'partial\' indicates some reads assigned but below threshold; \'failed\' indicates job error or no reads assigned.']
  fastq_i1_path varchar [note: 'Storage path or URI to the FASTQ file containing Index Read 1 (i7 index) sequences. Generated when index reads are retained in FASTQ format. Used for index QC validation and troubleshooting demultiplexing failures.']
  fastq_r1_path varchar [note: 'Storage path or URI to the FASTQ file containing Read 1 (forward reads) generated for this library after demultiplexing. Provides the authoritative link between the demux result and the resulting FASTQ output file for downstream bioinformatics pipeline ingestion.']
  fastq_r2_path varchar [note: 'Storage path or URI to the FASTQ file containing Read 2 (reverse reads) generated for this library after demultiplexing. Populated only for paired-end sequencing runs. Null for single-end runs.']
  index_mismatch_count varchar [note: 'Number of reads assigned to this library that contained one or more base mismatches in the index sequence relative to the expected index, within the allowed mismatch tolerance. Elevated counts may indicate index synthesis errors or library contamination.']
  index_sequence varchar [note: 'The nucleotide index sequence (i7 and optionally i5 dual-index) matched to assign reads to this library during demultiplexing. Supports single-index (e.g., ACGTACGT) and dual-index (e.g., ACGTACGT+TTGGCCAA) formats. Critical for multiplexing validation and index collision analysis.']
  instrument_platform varchar [note: 'The sequencing instrument platform model used for the run (e.g., NovaSeq 6000, NovaSeq X, NextSeq 2000, MiSeq). Determines flow cell format, lane count, and applicable demux parameters. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NovaSeq_6000|NovaSeq_X|NextSeq_2000|MiSeq|HiSeq_4000|PromethION|other — promote to reference product]']
  lane_number varchar [note: 'The physical lane number on the flow cell from which reads were demultiplexed. Flow cells may contain 1, 2, 4, or 8 lanes depending on instrument type (e.g., NovaSeq, HiSeq). Required for per-lane QC analysis and troubleshooting.']
  mean_phred_score_r1 decimal(6,2) [note: 'Mean Phred quality score across all bases in Read 1 for this library. Phred score (Q score) represents base call accuracy (Q30 = 99.9% accuracy). A key NGS QC metric; values below Q30 threshold may trigger QC failure.']
  mean_phred_score_r2 decimal(6,2) [note: 'Mean Phred quality score across all bases in Read 2 for this library. Typically lower than R1 due to signal degradation in paired-end sequencing. Null for single-end runs.']
  min_read_count_threshold varchar [note: 'The minimum number of assigned reads required for this library to pass QC, as defined by the applicable assay protocol or SOP. Stored at the record level to capture the threshold in effect at the time of demultiplexing, supporting historical QC audits.']
  mismatch_tolerance varchar [note: 'Number of allowable base mismatches permitted when matching a read\'s index sequence to the expected library index during demultiplexing. Typically 0 or 1. Higher values increase assignment rate but risk index cross-contamination between libraries.']
  pct_q30_r1 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of Read 1 bases with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (≥Q30). Industry-standard NGS QC threshold; Illumina specifies ≥75–80% Q30 as passing for most platforms. Used in run-level and library-level QC reporting.']
  pct_q30_r2 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of Read 2 bases with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (≥Q30). Null for single-end runs. Compared against run-specific QC thresholds for paired-end quality assessment.']
  qc_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Descriptive reason for QC failure when qc_pass_flag = False. Examples include: \'low_read_count\', \'high_undetermined_rate\', \'index_collision\', \'low_q30\'. Populated by automated QC rules or manual review. Null when qc_pass_flag = True.']
  qc_pass_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the demultiplexing result for this library passed all defined QC thresholds (read count, percentage assigned, undetermined rate). True = QC passed; False = QC failed. Authoritative gate for downstream bioinformatics pipeline processing.']
  read_length_r1 varchar [note: 'Length in base pairs (bp) of Read 1 sequences produced for this library. Determined by the sequencing run configuration. Critical for downstream alignment and variant calling pipeline compatibility.']
  read_length_r2 varchar [note: 'Length in base pairs (bp) of Read 2 sequences produced for this library. Null for single-end runs. Used to confirm paired-end symmetry and pipeline input validation.']
  reprocessing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this demux result was generated by reprocessing a previously demultiplexed run (e.g., due to sample sheet correction, software update, or QC failure remediation). True = reprocessed; False = original demux. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  run_use_type varchar [note: 'Intended use classification of the sequencing run from which this demux result originates. RUO (Research Use Only), IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic), LDT (Laboratory Developed Test), clinical_validation, or research. Governs applicable regulatory requirements and data handling obligations.']
  sample_sheet_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the sample sheet used to configure the demultiplexing run. The sample sheet defines library-to-index mappings and run parameters. Versioning ensures traceability and supports reprocessing with corrected configurations.']
  sequencing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sequencing assay type for this library (e.g., WGS — Whole Genome Sequencing, WES — Whole Exome Sequencing, targeted panel, amplicon, RNA-seq). Determines applicable QC thresholds and downstream bioinformatics pipeline routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted|amplicon|RNA-seq|single-cell|metagenomics|other — promote to reference product]']
  total_lane_read_count varchar [note: 'Total number of reads generated in the flow cell lane from which this library was demultiplexed. Used as the denominator for calculating assigned_read_pct and undetermined_read_pct. Enables per-lane yield assessment.']
  undetermined_read_count varchar [note: 'Number of reads in the lane that could not be assigned to any library due to index mismatch or ambiguity. High undetermined counts indicate index contamination, library preparation issues, or incorrect sample sheet configuration.']
  undetermined_read_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of total lane reads that were unassigned (undetermined_read_count / total lane reads × 100). A key QC metric; values exceeding the defined threshold (typically >5%) trigger run-level QC failure investigation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this demux result record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking and data lineage auditing.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the output of the demultiplexing process (bcl2fastq / DRAGEN BCL Convert) for each library within a pooled sequencing run. Records library ID, run ID, lane, index sequence matched, read count assigned, percentage of reads assigned, undetermined read count, mismatch tolerance used, demux software version, demux completion timestamp, and demux QC pass/fail status. Provides the authoritative link between a sequencing run and the resulting per-sample FASTQ files.'
}

Table sequencing.fastq_file {
  fastq_file_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the FASTQ file record in the sequencing data inventory. Primary key for this master resource entity representing a raw sequencing output file.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: FASTQ files are delivered to customers; account linkage is required for data access control, PHI consent enforcement, retention policy management, and customer billing. Genomics data portals enforce p']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: FASTQ files from IVD/clinical runs must be traceable to the regulatory approval governing their use classification for data governance and regulatory compliance. \'intended_use_class\' is a denormalized']
  assay_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.assay_version. Business justification: FASTQ files are generated under a specific assay version\'s sequencing parameters (read length, pipeline version, quality thresholds). CLIA labs must document which assay version governed the sequencin']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing instrument (e.g., Illumina NovaSeq, NextSeq, MiSeq) that generated the raw data for this FASTQ file. Enables instrument-level performance tracking, maintenance correlation, and QC trend analysis.']
  clinical_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.clinical_specimen. Business justification: FASTQ files are derived from a clinical specimen. While fastq_file links to sample_specimen_id, the clinical_specimen carries accession number, chain of custody, and consent status. Direct link enable']
  demux_result_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demultiplexing process execution that generated this FASTQ file from the raw BCL sequencing output. Enables traceability to the specific demultiplexing parameters, software version, and sample sheet used.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: FASTQ files are primary sequencing outputs evaluated per-experiment for data completeness and quality. Linking enables experiment-level data release decisions, analysis pipeline triggering based on ex']
  extraction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.extraction. Business justification: FASTQ files are the digital output derived from extracted nucleic acid. CAP/CLIA and GxP data lineage requirements mandate tracing each FASTQ file back to its source extraction event for audit trails ']
  flow_cell_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flow cell consumable used in the sequencing run that produced this FASTQ file. Enables flow cell-level QC analysis, lot traceability for reagent investigations, and consumable performance benchmarking.']
  library_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing library (prepared sample) from which this FASTQ file was generated. Enables traceability from raw data back to library preparation and upstream sample management.']
  license_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.license_entitlement. Business justification: FASTQ file storage and processing consume license entitlements (storage_quota_gb, data_processing_limit_gb). For license compliance reporting and usage-based billing in cloud genomics platforms, files']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.bioinformatics_pipeline_run. Business justification: FASTQ files are the direct input consumed by bioinformatics pipeline runs. Data management systems must track which pipeline run processed each FASTQ for reprocessing decisions, data release gating, a']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the research or clinical project under which this FASTQ file was generated. Enables project-level data aggregation, access control scoping, and cost allocation for sequencing activities.']
  qc_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: FASTQ files undergo formal QC assessment (qc_status, qc_failure_reason on fastq_file). In regulated clinical genomics (CLIA/CAP), the QC result for each FASTQ file must be formally documented and trac']
  research_protocol_id varchar [note: 'The IRB protocol identifier under which human subject samples associated with this FASTQ file were collected and sequenced. Required for clinical and research genomics data governance, consent verification, and regulatory audit trails.']
  run_lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.run_lane. Business justification: FASTQ files are generated per lane and currently have lane_number (integer) but no FK to run_lane. Adding run_lane_id links FASTQ files to their lane master record, enabling retrieval of lane-level QC']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing run that produced this FASTQ file. Links the file to its originating instrument run for traceability and run-level quality assessment.']
  service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_agreement. Business justification: FASTQ file retention policy, data sharing restrictions, encryption requirements, and delivery SLAs are contractually defined in the service agreement. Direct linkage enables automated policy enforceme']
  software_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.software_release. Business justification: FASTQ files are generated by specific basecalling/demultiplexing software versions. For data provenance, regulatory submissions (FDA, EMA), and FAIR data principles, the software release that generate']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: FASTQ file release decisions (pipeline_ready_flag, data_release_flag) are made against product specifications (Q30 threshold, read length, quality score). For data release QC and regulatory compliance']
  test_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_order. Business justification: FASTQ files are the primary raw data output for a clinical test order. CLIA audit trails require direct traceability from raw sequencing data files to the clinical test order, enabling data release au']
  adapter_trimming_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether adapter sequences were trimmed from reads during or after demultiplexing. Adapter trimming affects downstream alignment and variant calling accuracy. Relevant for pipeline configuration validation.']
  basespace_dataset_code varchar [note: 'The unique dataset identifier assigned by Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub to the dataset object containing this FASTQ file. Used for cross-referencing and API retrieval from BaseSpace.']
  basespace_file_code varchar [note: 'The unique file-level identifier assigned by Illumina BaseSpace Sequence Hub to this specific FASTQ file object. Enables direct file-level API operations and provenance tracking within BaseSpace.']
  compression_format varchar [note: 'The compression algorithm applied to the FASTQ file. Standard for NGS data is gzip (.gz). Determines decompression requirements for bioinformatics pipeline ingestion and storage cost calculations.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'The informed consent status of the subject associated with this FASTQ file\'s sample. Governs permissible uses of the genomic data (research, clinical, secondary use). Withdrawn consent triggers data deletion or de-identification workflows per GDPR and HIPAA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this FASTQ file record was first created in the sequencing data inventory (Silver Layer). Audit trail timestamp for data governance and lineage tracking.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'The data sensitivity classification level assigned to this FASTQ file per Genomics Biotech data governance policy. Restricted = highest sensitivity (PHI/PII), Confidential = sensitive business data, Internal = general internal use, Public = no restrictions. Drives access control, encryption, and retention policies.']
  encryption_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this FASTQ file is encrypted at rest in cloud storage. Encryption is mandatory for PHI-containing files under HIPAA Security Rule and for GxP-regulated data. Supports security compliance auditing.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'The full file name of the FASTQ output file including extension (e.g., SampleA_S1_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz). Follows Illumina naming conventions encoding sample, lane, read number, and index.']
  file_path varchar [note: 'Cloud storage URI indicating the full path to the FASTQ file on BaseSpace or Amazon S3 (e.g., s3://genomics-biotech-seq/runs/RUN001/SampleA_R1.fastq.gz). Serves as the authoritative location reference for downstream bioinformatics pipeline access.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'The size of the FASTQ file in bytes as stored in cloud storage. Used for storage capacity planning, data transfer cost estimation, and file integrity verification. Compressed (gzip) size is recorded.']
  file_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the FASTQ file in the sequencing data inventory. Pending = demultiplexing in progress, Available = ready for pipeline consumption, Archived = moved to cold storage, Deleted = purged per retention policy, Failed = generation error, Quarantined = held for QC investigation.']
  generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this FASTQ file was generated by the demultiplexing process. Represents the principal business event timestamp — the moment raw sequencing data became available as a FASTQ artifact. Used for TAT (Turnaround Time) calculations and data lineage.']
  index_sequence varchar [note: 'The nucleotide index sequence (barcode) used to identify and demultiplex this sample from a multiplexed sequencing run. Composed of ACGT bases. Critical for validating correct sample assignment and detecting index hopping or cross-contamination.']
  md5_checksum varchar [note: 'The MD5 cryptographic hash of the FASTQ file used to verify file integrity during transfer, storage, and archival. Mandatory for data integrity validation in GxP-regulated environments and regulatory submissions.']
  mean_phred_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The mean Phred quality score (Q-score) across all bases in this FASTQ file. Phred scores represent the probability of a base call error (Q30 = 99.9% accuracy). A key QC metric for raw sequencing data quality assessment per NGS best practices.']
  mean_read_length_bp varchar [note: 'The mean length of sequencing reads in this FASTQ file measured in base pairs (bp). Reflects the sequencing chemistry and run configuration. Relevant for alignment pipeline parameter selection and quality benchmarking.']
  pct_bases_q30 decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of bases in this FASTQ file with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (≥Q30), indicating ≥99.9% base call accuracy. Industry-standard QC threshold for NGS data quality; typically ≥80% Q30 is required for clinical-grade sequencing.']
  pct_reads_passing_filter decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of reads in this FASTQ file that passed the Illumina chastity filter (PF reads). Reads failing the filter are excluded from analysis. A key indicator of cluster quality and flow cell performance.']
  phi_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this FASTQ file is associated with Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined under HIPAA. When True, the file is subject to HIPAA Privacy and Security Rule requirements, access restrictions, and audit logging obligations. Drives data governance and access control enforcement.']
  pipeline_ready_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this FASTQ file has passed all QC checks and is cleared for ingestion into bioinformatics analysis pipelines. When True, the file is eligible for alignment, variant calling, and downstream processing. Prevents failed or quarantined files from entering production pipelines.']
  qc_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for QC failure when qc_status is \'fail\' or \'review\'. Documents specific quality issues (e.g., low Q30 percentage, insufficient read count, adapter contamination) for root cause analysis and CAPA processes.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'The QC assessment outcome for this FASTQ file based on Phred scores, read counts, and other quality metrics. Pass = meets all QC thresholds, Fail = below minimum thresholds, Review = borderline requiring manual review, Not Assessed = QC not yet performed. Governs eligibility for downstream bioinformatics processing.']
  read_number varchar [note: 'Designates the read type of this FASTQ file within a paired-end or indexed sequencing run. R1 = forward read, R2 = reverse read, I1 = index 1 (i7), I2 = index 2 (i5). Critical for demultiplexing validation and downstream alignment pipeline configuration.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'The calculated date on which this FASTQ file becomes eligible for deletion or archival based on the applied retention policy. Enables automated data lifecycle management and compliance with regulatory retention mandates.']
  retention_policy varchar [note: 'The data retention policy applied to this FASTQ file, governing how long the file must be retained before eligible for deletion or archival. Clinical data typically requires 10-year retention per CLIA/CAP; RUO data may follow shorter policies. Drives automated lifecycle management.']
  sequencing_assay_type varchar [note: 'The type of NGS sequencing assay that produced this FASTQ file. Determines applicable bioinformatics pipelines, QC thresholds, and regulatory classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|amplicon|RNA-seq|metagenomics|single_cell — promote to reference product]']
  storage_tier varchar [note: 'The cloud storage tier in which this FASTQ file currently resides. Hot = frequently accessed (active analysis), Warm = infrequent access, Cold = rare access, Archive = long-term retention (e.g., AWS Glacier). Drives storage cost management and retrieval SLA expectations.']
  total_read_count varchar [note: 'The total number of sequencing reads contained in this FASTQ file after demultiplexing. A primary indicator of sequencing yield for this sample-lane combination. Used to assess whether minimum coverage depth thresholds are met for downstream analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this FASTQ file record was most recently modified in the sequencing data inventory. Tracks status changes, QC updates, and metadata corrections for audit compliance.']
  upload_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this FASTQ file was successfully uploaded to cloud storage (BaseSpace or S3). Distinct from generation_timestamp; captures the transfer completion event for data availability tracking and SLA monitoring.']

  Note: 'Master record representing a FASTQ output file generated from demultiplexing. Captures file name, file path (cloud storage URI on BaseSpace/S3), file size (bytes), read number (R1/R2/I1/I2), total read count, mean read length (bp), compression format (gzip), MD5 checksum, generation timestamp, associated library and run, BaseSpace dataset ID, retention policy, and data classification (PHI/non-PHI). SSOT for raw sequencing data file inventory — the primary output artifact of the sequencing domain.'
}

Table sequencing.coverage_stat {
  coverage_stat_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each coverage statistic record computed post-demultiplexing for a sequencing library. Primary key for the coverage_stat data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Coverage statistics are reported to customers for QC sign-off, clinical reporting, and SLA compliance verification. Direct account linkage on coverage_stat enables per-customer QC dashboards and autom']
  assay_development_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.assay_development. Business justification: Coverage statistics (mean_coverage_depth, on_target_rate_pct, pct_bases_at_30x) are the core metrics evaluated in assay development validation. Direct linkage to assay_development enables validation s']
  assay_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.assay_version. Business justification: Coverage statistics are evaluated against assay-version-specific thresholds (minimum_coverage_depth, minimum_phred_score). Linking coverage_stat to assay_version enables version-specific QC pass/fail ']
  demux_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.demux_result. Business justification: Coverage statistics are computed from demultiplexed reads produced by the demultiplexing process. Adding demux_result_id to coverage_stat directly links the coverage computation to the specific demux ']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: Coverage statistics are analyzed per-experiment to assess whether sequencing depth meets experimental objectives (e.g., variant detection sensitivity requirements). Drives resequencing decisions, expe']
  extraction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.extraction. Business justification: Coverage statistics are correlated with extraction quality in method validation studies and QC trending dashboards. Linking coverage_stat to extraction enables analysis of how extraction RIN/yield imp']
  fastq_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.fastq_file. Business justification: Coverage statistics are computed directly from FASTQ output files post-demultiplexing. Adding fastq_file_id to coverage_stat creates a direct, traceable link from the computed coverage metrics back to']
  genomic_artifact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.genomic_artifact. Business justification: Coverage statistics are computed directly from BAM/alignment genomic artifacts. Labs must link coverage stats to the specific artifact for reprocessing triggers when artifacts are updated, QC dashboar']
  library_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing library for which this coverage statistic record was computed. Links to the library preparation entity in the sequencing domain.']
  oos_investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.oos_investigation. Business justification: Coverage stats below threshold (mean_coverage_depth < min_coverage_threshold on coverage_stat) trigger OOS investigations in clinical genomics. No existing FK covers this path. A clinical genomics QA ']
  performing_laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.performing_laboratory. Business justification: Coverage statistics are computed for runs performed at a specific performing laboratory. Linking enables per-lab coverage QC trending, CAP proficiency testing compliance reporting, and inter-lab perfo']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bioinformatics pipeline run that computed these coverage statistics post-demultiplexing. Enables data lineage tracing from raw FASTQ to coverage metrics.']
  qc_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Coverage statistics are a formal QC checkpoint in clinical genomics (qc_status on coverage_stat). In CLIA/CAP regulated labs, coverage QC outcomes must be traceable to formal qc_result records for sam']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample associated with the library. Enables traceability from coverage statistics back to the originating sample in the Sample Management domain.']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sequencing run that produced the raw reads from which this coverage statistic was derived. Enables traceability from coverage metrics back to the instrument run.']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Coverage statistics are evaluated against product specifications to determine approved_for_analysis status. Columns min_coverage_threshold and min_pct_bases_threshold are denormalized from product.spe']
  test_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical or research assay definition against which coverage QC thresholds are evaluated. Determines the minimum coverage requirements applicable to this library.']
  approved_for_analysis boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this library\'s coverage statistics have been reviewed and approved for downstream bioinformatics analysis (variant calling, annotation). Set to True only when qc_status is Pass and any required manual review has been completed.']
  assay_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sequencing assay type for which coverage statistics are computed. Determines applicable QC thresholds and on-target rate relevance. WGS (Whole Genome Sequencing), WES (Whole Exome Sequencing), Targeted panel, Amplicon, or RNA-Seq. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|Targeted|Amplicon|RNA-Seq|Other — promote to reference product if additional assay types are added]']
  computed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the coverage statistics were computed by the bioinformatics pipeline post-demultiplexing. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this record. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  coverage_uniformity_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of target bases covered at greater than or equal to 20% of the mean coverage depth. Measures how evenly reads are distributed across the target region. Higher uniformity indicates more consistent library preparation and hybridization efficiency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this coverage statistic record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  duplication_rate_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of mapped reads identified as PCR or optical duplicates.
