Critical for library preparation normalization in Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) workflows and Quality Control (QC) acceptance criteria.']
  concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the concentration value recorded in the concentration field. Common units include ng/µL (nanograms per microliter) for DNA/RNA quantification, nM (nanomolar) for library quantification, and copies/µL for digital Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) applications.']
  container_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the physical container holding the aliquot. \'in_use\' indicates container holds active aliquot material; \'empty\' indicates container has been emptied; \'discarded\' indicates container has been disposed of; \'pre_registered\' indicates container registered in LabVantage LIMS prior to receiving material (empty container inventory management).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this aliquot record was first created in the source LabVantage LIMS system and ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports audit trail requirements under GxP, CLIA, and SOX financial reporting for laboratory operations.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date on which the aliquot was physically disposed of or destroyed per retention policy, consent withdrawal, or regulatory requirement. Populated when lifecycle_status transitions to \'disposed\' or \'destroyed\'. Required for audit trail completeness under GxP and HIPAA.']
  disposal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the disposal or destruction of the aliquot. Supports regulatory audit trails and consent management reporting. \'consent_withdrawn\' is critical for GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure and HIPAA consent revocation workflows.']
  dna_integrity_number decimal(4,2) [note: 'DNA Integrity Number (DIN) score measured by TapeStation, ranging from 1 (fully degraded) to 10 (fully intact). Applicable to genomic DNA aliquots. Used to assess suitability for Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS) and Whole Exome Sequencing (WES) library preparation. Null for non-DNA aliquots.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the aliquot is considered expired and should not be used in clinical or research workflows. Calculated based on specimen type, storage conditions, and validated stability data per Standard Operating Procedure (SOP). Triggers automated alerts in LabVantage LIMS for disposal or review.']
  freeze_thaw_count varchar [note: 'Number of freeze-thaw cycles the aliquot has undergone since creation. Excessive freeze-thaw cycles degrade nucleic acid integrity and protein activity. Used to enforce maximum freeze-thaw limits per Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) and to flag aliquots that may be unsuitable for sensitive assays.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this aliquot within the parent-child hierarchy. Level 1 indicates a direct sub-division of the primary specimen; level 2 indicates an aliquot-of-aliquot; and so on. Enables efficient hierarchy traversal queries and enforces maximum sub-division depth policies.']
  initial_volume_ul decimal(12,4) [note: 'Original volume of biological material dispensed into the aliquot container at the time of aliquoting, expressed in microliters (µL). Provides the baseline for volume consumption tracking and yield calculations.']
  is_phi_deidentified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) associated with this aliquot has been de-identified in accordance with HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination methods. True indicates de-identified; False indicates PHI is present and access controls apply. Governs data sharing eligibility for research and external collaborations.']
  label varchar [note: 'Human-readable label or name assigned to the aliquot for identification in laboratory workflows, reports, and LIMS displays. May include specimen accession number, aliquot sequence suffix, or study-specific naming conventions.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the aliquot in its operational lifecycle. \'available\' indicates ready for use; \'consumed\' indicates fully used in a workflow; \'depleted\' indicates volume exhausted; \'reserved\' indicates allocated to a pending workflow; \'quarantined\' indicates held pending Quality Control (QC) review; \'disposed\' indicates removed per retention policy; \'destroyed\' indicates intentionally destroyed per protocol. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|consumed|depleted|reserved|quarantined|disposed|destroyed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lims_aliquot_code varchar [note: 'The native aliquot identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS at the time of accessioning or aliquoting. Used as the operational cross-reference key between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the LIMS source system.']
  purity_ratio_260_230 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Absorbance ratio at 260nm to 230nm measured by spectrophotometry. Indicates contamination by chaotropic salts, EDTA, or carbohydrates. Acceptable range is typically 2.0–2.2. Complements the A260/A280 ratio for comprehensive nucleic acid Quality Control (QC) assessment.']
  purity_ratio_260_280 decimal(6,3) [note: 'Absorbance ratio at 260nm to 280nm measured by spectrophotometry (e.g., NanoDrop). Indicates nucleic acid purity; acceptable DNA purity is typically 1.8–2.0 and RNA purity 2.0–2.2. Values outside range may indicate protein or solvent contamination affecting downstream Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) performance.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Intended downstream use of the aliquot. Drives workflow routing in LabVantage LIMS and determines which bioinformatics pipelines or Quality Control (QC) processes the aliquot will enter. Values include sequencing (Next-Generation Sequencing/NGS workflows), qc (Quality Control testing), archive (long-term storage), backup (redundancy), assay_validation (Clinical Assay Validation), and research (Research and Development).']
  qc_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by laboratory personnel during Quality Control (QC) assessment. Documents observations, deviations, conditional pass justifications, or remediation actions taken. Supports audit trail requirements under Good Practice Regulations (GxP) and CLIA.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Result of the Quality Control (QC) assessment for this aliquot. \'pass\' indicates aliquot meets all acceptance criteria for its intended purpose; \'fail\' indicates one or more criteria not met; \'pending\' indicates QC not yet completed; \'conditional_pass\' indicates aliquot approved with documented exceptions; \'not_required\' indicates QC waived per Standard Operating Procedure (SOP).']
  rack_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the storage rack, plate, or box within the storage unit where this aliquot is positioned. Combined with well_position provides the precise physical location for robotic retrieval and manual locating in biobank operations.']
  rin_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'RNA Integrity Number (RIN) score measured by Bioanalyzer or TapeStation, ranging from 1 (fully degraded) to 10 (fully intact). Applicable to RNA aliquots only. Minimum RIN thresholds are defined per assay type (e.g., RIN ≥ 7 for RNA-seq library preparation). Null for non-RNA aliquots.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Biological specimen type of the aliquot material (e.g., whole blood, plasma, serum, saliva, tissue, FFPE, cell line, DNA extract, RNA extract, cfDNA). Inherited from the parent specimen and critical for downstream workflow routing and assay compatibility checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|saliva|tissue|ffpe|cell_line|dna_extract|rna_extract|cfdna|urine|csf|buffy_coat — promote to reference product]']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Required storage temperature for this aliquot in degrees Celsius (°C). Common values include -80°C (ultra-low for long-term nucleic acid storage), -20°C (standard freezer), 4°C (refrigerated), and ambient (15–25°C). Drives cold-chain compliance monitoring and storage assignment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this aliquot record in the source LabVantage LIMS system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture, and audit trail completeness under GxP and ISO 15189.']
  use_restriction_code varchar [note: 'Standardized data use restriction code governing permissible downstream uses of this aliquot and its derived data. Codes follow GA4GH/dbGaP conventions: GRU (General Research Use), HMB (Health/Medical/Biomedical), DS (Disease-Specific), POA (Population Origins/Ancestry), NPU (Non-Profit Use Only), RUO (Research Use Only). Enforced during data access governance reviews.']
  volume_ul decimal(12,4) [note: 'Current measured volume of biological material in the aliquot container, expressed in microliters (µL). Decremented as material is consumed in workflows. Critical for determining aliquot sufficiency for downstream assays such as Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) library preparation.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for volume fields (volume_ul and initial_volume_ul). Standard unit is microliters (µL) for most genomics laboratory workflows; milliliters (mL) may be used for larger biobank specimens.']
  well_position varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric position identifier within the storage rack, plate, or box (e.g., A01, B12, H08 for a 96-well plate layout). Used for precise physical localization of the aliquot within its storage container for robotic liquid handling and manual retrieval.']

  Note: 'Represents a physical sub-division of a parent specimen created during aliquoting workflows, including the container holding it. Tracks aliquot volume, concentration, unit of measure, container identity (barcode, type, manufacturer, lot number, material, volume capacity), position in storage rack/plate/well, aliquot purpose (sequencing, QC, archive, backup), current lifecycle status, and container status (in-use, empty, discarded). Maintains parent-child lineage back to the originating specimen and supports multi-level aliquot hierarchies (aliquot-of-aliquot). Also serves as the master record for empty container inventory when pre-registered in LabVantage. Managed with barcode-driven tracking.'
}

Table sample.accession {
  accession_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the accession record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — represents the formal intake event of a specimen into laboratory custody.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Accessions represent received samples from customer accounts. Required for order fulfillment tracking, billing reconciliation, customer-specific SLA enforcement, and regulatory classification routing.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Clinical genomics accessions for regulated IVD assays must reference the regulatory approval (510(k), CE-IVD) under which the assay is cleared. gxp_applicable_flag on accession signals GxP context. Re']
  authorized_orderer_id varchar [note: 'National Provider Identifier (NPI) or internal identifier of the ordering physician or clinical provider who requisitioned the test. Used for result reporting, billing, and clinical communication. Classified as confidential.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Lab accessioning links each incoming specimen to the ordered assay/test product (e.g., WGS panel, array). This drives TAT tracking, billing, and result reporting. assay_panel_code is a denormalized re']
  collection_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.collection_event. Business justification: Accession (formal laboratory intake) and collection_event (physical specimen collection) are closely coupled transactional records in the sample lifecycle. An accession record corresponds to a specifi']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.controlled_document. Business justification: Accessioning is performed under a controlled SOP (e.g., specimen receipt and accessioning procedure). GxP and CLIA compliance require the accession record to reference the governing controlled documen']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Accessioning deviations (e.g., specimen received outside protocol temperature window, missing requisition) must be formally documented. GxP-regulated genomics labs require the accession event to refer']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating clinical or research order that triggered specimen collection and submission. Links the accession to the order management domain, enabling traceability from test requisition through laboratory intake.']
  inbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_delivery. Business justification: Specimen accession maps to an inbound delivery record for chain-of-custody, cold-chain compliance, and logistics tracking. Genomics biotech labs receiving specimens from external sites use inbound del']
  installed_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.installed_instrument. Business justification: In decentralized genomics testing models, accessions are processed on customer-installed instruments (e.g., on-site sequencers). Linking accession to installed_instrument enables instrument utilizatio']
  irb_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.irb_submission. Business justification: Accessions for human subjects research must be traceable to the IRB submission authorizing sample collection — a GxP regulatory requirement. accession.irb_protocol_number is a denormalized plain attri']
  lab_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory site that physically received and accessioned the specimen. Supports multi-site operations, routing logic, and site-level compliance reporting under CLIA and CAP.']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Specimens arriving in unacceptable condition (wrong tube, hemolysis, temperature excursion) trigger a nonconformance record. Genomics labs require QMS linkage from the accession receipt event to the f']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the de-identified or pseudonymized patient/subject record associated with this specimen. Classified as restricted PHI under HIPAA. Used for clinical traceability while maintaining PHI separation per de-identification protocols.']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Accessions (sample receipt events) in clinical genomics labs are assigned to production batches for processing. Linking accession to production_batch enables TAT tracking from receipt to batch complet']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Accessioned samples are collected under R&D projects. Linking accessions to projects enables project-level cohort analysis, R&D spend allocation to sample processing, IRB compliance reporting by proje']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample master record in the Sample Management domain. Establishes the chain-of-custody link between the accession intake event and the canonical sample identity record.']
  service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_agreement. Business justification: Accessions are governed by customer service agreements that define TAT targets, QC thresholds, and billing terms. SLA compliance reporting and invoice generation for accessioned samples require knowin']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.study. Business justification: Accessions are received in the context of specific studies. Study-level accession tracking is needed for enrollment monitoring, sample accountability reports, and TAT reporting by study. accession alr']
  subject_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.subject. Business justification: accession records the formal intake of a specimen into the laboratory. The specimen originates from a subject (the de-identified/pseudonymized biological source individual). accession has patient_id (']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: In IVD/clinical genomics, sample accessions collected under regulated clinical trials or IVD studies must reference the governing regulatory submission (IND, IDE, PMA). accession.regulatory_submission']
  test_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_catalog. Business justification: An accession is created for a specific orderable test. Linking accession to test_catalog normalizes the assay_panel_code denormalization, enables TAT SLA enforcement per test type, drives specimen req']
  accession_number varchar [note: 'LIMS-generated, externally-visible unique identifier assigned to the specimen at the point of intake into LabVantage. Serves as the primary chain-of-custody reference number communicated to ordering clinicians, research teams, and downstream bioinformatics pipelines. Format: ACC-{YYYY}-{8-digit sequence}.']
  accession_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the accession record within LabVantage. Drives downstream routing: \'received\' = physically in lab but not yet reviewed; \'in_review\' = condition assessment underway; \'accepted\' = cleared for processing; \'rejected\' = specimen rejected and not processed; \'on_hold\' = pending additional information or consent; \'cancelled\' = order cancelled prior to processing.']
  accessioning_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the specimen was formally registered and accessioned into the LIMS by the receiving technician. May differ from receipt_timestamp if there is a delay between physical receipt and system registration. Used for TAT (Turnaround Time) calculations.']
  arrival_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the physical condition of the specimen upon arrival at the laboratory. \'acceptable\' = meets pre-analytical quality criteria; \'compromised\' = degraded but may still be processable with notation; \'rejected\' = fails minimum quality criteria and cannot be processed. Triggers rejection workflows and recollection requests when set to \'rejected\'.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the biological specimen was collected from the patient or subject. Critical for pre-analytical stability assessments, TAT calculations, and clinical result interpretation. Distinct from receipt_timestamp.']
  collection_site varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the clinical facility, hospital, clinic, or research site where the specimen was collected from the patient. Supports multi-site clinical trial tracking, referring provider analytics, and logistics routing.']
  collection_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the specimen was collected from the patient or subject, including timezone. Used for time-sensitive pre-analytical stability calculations (e.g., RNA degradation, cell viability) and clinical result contextualization.']
  collector_name varchar [note: 'Name of the phlebotomist, nurse, or clinician who collected the specimen from the patient. Required for chain-of-custody documentation and pre-analytical error investigation. Classified as confidential business data.']
  condition_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of observed pre-analytical issues or anomalies noted at specimen receipt (e.g., hemolysis, lipemia, insufficient volume, broken container, temperature excursion, clotted sample). Supports root cause analysis and pre-analytical QC reporting.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Status of the patient or subject\'s informed consent for specimen use in research or clinical testing. \'consented\' = valid consent obtained; \'declined\' = subject declined consent; \'pending\' = consent process initiated but not completed; \'withdrawn\' = previously consented subject has withdrawn consent; \'not_required\' = clinical specimen exempt from research consent requirements.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type of container or tube in which the specimen was collected and transported (e.g., EDTA tube, SST tube, PAXgene RNA tube, cryovial, FFPE block, dry swab). Determines extraction protocol eligibility and pre-analytical QC criteria. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: edta_tube|sst_tube|paxgene_rna|paxgene_dna|cryovial|ffpe_block|dry_swab|streck_tube|citrate_tube — promote to reference product]']
  courier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the courier or logistics provider that transported the specimen to the laboratory (e.g., FedEx, World Courier, BioMatik, internal courier). Used for vendor performance tracking and temperature excursion investigations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this accession record was first created in the source LIMS system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and GxP 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  external_requisition_number varchar [note: 'Test requisition number assigned by the ordering facility\'s own system (e.g., hospital LIS, EHR order number). Enables bidirectional reconciliation between the external ordering system and the internal LIMS accession record.']
  gxp_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accession falls under GxP (Good Practice Regulations) requirements including GMP, GLP, or GCP. True = GxP controls apply, requiring full audit trail, electronic signature compliance per 21 CFR Part 11, and enhanced documentation in MasterControl.']
  phi_deidentified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) associated with this accession has been de-identified per HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination methods. True = PHI has been de-identified and the record may be used in downstream analytics without PHI restrictions; False = PHI is present and full access controls apply.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Processing priority assigned to the accession at intake. STAT (Short Turn-Around Time) specimens require immediate processing; routine specimens follow standard workflow queues; urgent specimens are expedited but below STAT; research specimens follow non-clinical timelines. Drives SLA (Service Level Agreement) enforcement and workflow queue prioritization in LabVantage.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the specimen was physically received at the laboratory, marking the transition from external courier custody to laboratory custody. This is the principal business event timestamp for the accession transaction. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  recollection_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recollection request has been issued to the ordering provider or collection site due to specimen rejection or insufficient quality. True = recollection has been requested; False = no recollection required.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this accession is associated with a regulatory submission (e.g., FDA IND/NDA clinical trial specimen, CE-IVD validation study, EMA submission). True = specimen is part of a regulated submission context requiring enhanced documentation and audit trail.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason a specimen was rejected at accessioning. Populated only when arrival_condition = \'rejected\'. Used for pre-analytical QC trending, rejection rate KPI reporting, and recollection request generation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insufficient_volume|hemolysis|lipemia|clotted|wrong_tube|unlabeled|temperature_excursion|expired_container|broken_container|mislabeled — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the LabVantage LIMS source system for this accession. Used for data lineage traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer and the operational system of record, supporting reconciliation and re-ingestion workflows.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the biological specimen material submitted for analysis (e.g., whole blood, FFPE tissue, saliva, buccal swab, plasma, serum, urine, bone marrow, CSF). Drives QC thresholds, extraction protocols, and assay eligibility rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|ffpe_tissue|saliva|buccal_swab|plasma|serum|urine|bone_marrow|csf|fresh_frozen_tissue — promote to reference product]']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Laboratory storage location code (freezer, rack, box, position) where the specimen was placed upon acceptance. Supports physical specimen retrieval, inventory management, and chain-of-custody continuity within the LIMS.']
  tat_target_hours varchar [note: 'Target Turnaround Time (TAT) in hours from specimen receipt to result delivery, as defined by the SLA (Service Level Agreement) for the assigned priority level and assay type. Used for TAT compliance monitoring and SLA breach alerting.']
  temperature_at_receipt_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured temperature of the specimen or shipping container at the time of receipt, expressed in degrees Celsius. Used to assess cold-chain compliance and flag potential temperature excursions that may affect specimen integrity and downstream assay validity.']
  transport_temperature_code varchar [note: 'Required transport temperature condition under which the specimen was shipped to the laboratory. Used to verify compliance with specimen stability requirements and to flag potential temperature excursions during transit.']
  tube_label_code varchar [note: 'Barcode or label identifier physically affixed to the specimen container at the time of collection or receipt. Used for physical specimen tracking, label verification at accessioning, and reconciliation against the LIMS accession number.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this accession record was most recently modified in the source LIMS system. Supports change detection in the Silver Layer ETL pipeline and GxP audit trail requirements.']
  use_classification varchar [note: 'Intended use classification of the specimen at the time of accessioning. \'clinical\' = IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) or LDT (Laboratory Developed Test) clinical testing; \'research\' = RUO (Research Use Only) investigation; \'QC\' = Quality Control specimen for assay performance monitoring; \'validation\' = analytical validation or clinical validation study specimen.']
  volume_received_ml decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured volume of the specimen received at the laboratory, expressed in millilitres. Used to assess adequacy for requested assays, trigger insufficient volume rejection flags, and support aliquoting planning. Represents the principal quantitative measurement of the accession transaction.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the volume_received_ml field. Typically mL for liquid specimens; mg or g for solid tissue specimens. Allows the schema to accommodate non-liquid specimen types without type conversion.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal intake and registration of a specimen into the laboratory information management system (LabVantage). Records accession number (LIMS-generated), receipt date/time, receiving lab site, courier/shipment reference, condition on arrival (acceptable, compromised, rejected), accessioning technician, priority level (STAT, routine), and linkage to the originating clinical order (FK to order domain). Represents the chain-of-custody entry point — the moment a specimen transitions from external custody to laboratory custody.'
}

Table sample.biobank_location {
  biobank_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a physical storage location within the biorepository hierarchy. Primary key for this master resource entity.']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Change controls often affect storage locations (e.g., equipment upgrades, temperature setpoint changes). Labs link change controls to affected locations for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  lab_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.lab_site. Business justification: biobank_location (freezers, racks, shelves in the biorepository) is physically located within a lab_site. biobank_location has facility_site_code (STRING) and room_identifier (STRING) that partially c']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Biobank location failures (equipment malfunction, temperature excursion beyond recovery, contamination event) trigger nonconformance records affecting all stored specimens. Direct linkage from biobank']
  parent_location_biobank_location_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate parent location in the biorepository hierarchy (e.g., a box\'s parent is a rack, a rack\'s parent is a shelf). Enables recursive hierarchy traversal for chain-of-custody and retrieval workflows.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_site. Business justification: Biobank locations are physically within manufacturing sites in genomics biotech (e.g., -80C freezers in GMP facilities). Linking biobank_location to manufacturing_site enables site-level inventory man']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Biobank storage equipment (ULT freezers, cryogenic tanks) must undergo IQ/OQ/PQ validation studies per GMP/ISO 20387. The biobank_location must reference its governing validation study for equipment q']
  access_restriction_level varchar [note: 'Physical and logical access control classification for this storage location. Governs who may retrieve specimens from this location. \'restricted\' and above require role-based authorization in LabVantage LIMS and physical access badge control. Relevant for PHI-linked specimens and BSL-classified materials.']
  backup_power_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage unit is connected to an uninterruptible power supply (UPS) or emergency generator backup. Critical for specimen integrity risk assessment and business continuity planning for irreplaceable biobank specimens.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Machine-readable barcode or 2D matrix code (e.g., DataMatrix, QR) affixed to the physical storage unit or position label. Scanned during specimen accessioning, retrieval, and chain-of-custody verification in LabVantage LIMS.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which this storage location was formally commissioned and made available for specimen storage following successful IQ/OQ/PQ qualification. Marks the start of the location\'s operational lifecycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this biobank location record was first created in the system of record (LabVantage LIMS or enterprise data platform). Supports audit trail requirements under GxP, ISO 13485, and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which this storage location was permanently decommissioned and removed from active service. Null for locations still in service. Required for equipment lifecycle tracking and regulatory audit trails.']
  equipment_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer of the physical storage equipment (e.g., \'Thermo Fisher Scientific\', \'Panasonic\', \'Chart Industries\'). Required for vendor qualification records and regulatory equipment documentation.']
  equipment_model varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model designation of the physical storage equipment at this location (e.g., \'Thermo Scientific TSX Series -80°C Freezer\'). Used for equipment qualification documentation, spare parts management, and maintenance scheduling.']
  equipment_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number of the physical storage equipment. Required for equipment qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ) documentation, warranty tracking, and regulatory audit trails per ISO 13485 and GxP requirements.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Level of this location within the biorepository physical hierarchy: facility → room → freezer → shelf → rack → box → position (well/slot). Enables hierarchical chain-of-custody traversal and capacity roll-up reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: facility|room|freezer|shelf|rack|box|position — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_cryogenic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location uses cryogenic conditions (liquid nitrogen vapor or immersion, or mechanical ultra-low temperature ≤ -130°C). Drives safety protocol assignment, PPE requirements, and cryogenic handling SOP routing.']
  is_gmp_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location is subject to GMP regulations (e.g., for clinical trial specimens, IVD reagent intermediates, or GMP-grade biological materials). GMP-controlled locations require enhanced documentation, change control, and audit trail requirements.']
  is_phi_segregated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specimens stored at this location are physically segregated due to PHI linkage, requiring enhanced access controls and HIPAA-compliant chain-of-custody documentation. Supports de-identification workflow management.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent temperature calibration or verification performed on this storage unit. Required for GxP compliance and ISO 15189 accreditation. Tracked in MasterControl and SAP QM.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this biobank location record. Required for change control audit trails under FDA 21 CFR Part 11, ISO 13485, and GxP data integrity requirements.']
  lims_location_code varchar [note: 'Native location identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS for this storage position. Used for system-of-record cross-referencing, data lineage tracing, and integration reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the operational LIMS.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code assigned to this storage location within the biorepository management system (LabVantage LIMS). Used for labeling, chain-of-custody documentation, and specimen retrieval workflows. Example: FRZ-A01-SH3-RK2-BX05.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name for the storage location, used in operational reports and LIMS displays. Example: \'Freezer A1 – Shelf 3 – Rack 2 – Box 05\'.']
  monitoring_sensor_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the continuous temperature/humidity monitoring sensor assigned to this storage location. Links to the environmental monitoring system (EMS) for real-time excursion alerting and 21 CFR Part 11-compliant audit trails.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next temperature calibration or verification of this storage unit. Drives preventive maintenance work orders in SAP S/4HANA PM and compliance alerts in MasterControl.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or non-conformance observations associated with this storage location (e.g., \'Door seal replaced 2024-03-15\', \'Reserved for clinical trial XYZ specimens only\').']
  occupied_positions varchar [note: 'Current number of specimen positions occupied at this storage location as tracked by LabVantage LIMS. Enables real-time utilization monitoring and triggers capacity alerts when approaching full occupancy.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of this biobank storage location. \'active\' = available for specimen storage; \'maintenance\' = temporarily unavailable due to servicing; \'quarantine\' = specimens held pending investigation; \'decommissioned\' = permanently retired from service.']
  position_column varchar [note: 'Numeric column designator for a specific well or slot position within a box or rack (e.g., \'1\', \'12\'). Applicable at the position/well hierarchy level. Combined with position_row to form the full position address.']
  position_row varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric row designator for a specific well or slot position within a box or rack (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'H\'). Applicable at the position/well hierarchy level. Combined with position_column to form the full position address.']
  qualification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current GxP equipment qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ) expires and requalification is required. Drives preventive maintenance scheduling and regulatory compliance alerts in MasterControl.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'GxP qualification status of the storage equipment: \'qualified\' = Installation Qualification (IQ), Operational Qualification (OQ), and Performance Qualification (PQ) completed and approved; \'requalification_required\' = qualification expired or triggered by maintenance event. Mandatory for GMP/GLP-regulated specimen storage.']
  responsible_custodian varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the designated biorepository custodian or laboratory manager responsible for this storage location. Accountable for specimen integrity, access authorization, and regulatory compliance at this location.']
  rfid_tag varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier assigned to this storage unit for automated inventory scanning and real-time location tracking within the biorepository. Supports high-throughput specimen management workflows.']
  room_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory or storage room within the facility where this location physically resides. Used for access control, environmental monitoring zone assignment, and GxP area classification.']
  specimen_type_compatibility varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of specimen types approved for storage at this location (e.g., \'whole_blood,plasma,FFPE,DNA,RNA,cell_line\'). Enforced during specimen accessioning in LabVantage LIMS to prevent storage condition mismatches. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|FFPE|DNA|RNA|cell_line|tissue|urine|saliva — promote to reference product]']
  storage_medium varchar [note: 'Physical medium used for cryogenic or ambient storage at this location. Distinguishes mechanical freezer cooling from liquid nitrogen (LN2) vapor-phase or immersion-phase storage, which affects specimen viability protocols and safety requirements.']
  storage_unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical storage unit at this location level (e.g., freezer, refrigerator, liquid nitrogen tank, ambient cabinet). Drives temperature monitoring SOP assignment and equipment maintenance scheduling.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Upper acceptable temperature limit in degrees Celsius for this storage location. Excursions above this threshold trigger alarms and non-conformance events per SOP.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Lower acceptable temperature limit in degrees Celsius for this storage location. Excursions below this threshold trigger alarms and non-conformance events per SOP.']
  temperature_setpoint_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Nominal target storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this location (e.g., -80.00 for ultra-low freezer, -196.00 for liquid nitrogen, 4.00 for refrigerator, 22.00 for room temperature). Critical for specimen integrity and regulatory compliance.']
  total_capacity_positions varchar [note: 'Maximum number of specimen positions (wells, slots, or tubes) this storage location can physically accommodate. Used for capacity planning, utilization reporting, and biorepository space management.']

  Note: 'Master data entity representing a physical storage location within the biorepository hierarchy — freezer, shelf, rack, box, and well/position. Captures location code, storage unit type, temperature setpoint (e.g., -80°C, -196°C LN2, 4°C, RT), facility site, capacity (total positions, occupied positions), and current operational status. Enables precise chain-of-custody tracking and retrieval of specimens and aliquots.'
}

Table sample.storage_event {
  storage_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each storage state change or custody transfer event record in the chain-of-custody audit trail. Primary key of the storage_event product.']
  aliquot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific aliquot (sub-divided portion of a specimen) involved in this storage event. Nullable when the event applies to the primary specimen rather than a derived aliquot. Populated from LabVantage LIMS aliquot tracking.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.asset. Business justification: Automated sample handling traceability: robotic liquid handlers and automated storage systems execute storage events in genomics labs. Linking storage_event to the instrument asset enables deviation r']
  container_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.container. Business justification: storage_event tracks custody and storage state changes for specimens and aliquots. The physical container is central to storage tracking — storage_event currently carries container_barcode (STRING) an']
  cryoprotectant_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: GMP biobanking traceability: cryopreservation storage events consume specific reagent lots (e.g., DMSO, glycerol). Regulatory and GMP chain-of-custody requirements mandate recording which cryoprotecta']
  destination_location_biobank_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location (freezer, rack, shelf, box, or position) to which the specimen or aliquot was moved or placed. Null for disposal or external transfer events where no internal destination exists.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Storage events (temperature excursions, unauthorized access, location errors) trigger deviations. Labs document which storage event caused each deviation for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Storage events with temperature excursions, unauthorized transfers, or chain-of-custody breaks trigger nonconformance records. Genomics biobanks require direct LIMS-to-QMS linkage from the storage eve']
  primary_storage_biobank_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location (freezer, rack, shelf, box, or position) from which the specimen or aliquot was retrieved or transferred. Null for initial placement events where no prior location exists.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary biological specimen (parent sample) involved in this storage event. Links to the specimen master record in LabVantage LIMS for full sample metadata and IRB consent context.']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the electronic acknowledgment or e-signature was applied to this storage event record. Distinct from the event_timestamp (when the physical action occurred). Required for FDA 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail completeness.']
  chain_of_custody_sequence varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing integer representing the ordinal position of this storage event within the complete chain-of-custody history for the specimen or aliquot. Enables ordered reconstruction of the full custody timeline for forensic genomics, legal, and regulatory audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this storage event record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Represents the record audit creation time, distinct from the event_timestamp which captures when the physical action occurred.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage event involved a deviation from the applicable SOP or standard storage protocol. True triggers a deviation investigation workflow in the quality management system (Veeva Vault or MasterControl) and requires documented justification.']
  electronic_acknowledgment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the storage event has received a valid electronic acknowledgment (e-signature) from the responsible operator or custodian in compliance with FDA 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements. True confirms the event is electronically signed and locked.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier for this storage event, formatted as SE-{YYYY}-{YYYYMMDD}-{sequence}. Used in audit reports, chain-of-custody documentation, and regulatory submissions to uniquely reference a specific custody transfer or placement event.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the storage event record. Indicates whether the event has been completed, is pending electronic acknowledgment, was cancelled, encountered a failure condition, or is under supervisory review for compliance purposes.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise real-world date and time at which the storage state change or custody transfer physically occurred, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. This is the principal business event time, distinct from the record audit timestamps. Critical for chain-of-custody sequencing and GxP compliance.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Categorical discriminator identifying the nature of the storage state change. Drives chain-of-custody logic and audit trail classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial_placement|relocation|retrieval|return|inter_section_transfer|external_transfer|disposal|quarantine_placement — promote to reference product]']
  external_site_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external facility, partner laboratory, or clinical site to which the specimen or aliquot was transferred during an inter-site or external transfer event. Populated only for event_type = external_transfer. Supports chain-of-custody documentation for multi-site genomics studies.']
  external_transfer_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Shipping or courier tracking number associated with an external specimen transfer event. Enables end-to-end shipment traceability for specimens transferred to external sites, partner labs, or clinical collaborators. Populated only for event_type = external_transfer.']
  freeze_thaw_cycle_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of freeze-thaw cycles the specimen or aliquot has undergone up to and including this storage event. Excessive freeze-thaw cycles degrade nucleic acid and protein integrity; this count is critical for sample quality assessment in genomic and clinical workflows.']
  is_immutable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage event record has been locked and made immutable following electronic acknowledgment and audit finalization. Immutable records cannot be modified or deleted, satisfying FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and GxP requirements for tamper-evident audit trails.']
  lims_event_reference varchar [note: 'The native event or transaction identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS for this storage event. Enables bidirectional traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer data product and the operational LIMS system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  phi_deidentified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specimen or aliquot involved in this storage event has been de-identified in accordance with HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination methods. True means PHI has been removed or anonymized; False means the specimen retains linkage to identifiable patient data.']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specimen or aliquot was placed into or removed from quarantine status as part of this storage event. Quarantined samples are restricted from use in assays or downstream workflows pending investigation or quality review.']
  quarantine_reason varchar [note: 'Standardized reason for placing the specimen or aliquot into quarantine. Populated only when quarantine_flag is True. Drives investigation workflows and determines the path to quarantine release or disposal.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining the business justification for the storage event. Used for operational reporting, compliance audits, and workflow analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: routine_storage|retrieval_for_assay|transfer_to_external_site|quarantine|inter_section_transfer|disposal_scheduled|emergency_retrieval — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context or justification for the storage event beyond the standardized reason code. Captures operator notes, special circumstances, or deviations from standard operating procedures (SOPs) that require documentation.']
  required_temp_max_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The maximum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the specimen or aliquot type involved in this event, as defined by the applicable SOP or specimen handling protocol. Used in conjunction with required_temp_min_c to define the acceptable temperature window.']
  required_temp_min_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The minimum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the specimen or aliquot type involved in this event, as defined by the applicable SOP or specimen handling protocol. Used to evaluate whether a temperature excursion occurred.']
  sop_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing the storage event type performed. Links to the controlled document in MasterControl or Veeva Vault for audit trail completeness and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this storage event was originated or ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between the lakehouse and upstream operational systems.']
  specimen_volume_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volume of the specimen or aliquot in microliters (µL) at the time of this storage event. Captured to track volume consumption across retrieval events and to flag low-volume specimens that may be insufficient for planned assays.']
  storage_condition_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the required storage condition category for the specimen or aliquot at the destination location. Drives storage unit assignment logic and temperature monitoring alert thresholds in the LIMS and facility management systems.']
  temperature_at_event_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recorded ambient or storage unit temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of the storage event. Captured from connected monitoring systems or manually entered by the operator. Used to assess sample integrity and document temperature excursion conditions.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion (deviation from the required storage temperature range) was detected or reported in association with this storage event. True signals a potential sample integrity risk requiring investigation and documentation per GxP and CLIA requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this storage event record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Tracks the last update for audit trail completeness and data lineage purposes. Immutable records will not have an updated_timestamp after locking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every storage state change and custody transfer for a specimen or aliquot — initial placement, relocation, retrieval, return, and inter-section transfers. Captures event type, source location, destination location, event timestamp, operator ID, custodian identity, reason code (routine storage, retrieval for assay, transfer to external site, quarantine), temperature excursion flag, and electronic acknowledgment. Provides the full chain-of-custody audit trail required for GxP, CLIA, and forensic genomics compliance. Serves as the immutable custody log for all in-facility specimen movements.'
}

Table sample.sample_consent_record {
  sample_consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the informed consent record within the Genomics Biotech data platform. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — this entity represents a binding ethical and regulatory agreement governing specimen use.']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Consent form version changes (new consent form version, updated permitted uses) require formal change controls under 21 CFR Part 50 and GDPR. The consent record must reference the change control gover']
  cohort_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.cohort. Business justification: Consent records in genomics research are typically obtained in the context of a specific cohort or study. sample_consent_record has research_protocol_id (cross-domain) but lacks a direct in-domain lin']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the consent form document as managed in Veeva Vault regulatory document management system. Enables traceability to the exact document version presented to the subject.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Consent deviations (expired consent used, missing re-consent for new data use, consent obtained outside protocol) are formal regulatory deviations in genomics research. Linking consent records to devi']
  irb_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.irb_submission. Business justification: Consent records must reference the IRB submission that authorized the research — a direct regulatory requirement (FDA 21 CFR Part 50, ICH E6 GCP). The IRB submission defines the approved consent form ']
  research_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical or research study protocol under which the specimen was collected and consent was obtained. Links to the governing study protocol document in Veeva Vault.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological specimen governed by this consent record. Links the consent agreement to the physical sample in the LabVantage LIMS sample tracking system.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.study. Business justification: Consent records are study-specific — consent form version, IRB protocol, and permitted uses are defined at the study level. Study-level consent tracking is required for regulatory compliance, data gov']
  subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the de-identified research subject or patient from whom consent was obtained. Links to the subject/participant master record in the sample domain.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Informed consent documentation is required evidence in regulatory submissions for clinical validation studies, IRB compliance verification, and post-market surveillance plans. Essential for FDA/notifi']
  assent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assent was obtained from a minor subject in addition to parental/guardian consent. Applicable for pediatric genomics studies. Required for IRB compliance in pediatric research protocols.']
  biobank_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to long-term biobanking and storage of their specimen for future unspecified research use. Governs specimen retention policies in LabVantage LIMS.']
  consent_collection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which informed consent was obtained and documented. \'paper\' — wet ink signature; \'electronic\' — eConsent platform; \'verbal_with_waiver\' — IRB-approved verbal consent with waiver of written documentation; \'remote_electronic\' — remote eConsent; \'delegated\' — consent delegated to authorized representative.']
  consent_collection_site varchar [note: 'Name or code of the clinical or research site where informed consent was obtained (e.g., hospital, clinic, research center). Supports multi-site study management and site-level compliance reporting.']
  consent_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the research subject or patient provided signed informed consent. This is the principal business event date for the consent agreement and is required for regulatory compliance timelines.']
  consent_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the consent authorization expires and must be renewed or re-consented. Null for open-ended broad consents. Used to trigger re-consent workflows and enforce data use restrictions.']
  consent_form_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the IRB-approved informed consent form presented to and signed by the subject (e.g., v1.0, v2.1). Critical for regulatory audit trails to confirm the subject received the current approved version.']
  consent_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for documenting special circumstances, subject questions, deviations from standard consent process, or other relevant observations recorded during the consent encounter. Confidential — not for general access.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the consent record. \'active\' — consent is valid and in force; \'withdrawn\' — subject has revoked consent; \'expired\' — consent period has lapsed without renewal; \'pending\' — awaiting subject signature or IRB approval; \'superseded\' — replaced by a newer consent version; \'revoked\' — administratively invalidated.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consent model applied. \'broad\' permits use across multiple future research purposes; \'specific\' restricts use to a defined study; \'tiered\' allows subject to select permitted uses; \'re_consent\' indicates updated consent obtained for changed scope; \'emergency_waiver\' denotes IRB-approved waiver; \'assent\' applies to minors. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broad|specific|tiered|re_consent|emergency_waiver|assent — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements under FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and GxP.']
  deidentification_status varchar [note: 'Current PHI de-identification status of the specimen and associated data. \'identified\' — PHI present; \'de_identified\' — HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination method applied; \'limited_dataset\' — HIPAA limited dataset with DUA; \'pseudonymized\' — GDPR pseudonymization applied; \'anonymized\' — irreversibly anonymized.']
  future_contact_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to being contacted for future research studies or re-consent requests. Governs subject outreach activities in Salesforce CRM.']
  gdpr_lawful_basis varchar [note: 'The GDPR Article 6/9 lawful basis under which personal and special-category genomic data is processed for subjects in EU/EEA jurisdictions. \'not_applicable\' for non-EU subjects. Required for GDPR compliance documentation.']
  incidental_findings_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to disclosure of incidental or secondary genomic findings identified during sequencing or analysis (e.g., ACMG secondary findings). Distinct from primary study results.']
  interpreter_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a language interpreter was used during the consent process. When true, additional interpreter documentation is required per GCP guidelines.']
  irb_protocol_number varchar [note: 'The unique protocol number assigned by the Institutional Review Board (IRB) governing the study or research program under which this consent was obtained. Required for regulatory traceability and audit.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code of the consent form presented to the subject (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'fr\', \'zh-CN\'). Ensures the subject received consent documentation in their primary language as required by FDA and ICH guidelines.']
  legally_authorized_representative boolean [note: 'Indicates whether consent was obtained from a Legally Authorized Representative (LAR) rather than the subject directly (e.g., for incapacitated adults or minors). Triggers additional documentation requirements.']
  lims_consent_record_code varchar [note: 'The native consent record identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS. Enables bidirectional traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer data product and the operational LIMS system of record.']
  permitted_use_clinical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has authorized use of their specimen for clinical diagnostic purposes, including In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) assay validation and Laboratory Developed Test (LDT) workflows under CLIA/CAP.']
  permitted_use_commercial boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has authorized use of their specimen and genomic data for commercial product development, including reagent validation, instrument development, and product commercialization activities.']
  permitted_use_data_sharing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has authorized sharing of their de-identified genomic data with external parties, including public genomic databases (e.g., dbGaP, GEO), research consortia, or partner institutions under the NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy.']
  permitted_use_research boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has authorized use of their specimen and associated genomic data for internal Research and Development (R&D) purposes. Governs bioinformatics pipeline processing and variant analysis workflows.']
  phi_authorization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has provided explicit HIPAA authorization for use and disclosure of their Protected Health Information (PHI) in conjunction with specimen and genomic data processing.']
  re_consent_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which re-consent must be obtained from the subject. Null if re-consent is not required. Used to manage compliance timelines and subject outreach scheduling.']
  re_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a re-consent is required from the subject due to protocol amendments, expanded data use scope, or consent expiry. Triggers re-consent workflow in LabVantage LIMS and MasterControl.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory jurisdiction applicable to this consent record, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes. Determines which regulatory framework (FDA, EMA, IVDR, etc.) governs consent requirements and data handling obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|EU|GBR|CAN|AUS|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA|OTHER — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  return_of_results_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to receive individual genomic research results or incidental findings. Governs clinical reporting workflows and genetic counseling referral processes.']
  sponsor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sponsoring organization or institution for the study under which consent was obtained. May be Genomics Biotech itself or an external sponsor for collaborative studies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Required for change tracking and audit trail compliance under FDA 21 CFR Part 11.']
  veeva_document_code varchar [note: 'The document identifier in Veeva Vault for the executed consent form document. Provides a direct link to the regulatory document management system for audit and inspection readiness.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'The date on which the subject formally withdrew consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. When populated, triggers downstream data quarantine and use-restriction enforcement per GDPR right-to-erasure and HIPAA requirements.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for consent withdrawal. Required for regulatory reporting and adverse event tracking. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: subject_request|adverse_event|protocol_deviation|lost_to_followup|deceased|administrative|other — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Authoritative record of IRB-approved informed consent obtained from a research subject or patient for specimen collection and use. Captures consent form version, IRB protocol number, consent date, consent type (broad, specific, re-consent), scope of permitted use (research, clinical, commercial, data sharing), withdrawal date if applicable, and PHI handling authorization. Links specimen to the governing ethical and regulatory framework. Supports HIPAA, GDPR, and NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance.'
}

Table sample.subject {
  subject_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the biological source individual (research participant, patient, donor, or animal model) within the Genomics Biotech data platform. Primary key for the subject master record. Role: MASTER_PARTY.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Research subjects enrolled through customer accounts (clinical sites, research institutions). Required for multi-site study management, institutional consent tracking, data access control, and site-sp']
  cohort_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking the enrollment record to the target cohort.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: In genomics biobank and clinical research operations, each enrolled subject has a designated customer contact (e.g., clinical coordinator or PI) responsible for re-consent workflows, return-of-results']
  irb_submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IRB or ethics committee protocol under which this subject was enrolled. Links the subject to the governing research protocol, consent framework, and permissible data use scope. Required for regulatory submissions, audit trails, and NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance.']
  lab_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.lab_site. Business justification: subject has a collection_site_code (STRING) that identifies the site where the subject was enrolled/collected. lab_site is the authoritative master for sites within this domain. Adding lab_site_id FK ']
  patient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.patient. Business justification: In precision medicine genomics, a research subject and a clinical patient represent the same individual. This link enables return-of-results workflows, incidental findings reporting obligations, longi']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.risk_assessment. Business justification: Subject-level re-identification risk assessments are a formal regulatory requirement under GDPR Article 35 and genomic data governance frameworks. The plain attribute reidentification_risk_level shoul']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.study. Business justification: Subjects are enrolled in studies — this is the fundamental enrollment relationship in clinical genomics research. Subject-to-study traceability is required for participant management, adverse event re']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: In IVD clinical studies and genomics trials, subjects are enrolled under a specific regulatory submission (IDE, IND). This is distinct from IRB submission — it tracks the device/drug regulatory filing']
  age_at_enrollment varchar [note: 'Age of the subject in whole years at the time of study enrollment, derived from year_of_birth and enrollment_date at data ingestion. Stored as a pre-computed integer to support cohort age-stratification queries without requiring DOB. Enables pediatric vs. adult cohort segmentation and age-related genomic analyses.']
  ancestry_group varchar [note: 'Self-reported or genetically inferred ancestry/ethnicity group of the subject, used for population genomics stratification, Minor Allele Frequency (MAF) reference panel selection, and pharmacogenomics analyses. Stored as a controlled vocabulary term aligned with gnomAD and 1000 Genomes Project population labels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AFR|AMR|EAS|EUR|MID|SAS|ASH|LAT|OTH — promote to reference product]']
  biobank_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to long-term biobanking of residual specimens for future research use beyond the primary study. Governs specimen retention policies, storage duration limits, and permissible secondary use of aliquots in LabVantage LIMS.']
  biological_sex varchar [note: 'Biological sex of the subject as recorded at enrollment, distinct from gender identity. Used for sex-stratified genomic analyses, reference genome selection (e.g., chrX/chrY copy number), population genomics cohort stratification, and clinical assay interpretation.']
  collection_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the subject\'s specimens were collected. Drives GDPR data residency requirements, export control compliance for genomic data, and population genomics geographic stratification.']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the informed consent document that was in effect and signed by the subject at the time of enrollment into this cohort. Required for IRB compliance and consent audit trails.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subject record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under GxP, HIPAA, and GDPR, and enables data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  data_access_tier varchar [note: 'Permissible data access level for genomic data derived from this subject, aligned with NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy tiers. Open access data is publicly available; registered access requires user registration; controlled access requires dbGaP or equivalent approval; restricted access is limited to named investigators under a data use agreement (DUA).']
  deidentification_method varchar [note: 'The HIPAA-recognized or GDPR-compliant method applied to de-identify or pseudonymize the subject record. HIPAA Safe Harbor removes 18 specified identifiers; Expert Determination uses statistical analysis to certify re-identification risk is very small. Pseudonymization replaces direct identifiers with a code while retaining a linkage key in a separate access-controlled system. Drives data access tier assignment and downstream PHI handling rules.']
  deidentification_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the subject record\'s de-identification level per HIPAA and GDPR frameworks. Fully de-identified records have no re-identification pathway; pseudonymized records retain a coded linkage to PHI managed separately; limited dataset records retain certain geographic and date information under a data use agreement (DUA). Governs permissible downstream data uses and sharing.']
  disease_indication varchar [note: 'Primary disease indication or phenotype associated with the subject, encoded using a standardized ontology term (e.g., ICD-10, OMIM, HPO). Drives cohort assembly for clinical genomics studies, variant interpretation in clinical assay workflows, and regulatory submission stratification. Free-text or ontology code depending on study protocol.']
  disease_indication_ontology varchar [note: 'Ontology or coding system used to encode the disease_indication field (e.g., ICD-10, OMIM, HPO, SNOMED-CT). Enables interoperability across clinical, research, and regulatory systems and ensures consistent variant interpretation and cohort queries.']
  family_relationship varchar [note: 'Role of this subject within the family pedigree identified by family_id. Used for trio-based variant calling, de novo mutation detection, and inheritance pattern analysis in WGS/WES (Whole Exome Sequencing) workflows. Null for unrelated subjects.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR applies to this subject\'s data, based on the subject\'s collection country and data processing location. When True, GDPR Article 9 special category data protections apply to genetic data, and data subject rights (access, erasure, portability) must be honored. Drives EU data residency and transfer compliance workflows.']
  genomic_data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has provided explicit consent for their genomic data to be shared with external repositories (e.g., dbGaP, EGA, UK Biobank). Required for NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance and international data sharing agreements. When False, data must not be submitted to public or controlled-access repositories.']
  hipaa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether HIPAA Privacy Rule applies to this subject\'s data, based on whether the subject is a US patient or the data is held by a HIPAA-covered entity or business associate. When True, PHI handling, minimum necessary standard, and de-identification requirements apply.']
  incidental_findings_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to receive incidental or secondary genomic findings (e.g., ACMG SF v3.2 actionable secondary findings) identified during WGS/WES analysis. Drives the return-of-results workflow and clinical reporting obligations for pathogenic variants in medically actionable genes.']
  lims_subject_code varchar [note: 'Native subject identifier assigned by LabVantage LIMS at the time of accessioning. Used for cross-system traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer data product and the operational LIMS workflow. Enables reconciliation and lineage tracking back to the system of record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for study coordinator or clinical staff annotations regarding the subject record, such as protocol deviations, special handling instructions, or enrollment exceptions. Must not contain direct PHI. Reviewed during data quality audits and IRB annual reviews.']
  phi_linkage_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a PHI linkage record exists for this subject in the access-controlled consent and identity management system. When True, the subject_code can be re-linked to direct PHI by authorized personnel only. When False, the record is fully de-identified with no re-linkage pathway. Supports HIPAA Safe Harbor compliance auditing.']
  primary_assay_type varchar [note: 'The primary genomic assay type intended or performed for this subject\'s specimens. Drives bioinformatics pipeline selection, QC (Quality Control) metric thresholds, and data product routing. WGS (Whole Genome Sequencing), WES (Whole Exome Sequencing), targeted panel, SNP (Single Nucleotide Polymorphism) array, RNA-seq, and methylation array are the principal assay categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|SNP_array|RNA_seq|methylation_array|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recontact_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has consented to being re-contacted for additional studies, incidental findings disclosure, or updated consent. Relevant for longitudinal genomics studies and return-of-results workflows. When False, no re-contact is permitted without a new consent process.']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number for the subject record, incremented on each update. Supports optimistic concurrency control, SCD Type 2 history tracking, and GxP audit trail requirements for regulated genomics data. Version 1 represents the initial record creation.']
  reference_genome_build varchar [note: 'The reference genome assembly version used as the default for bioinformatics pipeline alignment and variant calling for this subject\'s specimens (e.g., GRCh38 for human, GRCm39 for mouse). Ensures consistency between sample metadata and downstream BAM/VCF (Variant Call Format) outputs. Critical for multi-cohort studies where genome build consistency is required.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this subject record was originated or last updated. Enables data lineage traceability and reconciliation between the Databricks Silver Layer and upstream operational systems (primarily LabVantage LIMS for sample domain records).']
  species varchar [note: 'Biological species of the subject. Determines applicable reference genome, annotation pipeline, and regulatory classification (human vs. animal model). Critical for bioinformatics pipeline routing and IRB/IACUC compliance.']
  subject_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, de-identified alphanumeric code assigned to the subject at accessioning or enrollment. Used as the operational identifier across LIMS, bioinformatics pipelines, and clinical reports without exposing direct PHI. Equivalent to the participant ID or pseudonym in study documentation.']
  subject_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the subject was formally enrolled into the cohort. Required for IRB compliance and cohort timeline reporting.']
  subject_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subject\'s enrollment in the cohort. Tracks whether the subject is actively participating, has withdrawn, or is in a pending/suspended state.']
  subject_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the biological source individual by their role in the study or clinical workflow. Drives consent requirements, regulatory handling, and data access controls. Research participants and patients require IRB/ethics board oversight; animal models require IACUC approval.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subject record in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental pipeline processing, and GxP audit trail compliance.']
  use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory use classification for data and specimens derived from this subject. RUO (Research Use Only) specimens are not intended for clinical diagnosis; IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) specimens are used in validated clinical assays; LDT (Laboratory Developed Test) specimens are used in laboratory-developed clinical tests. Drives regulatory handling, labeling, and data access controls.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject formally withdrew consent or was removed from the study. Triggers downstream data use restriction workflows per HIPAA and GDPR right-to-erasure requirements. Populated only when subject_status is \'withdrawn\'.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for subject withdrawal from the study or clinical program, recorded when subject_status transitions to \'withdrawn\'. Supports regulatory audit trails, protocol deviation reporting, and IRB annual review documentation. Populated only when applicable.']
  year_of_birth varchar [note: 'Four-digit birth year of the subject. Stored instead of full date of birth (DOB) to comply with HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification requirements (45 CFR §164.514(b)(2)(i)), which prohibit retention of specific birth dates. Enables age-range calculations and cohort age-stratification for population genomics without re-identification risk.']

  Note: 'De-identified or pseudonymized master record of the biological source individual (research participant, patient, donor, or animal model) from whom specimens are collected.
