Stores subject code, species, biological sex, year of birth (not full DOB for de-identification), ethnicity/ancestry group (for population genomics), disease indication, de-identification method applied (HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination), de-identification tier (fully de-identified, pseudonymized, limited dataset), and re-identification risk level. Does NOT store direct PHI — PHI linkage is managed via consent_record and access-controlled separately per HIPAA Safe Harbor. Supports cohort membership tracking via external study enrollment references.'
}

Table sample.collection_event {
  collection_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the specimen collection event record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Collection events occur at customer sites. Essential for site-specific protocol compliance monitoring, collection kit logistics coordination, site performance metrics, and regulatory audit trails. Rea']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Collection kit batch traceability is a GMP requirement — knowing which supply batch of collection kit material was used at each collection event enables batch recalls and deviation investigations. col']
  collection_kit_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Collection events use specific collection kit products (PAXgene, Tempus, saliva kits). Critical for tracking kit performance across collection sites, supplier quality monitoring, and correlating pre-a']
  collection_kit_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Collection events use specific collection kit products. Currently denormalized as collection_kit_sku. Business need: kit lot traceability for regulatory compliance, collection protocol compliance veri']
  collection_kit_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: GMP/GLP collection kit lot traceability: collection_kit_lot_number is a plain text denormalization. Regulatory requirements (CAP, CLIA, ISO 15189) mandate linking collection events to specific reagent']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) document in MasterControl that governed this collection event. Ensures traceability to the approved procedure version used, supporting GxP compliance and audit readiness.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Collection events with protocol deviations (wrong tube, collection outside time window, fasting protocol violated) require formal deviation records. The plain attributes deviation_flag/description/typ']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.header. Business justification: Collection events are initiated by service orders in genomics biotech; linking to order.header enables TAT reporting, billing reconciliation, and order-to-collection traceability. collection_order_num']
  lab_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.lab_site. Business justification: collection_event has a collection_site_code column typed as BIGINT — this is semantically a reference to a lab_site rather than a free-text code. lab_site is the authoritative master for laboratory an']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Collection kits are materials with SKUs and lot numbers. Linking enables kit inventory management, automated consumption tracking, lot traceability for specimen provenance, and recall management. Remo']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Collection nonconformances (hemolysis at collection, insufficient volume collected, wrong anatomical site) trigger NCRs distinct from deviations. Genomics labs require direct linkage from collection e']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the research study, clinical trial, or diagnostic project under which this collection event was performed. Enables cohort-level aggregation and study-specific pre-analytical QC reporting.']
  research_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) approved protocol under which this collection event was conducted. Mandatory for research specimens; ensures regulatory traceability of consent and ethical approval.']
  sample_consent_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the informed consent record obtained from the subject prior to collection. Links to the IRB consent management entity. Mandatory for research and clinical genomics specimens; supports HIPAA, GDPR, and NIH Genomic Data Sharing Policy compliance.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary biological sample record created as a result of this collection event. Links to the sample master entity in the Sample Management domain for downstream LIMS workflow assignments, aliquoting, and storage tracking.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.study. Business justification: Collection events occur within studies — study-level collection tracking is required for enrollment monitoring, protocol deviation reporting, and collection timeline analysis. collection_event links t']
  subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the human subject, patient, or research participant from whom the specimen was collected. Links to the subject/patient master record. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category. Classified restricted/pii_identifier as it directly identifies a research or clinical participant.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: In clinical trial genomics, collection events are performed under a regulatory submission (IND, IDE, PMA clinical study). collection_event.irb_protocol_number signals regulated context. Linking to sub']
  test_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_order. Business justification: A specimen collection event is directly triggered by a clinical test order. This link enables collection-to-order traceability for TAT calculation, recollection workflows, and regulatory audit trails.']
  training_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.training_record. Business justification: Training records document that collectors are qualified to perform collections. Labs link training to collection events for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  anatomical_site varchar [note: 'Standardized anatomical location from which the specimen was collected (e.g., left antecubital vein, right breast core, nasopharynx). Encoded using SNOMED CT or ICD-10 topography codes where applicable. Critical for oncology and clinical genomics interpretation.']
  collection_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country where the specimen was collected. Required for international regulatory compliance (GDPR, IVDR), cross-border data transfer governance, and geographic cohort stratification.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Calendar date (yyyy-MM-dd) on which the specimen was collected. Stored separately from collection_timestamp to support date-level partitioning, reporting, and cases where only the date (not exact time) is known or required.']
  collection_event_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the collection event, assigned by LabVantage LIMS at accessioning. Used for chain-of-custody documentation, deviation reports, and cross-system traceability. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'Standardized method used to obtain the biological specimen. Drives pre-analytical QC interpretation and NGS library preparation protocol selection. Values: venipuncture (blood draw), biopsy (tissue), swab (mucosal/surface), saliva_kit (self-collected saliva), liquid_biopsy (cell-free DNA from blood), fine_needle_aspiration (FNA cytology).']
  collection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional collector observations, special circumstances, or contextual notes recorded at the time of collection that do not fit into structured fields. Supports clinical interpretation and deviation investigation.']
  collection_sop_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the SOP document used at the time of collection (e.g., v1.2, v3.0). Stored alongside the SOP ID to capture the exact approved version in effect, as SOP versions change over time.']
  collection_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient or transport temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of or immediately following specimen collection. Used to assess cold-chain compliance and pre-analytical stability. Critical for cfDNA liquid biopsy and RNA specimens.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the physical specimen was collected from the subject or environment. This is the principal real-world event time and is critical for pre-analytical time calculations (e.g., time-to-processing, cold-chain compliance). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  collection_tube_count varchar [note: 'Number of collection tubes or containers used during the collection event. Relevant for blood draw events with multiple tube types (EDTA, SST, Streck cfDNA). Supports aliquot planning and chain-of-custody reconciliation.']
  collection_urgency varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the collection event that governs Turnaround Time (TAT) expectations and workflow prioritization in LabVantage LIMS. \'stat\' = immediate priority; \'urgent\' = same-day; \'routine\' = standard TAT.']
  collection_volume_ml decimal(8,3) [note: 'Volume of specimen collected, expressed in millilitres (mL). Used for pre-analytical QC assessment (e.g., short draw detection), yield estimation, and aliquot planning. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category (non-monetary transaction).']
  collection_volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the collection volume field. Typically mL for liquid specimens; mg or g for solid tissue specimens; uL for micro-volume collections.']
  collector_certification_number varchar [note: 'Professional certification or license number of the individual who performed the collection (e.g., phlebotomy certification, nursing license). Required for CLIA and CAP compliance documentation and chain-of-custody integrity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the collection event record was first created in LabVantage LIMS. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA tracking. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the collection event workflow. \'scheduled\' = collection planned but not yet performed; \'in_progress\' = collector has initiated; \'completed\' = specimen successfully collected and handed off; \'cancelled\' = event voided before collection; \'deviated\' = completed with documented pre-analytical deviation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  fasting_status varchar [note: 'Pre-analytical fasting status of the subject at the time of collection. Relevant for metabolomics, lipid panels, and certain genomic assays where fasting state affects analyte stability and downstream data quality interpretation.']
  hemolysis_index varchar [note: 'Semi-quantitative assessment of hemolysis in the collected blood specimen, graded on a standardized scale (none, 1+ to 4+). Hemolysis is a key pre-analytical variable that degrades DNA/RNA quality and affects NGS library preparation success rates.']
  lims_accession_number varchar [note: 'Accession number assigned by LabVantage LIMS at the time of specimen receipt and registration. Serves as the primary LIMS-side identifier for chain-of-custody tracking and workflow assignment.']
  phi_deidentified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Protected Health Information (PHI) associated with this collection event has been de-identified per HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination method. True = de-identified; False = identifiable PHI present. Governs data access controls and downstream sharing permissions.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the specimen was received at the processing laboratory. Used to calculate time-in-transit and total pre-analytical time (collection to receipt). Critical for TAT reporting and cold-chain compliance verification.']
  scheduled_collection_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for the collection event as defined in the study protocol or clinical order. Compared against collection_date to calculate collection compliance and protocol adherence metrics.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Biological specimen type collected. Determines downstream processing protocols, storage conditions, and applicable NGS assay types (e.g., WGS, WES, liquid biopsy ctDNA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|buffy_coat|saliva|tissue_ffpe|tissue_fresh_frozen|buccal_swab|urine|csf|stool|bone_marrow|amniotic_fluid — promote to reference product]']
  time_to_processing_minutes varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in minutes between specimen collection timestamp and the start of primary processing (e.g., centrifugation, nucleic acid extraction). A critical pre-analytical variable for NGS data quality; exceeding thresholds may trigger QC flags or deviation records.']
  transport_condition varchar [note: 'Required transport temperature condition for the collected specimen from collection site to processing laboratory. Drives cold-chain logistics and is compared against actual temperature readings for pre-analytical QC.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the collection event record in LabVantage LIMS. Supports audit trail requirements and Silver Layer incremental load detection. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory use classification of the specimen collected. RUO = Research Use Only; IVD = In Vitro Diagnostic; LDT = Laboratory Developed Test; clinical_trial = collected under an approved clinical trial protocol; quality_control = internal QC specimen. Determines applicable regulatory framework (FDA, CLIA, CAP) and data handling requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of the physical specimen collection activity. Captures collection date/time, collection site (anatomical location or environmental site), collection method (venipuncture, biopsy, swab, saliva kit, liquid biopsy), collector ID, collection kit lot number, pre-analytical conditions (fasting status, time from collection to processing), and any collection deviations. Provides the pre-analytical metadata critical for NGS data quality interpretation.'
}

Table sample.extraction {
  extraction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the nucleic acid extraction record. Primary key.']
  aliquot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.aliquot. Business justification: In genomics workflows, nucleic acid extractions are frequently performed on aliquots (physical sub-divisions of a specimen) rather than the original specimen directly. extraction already has sample_sp']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Nucleic acid extraction in GxP/IVD genomics labs must be performed under a validated, regulatory-approved method. The extraction record must reference the approval governing the extraction kit/method ']
  assay_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.assay_version. Business justification: Nucleic acid extraction protocols are specified per assay version in genomics labs. Linking extraction to assay_version enables protocol compliance verification, version-specific yield/purity trending']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the automated extraction instrument used (e.g., QIAcube, KingFisher Flex). Null if manual extraction.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: GMP extraction processes require traceability of the specific supply batch of extraction kit material used. Enables batch recall impact assessment and deviation investigations. Complements existing ma']
  change_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.change_control. Business justification: Change controls may affect extraction methods (e.g., new kits, protocol updates). Labs link change controls to affected extractions for traceability and regulatory compliance.']
  clinical_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.clinical_specimen. Business justification: A nucleic acid extraction is performed on a specific clinical specimen. This link enables end-to-end clinical traceability from specimen receipt through extraction to genomic result, required for CLIA']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Extraction failures (low yield, contamination, protocol deviations) are common quality events. Labs link extractions to deviations for process improvement and regulatory documentation.']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: Extractions execute experimental protocols. Linking extraction to experiment enables protocol compliance verification, data lineage for publications, assay validation documentation, and regulatory sub']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Extractions are performed as part of customer service orders (sequencing, genotyping). Links lab processing steps to commercial transactions for billing, quality tracking, and regulatory documentation']
  kit_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Extraction uses specific kit products. Currently denormalized as extraction_kit_name. Business need: kit lot traceability for regulatory audits, cost-per-extraction calculation for pricing models, inv']
  kit_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Extraction uses specific reagent kit lots. Essential for correlating extraction yield/quality with reagent lot performance, investigating out-of-specification results, and maintaining GxP traceability']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Extraction kits are materials with material numbers in ERP/LIMS systems. Linking enables inventory consumption tracking, automated reordering, kit lot traceability for regulatory submissions, and CoA ']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Extraction failures (contamination_flag=true, yield below spec, RIN score failure) trigger nonconformance records. Genomics labs require direct linkage from the extraction record to the NCR for batch ']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Extracted nucleic acid is a direct input to manufacturing production batches. GMP batch records must document which extraction was used. Traceability from extraction to production batch is required fo']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.storage_location. Business justification: Chain-of-custody for extracted nucleic acids: extraction.storage_location is a plain text denormalization of the storage location entity. Linking to reagent.storage_location.storage_location_id enable']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.study. Business justification: Extractions are performed to support specific studies (e.g., genomic DNA extraction for a clinical genomics study). Study-level extraction traceability is required for regulatory submissions, study cl']
  training_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.training_record. Business justification: Training records document that operators are qualified to perform extractions. Labs link training to extraction activities for audit trails and regulatory compliance.']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Extraction method validations use real extraction runs to demonstrate performance. Labs track which extractions were part of validation studies for regulatory submissions and method qualification.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_order. Business justification: Nucleic acid extraction is a controlled manufacturing operation executed via work orders in GMP/GLP environments. This link supports batch record documentation, process validation, deviation tracking,']
  a260_a230_ratio decimal(5,3) [note: 'Absorbance ratio at 260nm and 230nm wavelengths, indicating contamination by organic compounds or chaotropic salts. Expected range is 2.0-2.2 for pure nucleic acid.']
  a260_a280_ratio decimal(5,3) [note: 'Absorbance ratio at 260nm and 280nm wavelengths, indicating nucleic acid purity. Pure DNA typically has ratio ~1.8, pure RNA ~2.0. Lower values indicate protein contamination.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Unique barcode identifier assigned to the extracted nucleic acid aliquot for tracking and traceability.']
  concentration_ng_per_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Concentration of extracted nucleic acid measured in nanograms per microliter (ng/µL), typically measured by spectrophotometry or fluorometry.']
  contamination_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether contamination was detected during or after extraction (True = contamination detected, False = no contamination).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this extraction record was first created in the LIMS system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  dv200_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of RNA fragments greater than 200 nucleotides, used as quality metric for degraded RNA samples (e.g., FFPE). Values >30% are typically acceptable for NGS.']
  elution_volume_ul decimal(10,2) [note: 'Final elution volume of the extracted nucleic acid, measured in microliters (µL).']
  extraction_date date [note: 'Date when the nucleic acid extraction was performed, in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  extraction_method varchar [note: 'The nucleic acid extraction methodology applied to the specimen (e.g., column-based, magnetic bead, phenol-chloroform, manual, automated, salting-out).']
  extraction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the extraction process was completed, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  input_mass_mg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mass of input material used for extraction (applicable for solid tissue specimens), measured in milligrams (mg).']
  input_material_type varchar [note: 'Type of biological specimen used as input for extraction (e.g., whole blood, plasma, serum, tissue, saliva, buccal swab, urine, cerebrospinal fluid, FFPE, fresh frozen). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|tissue|saliva|buccal_swab|urine|csf|ffpe|fresh_frozen — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  input_volume_ul decimal(10,2) [note: 'Volume of input material used for extraction, measured in microliters (µL).']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'Intended downstream application for the extracted nucleic acid (e.g., NGS library preparation, qPCR, array, ddPCR, storage, research, clinical testing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ngs_library_prep|qpcr|array|ddpcr|storage|research|clinical_testing — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this extraction record was last modified in the LIMS system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator notes, observations, or deviations from standard protocol during the extraction process.']
  nucleic_acid_type varchar [note: 'Type of nucleic acid extracted: DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid), RNA (ribonucleic acid), cfDNA (cell-free DNA), gDNA (genomic DNA), total nucleic acid, or mRNA (messenger RNA).']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) or protocol used for extraction, ensuring traceability to documented procedures.']
  qc_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the extraction failed QC criteria (e.g., low yield, poor purity ratios, degradation). Null if QC passed.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Quality control determination for the extraction: pass (meets all QC criteria), fail (does not meet QC criteria), pending (QC not yet complete), conditional_pass (marginal quality, proceed with caution).']
  quantification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to quantify the extracted nucleic acid (e.g., NanoDrop spectrophotometry, Qubit fluorometry, Bioanalyzer, TapeStation, qPCR, PicoGreen).']
  rin_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'RNA Integrity Number score (1-10 scale) indicating RNA quality and degradation level. Applicable only for RNA extractions. Higher scores indicate better integrity.']
  run_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch or run in which this extraction was performed, linking multiple extractions processed together.']
  storage_temperature_c varchar [note: 'Storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the extracted nucleic acid (e.g., -20°C for DNA, -80°C for RNA).']
  total_yield_ng decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total yield of extracted nucleic acid in nanograms (ng), calculated as concentration multiplied by elution volume.']

  Note: 'Records the nucleic acid extraction process applied to a specimen or aliquot — producing DNA, RNA, cfDNA, gDNA, or total nucleic acid. Captures extraction method (column-based, magnetic bead, manual, automated), instrument used, extraction kit lot number, input material type and volume, elution volume, yield (ng/µL), purity ratios (A260/A280, A260/A230), extraction date, operator, and pass/fail QC determination. Critical pre-analytical step linking specimen to library preparation in the NGS workflow.'
}

Business justification: QC measurements are a primary output of the extraction process — purity ratios (A260/A280, A260/A230), RIN scores, and yield measurements are all performed on extracted nucleic acids. qc_measurement h']
  instrument_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch or run in which this measurement was performed. Groups multiple measurements performed together for batch-level QC and traceability.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: QC reagents and assay kits are materials. Linking enables reagent lot traceability, validation of QC results against reagent specifications, automated inventory depletion, and regulatory compliance (2']
  measurement_reagent_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: QC measurements (Qubit quantification, Bioanalyzer sizing, qPCR validation) use specific reagent/standard lots. Essential for assessing measurement validity, investigating out-of-specification results']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: A qc_measurement with pass_fail_flag=FAIL triggers a nonconformance record (e.g., reagent lot failure, instrument out-of-spec). Direct LIMS-to-QMS linkage from the QC measurement to the NCR is require']
  oos_investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.oos_investigation. Business justification: An out-of-specification QC measurement directly initiates an OOS investigation per 21 CFR Part 211 and ICH Q2. The qc_measurement record must reference the OOS investigation for Phase I/II investigati']
  original_measurement_qc_measurement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original QC measurement record if this is a repeat measurement. Enables traceability of repeat testing and comparison of results.']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: In-process QC measurements on samples (library QC, fragment analysis, quantification) are taken during manufacturing production batches. Linking qc_measurement to production_batch supports batch dispo']
  qc_reagent_catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: QC measurements use specific reagent products (Qubit assays, Bioanalyzer chips, TapeStation reagents). Business need: measurement validity verification, reagent lot correlation with QC failures, regul']
  qc_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.qc_specification. Business justification: QC release testing process: every qc_measurement is evaluated against a defined reagent qc_specification (acceptance criteria, limits, test method). Genomics QC analysts require this link to determine']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample or aliquot on which this QC measurement was performed. Links to the sample master record in LabVantage LIMS.']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: QC measurements (concentration, RIN, A260/A280) are assessed against product specification thresholds (reference_range_lower/upper). Regulatory traceability requires recording which specification vers']
  test_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.test_method. Business justification: Every QC measurement is performed using a specific validated test method (e.g., Qubit fluorometry, Bioanalyzer RIN, PicoGreen). Linking to test_method provides analytical traceability, method version ']
  test_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_order. Business justification: Pre-analytical and analytical QC measurements are performed as part of specific test order fulfillment. Linking qc_measurement to test_order enables order-level QC reporting, rejection/repeat workflow']
  a260_a230_ratio decimal(10,4) [note: 'Ratio of absorbance at 260 nm to 230 nm, used to detect contamination from carbohydrates, salts, or phenol. Expected range is 2.0-2.2 for pure nucleic acids.']
  a260_a280_ratio decimal(10,4) [note: 'Ratio of absorbance at 260 nm to 280 nm, used to assess nucleic acid purity. Pure DNA typically has a ratio of ~1.8, pure RNA ~2.0. Values outside this range indicate contamination.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the measurement was reviewed and approved by quality assurance personnel. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the instrument used for this measurement was last calibrated. Ensures measurement accuracy and compliance with quality standards. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  concentration_ng_ul decimal(18,6) [note: 'Nucleic acid concentration measured in nanograms per microliter. Commonly used for DNA and RNA quantification via Qubit or NanoDrop.']
  control_sample_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this measurement was performed on a control sample (positive control, negative control, or reference standard) rather than a patient or research specimen. True indicates control.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Type of control sample used in the measurement, if applicable. Defines the role of the control in the quality assurance process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this QC measurement record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  din_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'DNA Integrity Number (DIN) score ranging from 1 (highly degraded) to 10 (highly intact). Measured via TapeStation electrophoresis. Assesses genomic DNA fragmentation.']
  fragment_size_bp varchar [note: 'Average or modal fragment size of nucleic acid measured in base pairs. Determined via electrophoresis (Bioanalyzer, TapeStation, or Fragment Analyzer). Critical for library prep QC.']
  fragment_size_distribution varchar [note: 'Textual or encoded representation of the fragment size distribution profile. May include peak positions, distribution shape, or reference to an electropherogram file.']
  lims_record_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this measurement in the source Laboratory Information Management System (LabVantage LIMS). Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation.']
  lod_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the measured value is below the Limit of Detection (LOD) for the assay. True indicates below LOD. Critical for clinical and research interpretation.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Quantitative result of the QC measurement. The numeric value obtained from the instrument or assay. Precision supports high-resolution measurements.']
  measurement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by the operator regarding the measurement. May include observations about sample quality, instrument behavior, or deviations from SOP (Standard Operating Procedure).']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the QC measurement. Tracks the measurement from initiation through completion or failure.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the QC measurement was performed. Critical for traceability and TAT (Turnaround Time) tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this QC measurement record was last modified. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  pass_fail_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the measured value falls within the acceptable reference range. True indicates pass, False indicates fail. Drives pre-analytical QC gating decisions.']
  reference_range_lower decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower bound of the acceptable reference range for this measurement type. Used to determine pass/fail status and quality gating decisions.']
  reference_range_upper decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper bound of the acceptable reference range for this measurement type. Used to determine pass/fail status and quality gating decisions.']
  repeat_measurement_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this measurement is a repeat or retest of a previous measurement. True indicates repeat. Used for tracking measurement reproducibility and troubleshooting.']
  rin_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'RNA Integrity Number (RIN) score ranging from 1 (completely degraded) to 10 (fully intact). Measured via Bioanalyzer or TapeStation electrophoresis. Critical for RNA-seq quality.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Type of biological specimen or nucleic acid material on which the QC measurement was performed. Defines the material being assessed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|saliva|tissue|dna|rna|cdna|library|amplicon|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Storage temperature or condition of the specimen prior to QC measurement. Impacts nucleic acid stability and quality. Critical for pre-analytical quality assessment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the measured value. Standardized units ensure consistent interpretation across laboratories and workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ng_ul|ng|ug|pg|ng_ml|a260_a280_ratio|rin|din|bp|percent — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Captures quantitative and qualitative QC measurements performed on specimens, aliquots, or extracted nucleic acids at any stage of the sample lifecycle. Records measurement type (Qubit fluorometry, NanoDrop spectrophotometry, Bioanalyzer/TapeStation electrophoresis, DIN score, RIN score, fragment size distribution), instrument ID, reagent lot, measured value, unit, reference range, pass/fail flag, and measurement timestamp. Supports LOD tracking and pre-analytical QC gating decisions.'
}

  biobank_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.biobank_location. Business justification: Containers (tubes, vials, cryovials) are physically stored within biobank locations (freezers, racks, shelves). container currently references reagent.storage_location (cross-domain) but lacks an in-d']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: GMP container/consumable lot traceability: container.lot_number is a plain text denormalization of the reagent lot entity. In GMP environments, specimen containers (tubes, plates) are tracked as reage']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Containers (tubes, plates, vials) are consumable materials with material numbers. Linking enables consumable inventory tracking, automated procurement, cost allocation, and supplier management. Remove']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Container failures (cracked tube, label failure, contamination, wrong container type) trigger nonconformance records. Genomics labs require direct linkage from the container record to the NCR for spec']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample or aliquot currently held within this container. Links container to the sample domain for chain-of-custody traceability.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical storage location (freezer, rack, shelf, position) where this container is currently housed. Supports barcode-driven inventory management.']
  validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: Container types used for specimen storage must be validated (container compatibility studies, leachables/extractables, freeze-thaw stability). Genomics labs reference the validation study governing ea']
  accessioned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the container was first registered and accessioned into the LabVantage LIMS system. Marks the start of the container lifecycle and chain-of-custody record. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Unique machine-readable barcode label affixed to the physical container. Used for barcode-driven sample tracking and automated liquid handling workflows in LabVantage LIMS. Must be globally unique across all container inventory.']
  cap_color varchar [note: 'Color of the container cap or closure used for visual specimen type identification per laboratory SOP (e.g., red = serum, purple = EDTA whole blood, yellow = SST). Supports pre-analytical sorting and specimen type verification at accessioning.']
  chain_of_custody_status varchar [note: 'Current integrity status of the chain-of-custody record for this container. Intact = unbroken custody from collection to current location; Broken = a custody gap or anomaly has been detected; Under Review = custody discrepancy under investigation; Verified = custody independently confirmed.']
  concentration_ng_ul decimal(12,4) [note: 'Measured concentration of the nucleic acid or analyte within the container expressed in nanograms per microliter (ng/µL). Captured at accessioning or post-QC measurement. Used for library preparation input normalization and downstream NGS workflow eligibility.']
  container_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the container. Governs whether the container is available for use, actively holding a specimen, reserved for a workflow, or has been removed from inventory. Drives container inventory management and QC workflows.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Physical form factor classification of the container. Drives liquid handling automation compatibility, storage configuration, and workflow routing in LabVantage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tube|plate|vial|cryovial|microplate_well|strip_tube|cartridge — promote to reference product]']
  current_volume_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Current volume of specimen or reagent held within the container expressed in microliters (µL). Updated by LabVantage LIMS upon each aliquoting, dispensing, or consumption event. Enables remaining volume tracking for sample sufficiency checks.']
  discarded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the container was formally discarded or destroyed in LabVantage LIMS. Null if the container has not been discarded. Required for chain-of-custody closure, biohazardous waste disposal documentation, and regulatory audit trails.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of the container and its contents upon discard or destruction. Required for biohazardous waste compliance documentation and environmental health and safety (EHS) reporting.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Manufacturer-specified expiration date for the container (e.g., sterility expiry, additive expiry for collection tubes with anticoagulants or preservatives). After this date the container must not be used for specimen collection or storage per GMP and ISO 13485 requirements.']
  external_code varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by an external system, partner laboratory, or originating institution (e.g., hospital LIMS ID, biobank accession number). Enables cross-system reconciliation and chain-of-custody linkage.']
  freeze_thaw_count varchar [note: 'Number of freeze-thaw cycles the container and its contents have undergone. Tracked to assess specimen integrity degradation risk. Exceeding the maximum allowable freeze-thaw cycles per SOP may trigger QC review or specimen disqualification.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'Intended use classification of the container and its contents. Determines applicable regulatory framework: clinical specimens are subject to CLIA/CAP and IVD regulations; research specimens fall under RUO designation; QC specimens support internal quality programs; manufacturing specimens are governed by GMP.']
  irb_protocol_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Institutional Review Board (IRB) approved protocol under which the specimen in this container was collected. Required for research use specimens to ensure informed consent compliance. Links container to the governing research ethics approval.']
  is_dnase_rnase_free boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container is certified DNase- and RNase-free (True), ensuring nucleic acid integrity for genomic and transcriptomic applications. Critical for NGS library preparation, RNA sequencing, and qPCR workflows.']
  is_low_bind boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container has a low-binding surface treatment (True) to minimize nucleic acid or protein adsorption to container walls. Important for low-input NGS library preparation and single-cell genomics workflows.']
  is_phi_deidentified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container label and associated metadata have been de-identified to remove Protected Health Information (PHI) per HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination method. True = de-identified; False = identifiable PHI present. Governs data access controls and downstream analytics eligibility.']
  is_sterile boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container was manufactured and certified as sterile (True) or is non-sterile (False). Determines eligibility for clinical specimen collection, cell culture applications, and GMP manufacturing workflows.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the container record in LabVantage LIMS. Used for audit trail maintenance, data synchronization, and change detection in the Databricks Silver layer. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  lims_container_code varchar [note: 'Native container identifier assigned by the LabVantage LIMS source system. Preserved for bidirectional reconciliation between the Databricks Silver layer and the operational LIMS. Distinct from the surrogate container_id.']
  material varchar [note: 'Primary material composition of the container (e.g., polypropylene, glass, polystyrene). Determines chemical compatibility with specimen types, sterilization method eligibility, and storage temperature range.']
  max_freeze_thaw_cycles varchar [note: 'Maximum number of freeze-thaw cycles permitted for the specimen type held in this container before the sample is considered compromised per laboratory SOP. Drives automated QC alerts in LabVantage when freeze_thaw_count is exceeded.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for laboratory staff to record observations, anomalies, handling instructions, or special conditions associated with the container (e.g., visible hemolysis, lipemia, container damage, special handling requirements). Supports chain-of-custody documentation.']
  plate_position varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric well position identifier for containers that are wells within a microplate or array plate (e.g., A01, H12, P24). Null for standalone tubes and vials. Enables plate-map tracking and automated liquid handling coordination.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Current Quality Control (QC) assessment status of the container contents. Pending = QC not yet performed; Passed = contents meet acceptance criteria; Failed = contents do not meet acceptance criteria; Waived = QC waived per SOP exception; Not Required = QC not applicable for this container type.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the container lot was received into laboratory inventory from the manufacturer or supplier. Used for FIFO inventory management, shelf-life calculation, and incoming QC inspection records in SAP MM.']
  ruo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container is designated for Research Use Only (RUO) and is not intended for use in clinical diagnostic procedures. True = RUO; False = clinical or IVD use. Required for FDA regulatory compliance labeling and downstream workflow routing.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the biological specimen held in the container (e.g., whole blood, FFPE tissue, saliva, buccal swab, plasma, serum, DNA extract, RNA extract, cell line). Drives pre-analytical handling rules, storage temperature requirements, and assay eligibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|plasma|serum|dna_extract|rna_extract|ffpe_tissue|saliva|buccal_swab|cell_line|urine — promote to reference product]']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature for the container and its contents expressed in degrees Celsius. Used to validate storage location assignment and trigger temperature excursion alerts. Typical values: 4°C (refrigerator), 25°C (ambient), -20°C (freezer).']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature for the container and its contents expressed in degrees Celsius. Used to validate storage location assignment and trigger alerts for temperature excursions. Typical values: -196°C (liquid nitrogen), -80°C (ultra-low freezer), -20°C (standard freezer).']
  tube_format varchar [note: 'Standardized format designation of the container (e.g., 1.5 mL Eppendorf, 96-well plate, 384-well plate). Determines compatibility with automated liquid handling instruments, centrifuge rotors, and storage rack configurations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 0.2ml|0.5ml|1.5ml|2.0ml|5.0ml|15ml|50ml|96_well|384_well — promote to reference product]']
  volume_capacity_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity of the container expressed in microliters (µL). Used for liquid handling automation, aliquot volume validation, and storage planning. Typical values range from 0.2 µL (PCR strip tube) to 50,000 µL (50 mL conical).']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a physical container (tube, plate, vial, cryovial, microplate well) used to hold a specimen or aliquot. Captures container barcode, container type, manufacturer, lot number, material (polypropylene, glass), volume capacity, current contents reference, and container status (empty, in-use, discarded). Enables barcode-driven sample tracking and container inventory management within LabVantage.'
}

Table sample.run_sample {
  run_sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this specimen-run assignment record. Primary key for the run_sample association.']
  aliquot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.aliquot. Business justification: run_sample represents the operational assignment of biological material to an instrument run (sequencing, array, etc.). In practice, it is the aliquot — not the original specimen — that is physically ']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Sequencing runs in clinical/IVD genomics must reference the regulatory approval (e.g., NGS panel 510(k) clearance) under which the run is performed. This is a named compliance requirement for clinical']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Each sequencing run sample is processed using a specific reagent kit or flow cell product (catalog item). NGS run planning, yield reporting, and cost-per-sample calculations require knowing which sequ']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Run-level deviations (index hopping, lane failure, loading concentration outside spec) require formal deviation records. Genomics sequencing labs link run_sample events to deviations for GxP complianc']
  experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.experiment. Business justification: A run_sample is the direct execution of an experiment\'s sequencing step. Linking run_sample to experiment enables experiment-level sequencing metrics aggregation (coverage, Q30, reads generated) and s']
  extraction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.extraction. Business justification: In genomics sequencing workflows, the material loaded onto an instrument run is typically extracted nucleic acid (DNA/RNA) produced by an extraction process. run_sample should reference the extraction']
  instrument_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the instrument run in which this specimen was sequenced. References the run execution record.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.line. Business justification: Each sequencing run sample fulfills a specific ordered sequencing service line. Linking run_sample to order.line supports per-line service delivery confirmation, TAT compliance tracking, and revenue r']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: A run_sample with run_qc_status=FAIL (e.g., coverage below spec, high duplicate rate) triggers a nonconformance. Sequencing labs require LIMS-to-QMS linkage from the failed run sample to the NCR for r']
  oos_investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.oos_investigation. Business justification: Run samples with OOS sequencing metrics (e.g., percent_q30 below specification, actual_coverage_depth below target) trigger OOS investigations. Direct linkage supports Phase I/II investigation trackin']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: Sequencing runs in genomics biotech are manufacturing production events. Linking run_sample to production_batch enables batch-level QC reporting, yield tracking, and GMP batch record completion. Opera']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the biological specimen that was loaded into this sequencing run. References the core specimen master entity.']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Run QC pass/fail (percent_q30, actual_coverage_depth, actual_reads_generated) is evaluated against a product specification (q30_score_threshold_percent, coverage_depth_min). Regulatory and GxP complia']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.study. Business justification: Sequencing runs are performed in the context of studies. Study-level run tracking is required for coverage reporting, data delivery to study sponsors, and study closeout documentation in clinical geno']
  test_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_order. Business justification: A sequencing run sample record must reference the clinical test order it fulfills for TAT tracking, order completion workflows, and billing. In genomics labs, run_sample is the operational record link']
  actual_coverage_depth decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual average sequencing coverage depth achieved for this specimen in this run, calculated from actual_reads_generated and reference genome size. Compared against target_coverage_depth to assess run success.']
  actual_reads_generated varchar [note: 'Total number of sequencing reads (or read pairs for paired-end) generated for this specimen in this run, as determined by demultiplexing. Used to calculate actual coverage and assess run balance. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specimen was assigned to this run in the LIMS system. Captures when the multiplexing decision was made.']
  demultiplexed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when demultiplexing completed for this specimen, producing specimen-specific FASTQ files. Marks when per-specimen data became available.']
  duplicate_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of reads identified as PCR duplicates for this specimen. High duplicate rates indicate over-amplification during library prep or low library complexity.']
  fastq_file_path varchar [note: 'File system path or cloud storage URI to the demultiplexed FASTQ files for this specimen from this run. Links sequencing data to downstream bioinformatics pipelines.']
  index_sequence varchar [note: 'Unique index barcode sequence (or dual-index pair) assigned to this specimen for multiplexed sequencing. Used by demultiplexing software to separate reads by specimen. Format: single index (ACGTACGT) or dual index (ACGTACGT+TGCATGCA). Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  lane_number varchar [note: 'Physical lane number on the flow cell where this specimen was loaded (1-8 for HiSeq/NovaSeq). Critical for demultiplexing and per-lane quality assessment. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  loading_concentration_nm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Molar concentration (nanomolar, nM) of this specimen\'s library at the time of loading into the sequencing run. Influences cluster density and read balance across multiplexed specimens. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  mean_insert_size varchar [note: 'Average insert size (distance between paired-end reads) for this specimen\'s library in base pairs. Used to assess library preparation quality and detect size selection issues.']
  percent_aligned decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of this specimen\'s reads that successfully aligned to the reference genome. Low alignment rates indicate contamination, adapter issues, or sample quality problems.']
  percent_q30 decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of bases with Phred quality score of 30 or higher (≥99.9% accuracy) for this specimen\'s reads in this run. Key quality metric for per-specimen QC assessment. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  pool_position varchar [note: 'Position or slot number of this specimen within the multiplexed pool loaded into the lane. Used for tracking specimen order during library pooling. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  qc_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when QC review was completed and run_qc_status was assigned for this specimen-run pairing.']
  resequencing_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this specimen requires re-sequencing due to insufficient coverage, quality issues, or run failure. Drives downstream workflow decisions.']
  run_qc_status varchar [note: 'Quality control status for this specimen in this run: pass (meets quality thresholds), fail (requires re-sequencing), warning (marginal quality), pending (QC not yet complete). Drives re-sequencing decisions. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  target_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'Planned average sequencing coverage depth (e.g., 30X for WGS, 100X for WES) for this specific specimen in this run. May differ from the run-level target if specimens have different coverage requirements. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment of a biological specimen to a specific instrument sequencing run. In genomics operations, multiple specimens are routinely multiplexed into a single sequencing run (8-96+ specimens per run), and individual specimens may be re-sequenced across multiple runs for additional coverage, validation, or QC failure recovery. Each record captures the lane assignment, index sequences for demultiplexing, loading concentration, per-specimen read counts, quality metrics, and QC status for each specimen-run pairing. This is the core operational entity that enables multiplexed sequencing workflows and per-specimen quality tracking across the sequencing lifecycle.. Existence Justification: In genomics sequencing operations, specimens and instrument runs have a genuine many-to-many relationship. Multiple specimens (8-96+) are routinely multiplexed into a single sequencing run to maximize instrument utilization and reduce per-specimen cost. Individual specimens are frequently re-sequenced across multiple runs when initial runs fail QC, when additional coverage is needed, or for validation purposes. The business actively manages these specimen-run assignments as operational entities with specific lane assignments, index sequences, loading concentrations, and per-specimen quality metrics.'
}

  Note: 'Master record for every physical instrument asset owned or managed by Genomics Biotech — sequencing platforms (NGS, WGS), array scanners, PCR/qPCR systems, liquid handlers, and gene-editing equipment. Captures serial number, model, firmware version, asset tag, acquisition date, warranty expiry, GxP classification (RUO/IVD), site location, and current operational status. This is the SSOT for instrument identity and lifecycle within the instrument domain.'
}

  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Manufacturer-specified mean time between failures (MTBF) in operational hours. Key reliability metric for capacity planning and uptime forecasting.']

  model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the instrument model in the product catalog. Active models are available for purchase; discontinued models are no longer sold but may still be supported.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this instrument model record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.']
  multiplexing_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of samples that can be pooled and sequenced simultaneously using index barcodes. Enables high-throughput sample processing.']
  physical_dimensions varchar [note: 'Instrument physical dimensions (length x width x height) in standard units. Required for laboratory space planning and installation.']
  platform_generation varchar [note: 'Generation or version of the sequencing/analysis platform (e.g., NextSeq 1000/2000, NovaSeq X Series, Illumina DRAGEN). Indicates technology evolution and capability tier.']
  power_requirements varchar [note: 'Electrical power specifications (voltage, amperage, phase) required for instrument operation. Critical for facility planning and installation qualification (IQ).']
  preventive_maintenance_schedule varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-recommended preventive maintenance schedule (e.g., quarterly, semi-annual, annual tasks). Ensures instrument reliability and regulatory compliance.']
  read_length_capability varchar [note: 'Supported read length configurations in base pairs (e.g., 2x150bp, 2x250bp, single-end 75bp). Critical for application compatibility (WGS, WES, RNA-Seq).']
  run_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Typical or maximum run time in hours for a standard sequencing or analysis run. Critical for turnaround time (TAT) planning and capacity modeling.']
  sequencing_chemistry varchar [note: 'Supported sequencing chemistry or detection method (e.g., Sequencing by Synthesis (SBS), Reversible Terminator, Two-Channel SBS). Defines compatible reagent kits and flow cells.']
  service_contract_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether extended service contracts or service level agreements (SLAs) are available for this instrument model. True if available, False otherwise.']
  software_platform varchar [note: 'Onboard or companion software platform name and version (e.g., Illumina DRAGEN, BaseSpace, Instrument Control Software). Critical for bioinformatics pipeline compatibility.']
  supported_applications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of validated genomic applications (e.g., WGS, WES, RNA-Seq, Targeted Sequencing, Methylation, CNV Detection, SNP Genotyping). Guides customer application selection.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Standard manufacturer warranty period in months from date of installation. Impacts service agreement planning and total cost of ownership.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Instrument weight in kilograms. Required for shipping, handling, and facility floor load calculations.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of instrument model definitions — sequencing platforms, array scanners, PCR/qPCR analyzers, liquid handlers, and CRISPR delivery systems. Stores manufacturer, model number, platform generation, read length capability, throughput specifications, supported chemistries, regulatory clearance class (IVD/RUO), and compatible reagent families. Serves as the template from which individual instrument assets are instantiated.'
}

  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: GMP/GxP IQ execution requires traceability of specific material batches (calibration standards, reagents) consumed during qualification. 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11 mandate batch-level traceability']
  customer_signoff_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: GxP and 21 CFR Part 11 require a named, traceable customer signatory on IQ completion. The product has customer_signature_captured_flag and customer_acceptance_date but no FK to the actual contact. Ro']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: IQ failures or protocol deviations observed during instrument installation qualification in GxP genomics labs must be formally documented as quality deviations (21 CFR Part 11). Linking IQ to deviatio']
  equipment_qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.equipment_qualification. Business justification: GxP regulatory requirement: instrument IQ must be traceable to the manufacturing equipment qualification record (21 CFR Part 11, EU Annex 11). QA auditors and validation engineers expect direct tracea']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.model. Business justification: IQ/OQ/PQ qualification protocols in GxP-regulated genomics environments are model-specific — the qualification procedure, acceptance criteria, and protocol version are defined at the instrument model ']
  performing_laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.performing_laboratory. Business justification: IQ/OQ/PQ qualifications for instruments at CLIA-certified labs must be tied to the specific performing laboratory entity for accreditation records. CAP inspectors verify that qualification documentati']
  protocol_document_id varchar [note: 'Document management system identifier for the qualification protocol, linking to the controlled document repository (e.g., Veeva Vault, MasterControl).']
  qualification_reagent_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: GxP regulations (21 CFR Part 211, GAMP 5) mandate traceability of the exact reagent lots used during IQ execution on genomics instruments. IQ protocols require specific reagent lots for qualification ']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.risk_assessment. Business justification: IQ protocols in GxP genomics environments are risk-based; the risk assessment determines IQ scope, test case depth, and acceptance criteria (GAMP 5, ICH Q9). Linking IQ to its governing risk assessmen']
  software_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.software_release. Business justification: GxP IQ protocols qualify a specific software release on an instrument. FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11 require documenting which software version was validated during IQ. A genomics biotech validat']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification results were formally approved by the quality assurance authority.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the quality assurance manager or authorized signatory who approved the qualification results.']
  cfr_part_11_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator confirming whether electronic records and signatures for this qualification meet FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements for electronic records in regulated environments.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes from the qualification engineer, quality reviewer, or customer regarding special conditions, observations, or follow-up actions.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification was finalized, including all documentation, approvals, and closeout activities.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this qualification record, supporting audit trail requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this qualification record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  customer_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer formally accepted the qualification results and instrument readiness for use.']
  customer_signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator confirming whether customer acceptance signature was captured as part of the qualification closeout process.']
  deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of deviations or non-conformances identified during the qualification execution.']
  deviation_summary varchar [note: 'Summary description of deviations, non-conformances, or out-of-specification results noted during qualification, including corrective actions taken.']
  environmental_conditions varchar [note: 'Recorded environmental conditions during qualification execution, including temperature, humidity, and other relevant parameters required for instrument performance validation.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification protocol was executed and testing performed. Principal business event timestamp for the qualification lifecycle.']
  gxp_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator confirming whether this qualification was performed under GxP-regulated conditions (GMP, GLP, GCP) as required for FDA, EMA, or other regulatory submissions.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this qualification record, supporting audit trail and change control requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this qualification record was last updated, supporting audit trail and change control requirements.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final pass/fail outcome of the qualification event. Conditional pass indicates acceptance with documented deviations or corrective actions.']
  performed_by_engineer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the field service engineer or validation specialist who executed the qualification protocol.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the qualification protocol document used for this execution, ensuring traceability to approved procedures.']
  qualification_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique document number or protocol identifier for this IQ/OQ/PQ qualification event, used for traceability and regulatory submission.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification event: scheduled, in progress, passed, failed, conditional pass (with deviations), cancelled, or pending approval. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in_progress|passed|failed|conditional_pass|cancelled|pending_approval — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualification performed: IQ (Installation Qualification), OQ (Operational Qualification), PQ (Performance Qualification), or combined phases.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator denoting whether this qualification record is required for or has been included in a regulatory submission to FDA, EMA, or other governing bodies.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for the qualification event, used for resource planning and customer coordination.']
  service_order_number varchar [note: 'ServiceNow or SAP PM service order number associated with the field service dispatch for this qualification event, enabling integration with field service management.']
  test_case_count varchar [note: 'Total number of test cases or acceptance criteria executed as part of the qualification protocol.']
  test_case_passed_count varchar [note: 'Number of test cases that met acceptance criteria and passed during qualification execution.']

  Note: 'Records the IQ/OQ/PQ (Installation Qualification, Operational Qualification, Performance Qualification) lifecycle for each instrument asset at a customer or internal site. Captures qualification type (IQ/OQ/PQ), execution date, performed-by engineer, pass/fail outcome, deviations noted, approval signatures, and GxP compliance attestation. Required for FDA 21 CFR Part 11, ISO 13485, and GxP-regulated environments.'
}

Table instrument.calibration_record {

  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the instrument asset that was calibrated. Links to the instrument master data product.']

  calibration_standard_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: GMP calibration requires traceability of the specific batch of calibration standard used.
