  metric_category varchar [note: 'Functional category grouping the QC metric for reporting and trend analysis. Enables domain-specific QC dashboards (e.g., coverage metrics vs. base quality metrics vs. alignment metrics). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: coverage|base_quality|duplication|alignment|contamination|library_complexity|variant_calling — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  metric_computation_duration_sec varchar [note: 'Wall-clock time in seconds taken by the QC tool to compute this metric. Supports HPC/cloud compute resource optimization and pipeline performance benchmarking.']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Standardized name of the QC metric being recorded (e.g., mean_coverage, pct_q30_bases, duplication_rate, on_target_rate, uniformity, insert_size_median, gc_bias_score). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mean_coverage|pct_q30_bases|duplication_rate|on_target_rate|uniformity|insert_size_median|gc_bias_score|chimeric_read_rate|adapter_contamination_rate|phred_score_mean — promote to reference product]']
  metric_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the metric value (e.g., \'x\' for coverage depth, \'%\' for percentage-based metrics, \'bp\' for base-pair measurements, \'reads\' for read counts). Enables correct interpretation and cross-metric comparison.']
  metric_value double [note: 'Numeric value of the QC metric as computed by the QC tool for this pipeline step and sample. This is the principal quantitative observation (e.g., 45.3 for mean_coverage, 0.92 for pct_q30_bases).']
  pass_threshold double [note: 'Minimum (or maximum, per threshold_direction) acceptable value for this metric to be considered passing. Defines the QC gate boundary used for automated pass/fail evaluation.']
  pipeline_stage varchar [note: 'Logical stage of the bioinformatics pipeline at which this QC metric was evaluated. Enables stage-specific QC dashboards and failure pattern analysis across the FASTQ-to-VCF data lineage.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Automated evaluation result for this metric against the defined pass threshold. \'pass\' indicates the metric meets the threshold; \'fail\' triggers remediation workflow; \'warn\' indicates a marginal result requiring review; \'pending\' indicates evaluation not yet completed; \'not_evaluated\' indicates the metric was captured but no threshold was applicable.']
  qc_tool_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bioinformatics QC tool that computed this metric (e.g., FastQC, Picard, MultiQC, Samtools). Enables tool-specific metric interpretation and version-controlled reproducibility tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FastQC|Picard|MultiQC|GATK|Trimmomatic|Samtools|Qualimap|Fastp|Mosdepth|VerifyBamID — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  qc_tool_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the QC tool used to compute this metric (e.g., \'0.11.9\' for FastQC, \'2.27.5\' for Picard). Essential for reproducibility, audit trails, and identifying metric drift caused by tool upgrades.']
  read_length_bp varchar [note: 'Sequencing read length in base pairs (bp) for the run associated with this QC metric (e.g., 150 for 2x150bp paired-end). Read length affects coverage and base quality metric interpretation.']
  regulatory_use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the assay context in which this QC metric was generated (IVD, RUO, LDT, clinical trial, research). Determines applicable QC standards and regulatory reporting obligations.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Documented corrective or remediation action taken or recommended in response to a QC failure (e.g., \'re-sequence sample\', \'increase library input\', \'repeat library preparation\'). Supports CAPA workflows and GxP compliance.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation workflow for a failed QC metric. \'not_required\' for passing metrics; \'pending\', \'in_progress\', \'resolved\', or \'escalated\' for failing metrics requiring corrective action.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Formal outcome of the QA/QC reviewer\'s assessment of this metric result. \'waived\' indicates a documented exception approval for a failing metric. Required for GxP and clinical laboratory compliance.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when a QA/QC reviewer formally reviewed this metric result. Supports GxP electronic record requirements and TAT (Turnaround Time) tracking for clinical workflows.']
  sop_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier of the SOP governing the QC evaluation procedure for this metric (e.g., \'SOP-BIO-042-v3\'). Links the metric record to the controlled document system for GxP and regulatory compliance.']
  source_file_path varchar [note: 'File system or object storage path to the QC tool output file (e.g., FastQC HTML report, Picard metrics file, MultiQC JSON) from which this metric was extracted. Supports data lineage and audit traceability.']
  target_region_bed_file varchar [note: 'File path or identifier of the BED file defining the genomic target regions used for on-target rate and coverage uniformity calculations. Applicable for WES and targeted panel assays.']
  threshold_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the pass_threshold is a minimum bound (metric must be >= threshold), maximum bound (metric must be <= threshold), or a range (metric must fall within defined bounds). Required for correct automated QC gating logic.']
  threshold_lower_bound double [note: 'Lower boundary of an acceptable range for this metric when threshold_direction is \'range\'. Null when threshold_direction is \'min\' or \'max\'.']
  threshold_upper_bound double [note: 'Upper boundary of an acceptable range for this metric when threshold_direction is \'range\'. Null when threshold_direction is \'min\' or \'max\'.']
  threshold_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the QC threshold specification applied during evaluation (e.g., \'v1.2.0\'). Enables audit traceability when thresholds are updated over time and supports longitudinal comparability of QC results.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this QC metric record was last modified (e.g., following reviewer update or remediation status change). Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  waiver_justification varchar [note: 'Documented scientific or clinical justification for waiving a QC failure and allowing downstream processing to proceed. Populated only when review_outcome is \'waived\'. Required for regulatory audit trails.']

  Note: 'Stores structured QC metric records generated at each pipeline stage — including metric name (mean_coverage, pct_q30_bases, duplication_rate, on_target_rate, uniformity), metric value, unit, pass/fail threshold, QC tool (FastQC, Picard, MultiQC), pipeline run and step reference, evaluation timestamp, and failure flag with remediation notes when thresholds are breached. Enables automated QC gating, longitudinal QC trending, and systematic quality failure pattern analysis.'
}

Table bioinformatics.genomic_artifact {
  genomic_artifact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each genomic data artifact record in the master registry. Primary key for the genomic_artifact data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a managed data file asset produced or consumed by bioinformatics pipelines.']
  accession_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.accession. Business justification: Clinical genomic artifacts must be traceable to the originating accession for result delivery, data access authorization, and regulatory compliance. PHI-containing artifacts (BAM, VCF) require accessi']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Genomic data files (BAMs, VCFs, analysis reports) generated for customer accounts in commercial service or collaborative research contexts. Essential for data delivery tracking, customer portal access']
  aliquot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.aliquot. Business justification: Genomic artifacts (FASTQ, BAM, VCF files) are generated from a specific aliquot. Data retention policies, PHI management, and GxP data integrity requirements in regulated genomics environments mandate']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Reference datasets, genome assemblies, annotation databases are sold as catalog items (e.g., Illumina BaseSpace datasets, GATK resource bundles). Business need: product sales tracking, licensing enfor']
  delivery_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.delivery_line. Business justification: Genomic artifacts (VCF, BAM, reports) are the digital deliverables fulfilled against delivery line items. Linking artifact to delivery_line enables proof-of-delivery tracking for digital genomics outp']
  demux_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.demux_result. Business justification: Artifacts originate from demultiplexing, critical for file provenance and storage management. This link enables tracking FASTQ files through processing pipelines, required for data lineage audits and ']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Genomic artifacts with data integrity issues (checksum mismatch, unexpected file size, QC failure) trigger formal deviations in GxP genomics operations. Linking genomic_artifact to quality.deviation e']
  extraction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.extraction. Business justification: Genomic artifacts are the computational representation of an extraction\'s nucleic acid. File provenance tracking, regulatory audit trails, and data lineage documentation require linking artifacts to t']
  flow_cell_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sequencing flow cell on which the originating sequencing run was performed. Enables traceability to the physical consumable lot, instrument, and run conditions. Critical for QC investigations and reagent lot correlation analysis.']
  instrument_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instrument.instrument_run. Business justification: Raw sequencing files (FASTQ, BCL) are generated by instrument runs. This link establishes data provenance from file creation through analysis pipeline, required for regulatory submissions (FDA/CLIA), ']
  library_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.library. Business justification: Genomic artifacts (BAM/VCF files) are library-specific outputs required for data management, archival, and retrieval. This link enables library-level file organization, storage policy enforcement, and']
  parent_artifact_genomic_artifact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate upstream artifact from which this artifact was derived (e.g., a BAM artifact\'s parent is the FASTQ artifact; a VCF artifact\'s parent is the BAM artifact). Enables reconstruction of the full data lineage chain from raw to interpreted results.']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bioinformatics pipeline run that produced or last processed this artifact. Establishes provenance linkage from the artifact back to the specific pipeline execution, algorithm version, and compute parameters used. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE (resource reference) for this entity.']
  pipeline_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.pipeline_version. Business justification: genomic_artifact has a denormalized `pipeline_version` STRING column. Every genomic artifact (FASTQ, BAM, VCF, etc.) is produced by a specific pipeline version, and tracking which version produced whi']
  qc_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.qc_result. Business justification: Genomic artifacts (FASTQ, BAM, VCF) are generated from samples that underwent laboratory QC testing. Linking enables complete data lineage from sample receipt through QC to final bioinformatics output']
  run_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.run_sample. Business justification: FASTQ artifacts are generated per run_sample after demultiplexing. The run_sample record defines the barcode/index sequence and lane that produced the artifact. This link is essential for demultiplexi']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample from which this genomic artifact was derived. Enables traceability from the final computational artifact back to the physical sample, supporting clinical reporting, QC, and regulatory audit requirements.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Genomic artifacts (BAM, VCF, FASTQ files) submitted as raw data packages in FDA/EMA regulatory submissions must be directly traceable to the submission record. Required for data integrity verification']
  archived_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this artifact was moved to archival storage (e.g., AWS S3 Glacier), in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Null if the artifact has not been archived. Used for storage cost tracking, retrieval SLA management, and compliance with data lifecycle policies.']
  artifact_external_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for the artifact as assigned by the originating system of record (e.g., Illumina BaseSpace file ID, LabVantage LIMS artifact accession number, or cloud storage object key). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  artifact_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label assigned to the genomic artifact, typically derived from the sample identifier, run identifier, and artifact type (e.g., \'SampleA_WGS_R1.fastq.gz\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  artifact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the genomic artifact within the bioinformatics pipeline ecosystem. \'pending\' indicates awaiting generation; \'available\' indicates ready for downstream consumption; \'processing\' indicates actively being transformed; \'failed\' indicates a pipeline error; \'archived\' indicates moved to cold storage; \'deleted\' indicates purged per retention policy. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  artifact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the genomic data artifact by its file format and biological content type. Drives downstream pipeline routing, storage tier assignment, and retention policy application. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FASTQ|BAM|CRAM|VCF|gVCF|BED|ANNOTATION|REPORT|INDEX|QC_METRICS — promote to reference product]']
  barcode_sequence varchar [note: 'Index/barcode nucleotide sequence used to identify this sample within a multiplexed sequencing pool during demultiplexing. Enables traceability of the artifact to its specific library preparation and sample assignment in a pooled run.']
  checksum_md5 varchar [note: 'MD5 hash digest of the artifact file content used for data integrity verification during transfer, storage, and retrieval. Enables detection of file corruption or unauthorized modification. Widely used in genomics data exchange protocols.']
  checksum_sha256 varchar [note: 'SHA-256 cryptographic hash of the artifact file content providing stronger integrity assurance than MD5. Required for regulatory-grade data integrity verification under GxP and HIPAA security rule compliance. Preferred checksum for clinical and regulatory submissions.']
  compression_format varchar [note: 'Compression algorithm applied to the artifact file. BGZF (Blocked GNU Zip Format) is standard for BAM/CRAM/VCF files enabling random access; GZIP is standard for FASTQ files. Determines decompression requirements for downstream pipeline steps and storage cost calculations.']
  contains_phi boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this artifact contains Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined under HIPAA. Drives access control enforcement, audit logging requirements, and data handling procedures. Must be set to True for any artifact linked to an identified clinical sample.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this genomic artifact record was first created in the registry, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and retention policy calculation.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Privacy and regulatory classification of the genomic data contained in this artifact. \'PHI\' (Protected Health Information) indicates data subject to HIPAA; \'de_identified\' indicates PHI has been removed per HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination; \'research\' indicates IRB-governed research data; \'restricted\' indicates controlled-access data (e.g., dbGaP); \'public\' indicates open-access data.']
  file_generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the physical artifact file was generated by the pipeline or instrument, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Distinct from the registry record creation timestamp — represents the actual file creation time as reported by the pipeline or storage system. Used for data freshness assessment and TAT (Turnaround Time) calculations.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Physical size of the genomic artifact file in bytes at the time of creation or last verification. Used for storage capacity planning, cost allocation, and data transfer monitoring. Serves as the principal MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  genome_build varchar [note: 'Specific reference genome assembly build used during alignment or variant calling (e.g., GRCh38, GRCh37/hg19). Stored directly on the artifact for rapid querying without joining to the reference genome table. Critical for variant interpretation and cross-cohort comparability.']
  index_storage_uri varchar [note: 'Full URI path to the index file associated with this artifact (e.g., \'.bai\' for BAM, \'.tbi\' for VCF). Stored alongside the artifact URI to enable direct access by pipeline tools without requiring a separate lookup.']
  is_indexed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a corresponding index file exists for this artifact (e.g., BAI index for BAM, TBI/CSI index for VCF). Indexed artifacts support random-access queries by genomic coordinate, which is required for most downstream analysis tools.']
  is_paired_end boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the sequencing reads in this artifact were generated using paired-end sequencing chemistry (True) or single-end (False). Paired-end reads improve alignment accuracy and structural variant detection. Determines applicable alignment and QC parameters.']
  lane_number varchar [note: 'Lane number on the sequencing flow cell from which the reads in this artifact were generated. Used for per-lane QC analysis, demultiplexing traceability, and identification of lane-specific sequencing artifacts or failures.']
  mean_coverage_depth decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean sequencing coverage depth (average number of reads covering each base) for alignment artifacts (BAM/CRAM). A key QC metric indicating whether sufficient data was generated for reliable variant calling. Expressed as fold-coverage (e.g., 30.0x for 30-fold coverage).']
  mean_phred_quality_score decimal(6,2) [note: 'Mean Phred base quality score across all reads in the artifact. Phred score (Q) represents the probability of an incorrect base call (Q30 = 99.9% accuracy). A primary QC metric for sequencing run quality assessment per CLIA and CAP standards.']
  percent_q30 decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of bases in the artifact with a Phred quality score of 30 or higher (≥Q30), indicating ≥99.9% base call accuracy. Industry-standard QC threshold metric for NGS data quality acceptance. Typically required to be ≥80% for clinical-grade sequencing.']
  pipeline_stage varchar [note: 'Stage in the bioinformatics processing pipeline at which this artifact was produced. Defines the artifact\'s position in the data lineage chain from raw sequencing output to interpreted genomic results: \'raw\' (FASTQ from demux), \'aligned\' (BAM/CRAM), \'variant_called\' (VCF/gVCF), \'annotated\' (annotated VCF), \'filtered\' (filtered VCF), \'reported\' (final clinical/research report input).']
  qc_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the reason(s) for a QC failure or warning on this artifact (e.g., \'low_coverage\', \'high_duplication_rate\', \'adapter_contamination\', \'low_q30\'). Populated only when qc_status is \'fail\' or \'warning\'. Supports root cause analysis and corrective action tracking.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Quality control assessment outcome for this artifact based on applicable QC metrics (coverage depth, %Q30, alignment rate, etc.). \'pass\' indicates all QC thresholds met; \'fail\' indicates one or more critical thresholds not met; \'warning\' indicates borderline metrics requiring review; \'pending_review\' indicates QC evaluation not yet completed.']
  read_length_bp varchar [note: 'Length of sequencing reads in base pairs for FASTQ and BAM artifacts (e.g., 150 for 2x150bp paired-end). Determines applicable alignment parameters, variant calling sensitivity, and QC thresholds. Stored in base pairs (bp).']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this artifact becomes eligible for archival or deletion per the assigned retention policy. Calculated from the artifact creation date and the applicable retention period. Used by automated data lifecycle management processes.']
  retention_policy varchar [note: 'Retention policy classification governing how long this artifact must be retained before it is eligible for archival or deletion. \'short_term\' (e.g., 90 days for intermediate files); \'standard\' (e.g., 2 years); \'long_term\' (e.g., 7 years for clinical); \'permanent\' (e.g., reference datasets); \'regulatory_hold\' (litigation or regulatory investigation hold).']
  sequencing_assay_type varchar [note: 'Type of genomic sequencing or array assay that generated the raw data underlying this artifact. Determines applicable QC thresholds, coverage expectations, and downstream analysis pipelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|RNA_SEQ|AMPLICON|TARGETED_PANEL|ARRAY|CHIP_SEQ|METHYL_SEQ|ATAC_SEQ|SINGLE_CELL — promote to reference product]']
  storage_platform varchar [note: 'Cloud or on-premises storage platform where the artifact is physically stored. Drives data access patterns, cost allocation, and egress fee tracking. Supports multi-cloud and hybrid storage architecture management.']
  storage_uri varchar [note: 'Full URI path to the artifact file in the storage backend (e.g., S3 bucket path \'s3://genomics-biotech-prod/artifacts/sample123/output.bam\', HDFS path, or NFS mount path). Used by pipeline orchestration and data access layers to locate and retrieve the file.']
  total_read_count varchar [note: 'Total number of sequencing reads contained in this artifact. For FASTQ files, represents raw read count; for BAM/CRAM files, represents total aligned and unaligned reads. Used for QC assessment, yield reporting, and billing calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this genomic artifact record in the registry, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks record modifications for audit trail compliance and change detection in downstream pipelines.']
  use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory use classification of the artifact indicating its intended application context. \'RUO\' (Research Use Only) — not for diagnostic use; \'IVD\' (In Vitro Diagnostic) — cleared/approved for clinical diagnostics; \'LDT\' (Laboratory Developed Test) — validated in-house clinical test; \'clinical\' — used in clinical reporting; \'research\' — used in research studies; \'internal_qc\' — internal quality control purposes.']
  variant_count varchar [note: 'Total number of genomic variants (SNPs, indels, CNVs, SVs) recorded in this artifact for VCF and gVCF file types. Provides a high-level QC indicator and enables rapid assessment of variant calling yield without parsing the full VCF file.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all genomic data artifacts produced or consumed by bioinformatics pipelines — including FASTQ, BAM, CRAM, VCF, gVCF, BED, and annotation output files. Captures artifact type, file path (S3/HDFS/NFS), file size, checksum (MD5/SHA256), compression format, creation timestamp, pipeline run provenance, upstream sequencing run reference (flow cell, lane, barcode, demux batch), retention policy, and data classification (PHI, de-identified, research). Serves as the data asset inventory and input lineage anchor linking raw sequencing outputs to the bioinformatics processing layer.'
}

Table bioinformatics.data_lineage_event {
  data_lineage_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each data lineage event record in the bioinformatics processing chain. Primary key for this table. Entity role: EVENT_LOG — captures immutable transformation events across the genomic data processing pipeline from raw FASTQ ingestion to clinical report generation.']
  aliquot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.aliquot. Business justification: Data lineage events must close the physical-to-digital provenance gap at the aliquot level. GxP data integrity (21 CFR Part 11) and HIPAA audit trail requirements mandate that every data transformatio']
  compute_job_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bioinformatics.compute_job. Business justification: data_lineage_event tracks transformations and has hpc_job_id as STRING. compute_job is the authoritative registry of HPC/cloud jobs with compute_job_id as PK. Normalizing hpc_job_id STRING to compute_']
  demux_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.demux_result. Business justification: Lineage events capture demultiplexing as a transformation step in data processing workflows. This link enables tracking FASTQ file generation and subsequent processing, required for GxP-compliant data']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.deviation. Business justification: Data lineage events recording errors or unexpected data transformations (event_status=error, unexpected record counts) trigger formal deviations in GxP genomics operations. This link enables investiga']
  extraction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sample.extraction. Business justification: Data lineage events must trace back to the extraction step as the origin of digital genomic data. GxP data integrity requirements and regulatory submissions require a complete, unbroken chain from ext']
  library_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.library. Business justification: Lineage tracking at library level enables sample-level audit trails and consent management. This link supports clinical laboratories in demonstrating complete chain of custody from sample receipt thro']
  parent_lineage_event_data_lineage_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediately preceding lineage event in the processing chain. Enables construction of a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of transformation events for end-to-end data lineage traversal from raw FASTQ to clinical report.']
  pipeline_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent bioinformatics pipeline run that encompasses this lineage event. Enables grouping of all lineage events within a single pipeline execution for end-to-end traceability.']
  pipeline_run_step_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific pipeline run step that produced or triggered this lineage event. Links the lineage record to the bioinformatics pipeline execution context for reproducibility and audit traceability.']
  primary_data_genomic_artifact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the genomic data artifact (e.g., FASTQ, BAM, VCF, gVCF, annotation file) that served as the input to this transformation event. Supports forward and backward data lineage tracing.']
  sample_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological sample associated with this lineage event, as tracked in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS). Provides traceability from raw genomic data back to the originating sample.']
  sequencing_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sequencing.sequencing_run. Business justification: Lineage events track transformations from sequencing runs through pipeline stages, required for GxP compliance and FDA 21 CFR Part 11 audit trails. This link enables complete data provenance from raw ']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Data lineage events in GxP bioinformatics environments must be traceable to the regulatory submission they support, satisfying 21 CFR Part 11 and Annex 11 audit trail requirements. Regulators inspecti']
  target_artifact_genomic_artifact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the genomic data artifact produced as the output of this transformation event (e.g., aligned BAM, called VCF, annotated VCF, clinical report). Enables downstream lineage traversal.']
  algorithm_parameters varchar [note: 'Serialized string (JSON or key=value pairs) of the runtime parameters passed to the bioinformatics tool for this transformation event (e.g., minimum base quality score, minimum mapping quality, ploidy setting). Critical for reproducibility and regulatory audit trails.']
  assay_type varchar [note: 'Type of genomic assay associated with this lineage event, indicating the sequencing or array methodology applied (e.g., Whole Genome Sequencing (WGS), Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), targeted gene panel, SNP array, Copy Number Variation (CNV) analysis, RNA-seq, amplicon sequencing). Used for assay-specific pipeline routing and regulatory classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|SNP_array|CNV_analysis|RNA_seq|amplicon — promote to reference product]']
  compute_platform varchar [note: 'The computing infrastructure platform on which this transformation event was executed (e.g., AWS Batch, AWS EMR, HPC SLURM cluster, Databricks). Used for cost attribution, performance benchmarking, and infrastructure capacity planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aws_batch|aws_emr|hpc_slurm|hpc_lsf|databricks|local|on_premise_hpc — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cpu_cores_used varchar [note: 'Number of CPU cores allocated and consumed by the compute job executing this transformation event. Used for HPC/cloud resource consumption tracking, cost attribution, and pipeline performance optimization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when this lineage event record was first captured in the data platform. Distinct from event_timestamp (the real-world transformation time). Required for GxP audit trails and 21 CFR Part 11 record integrity.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Structured error or exception code returned by the bioinformatics tool or pipeline orchestrator when a transformation event fails (e.g., TOOL_EXIT_NONZERO, INSUFFICIENT_COVERAGE, CHECKSUM_MISMATCH, TIMEOUT). Used for failure root-cause analysis and automated alerting.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Human-readable error or exception message captured when a transformation event fails or produces a warning. Provides detailed context for failure root-cause analysis, pipeline debugging, and GxP deviation investigation.']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this lineage event, formatted as LIN- followed by an alphanumeric code. Used for cross-system referencing, audit log correlation, and regulatory submission traceability.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the data transformation event. Indicates whether the transformation is pending execution, actively running, successfully completed, failed with error, skipped due to conditional logic, or retried after a prior failure. Used for pipeline monitoring and failure root-cause analysis.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the data transformation event occurred in the bioinformatics pipeline. This is the principal real-world event time, distinct from record audit timestamps. Critical for GxP audit trails and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Categorical discriminator identifying the kind of data transformation that occurred in the bioinformatics pipeline. Values: ingest (raw FASTQ ingestion), transform (alignment/variant calling), filter (QC filtering), merge (combining artifacts), split (demultiplexing), annotate (variant annotation), export (clinical report generation), archive (long-term storage). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ingest|transform|filter|merge|split|annotate|export|archive — promote to reference product]']
  execution_duration_seconds decimal(12,3) [note: 'Wall-clock elapsed time in seconds for the transformation event to complete, from job start to job end. Used for pipeline performance benchmarking, Turnaround Time (TAT) analysis, and SLA monitoring.']
  gxp_audit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transformation event is subject to GxP (Good Practice Regulations including GMP, GLP, GCP) audit trail requirements (True = GxP-regulated event, False = non-regulated event). Used to filter events for regulatory audit reporting and inspection readiness.']
  hipaa_phi_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transformation event processed or produced data containing Protected Health Information (PHI) as defined under HIPAA (True = PHI involved, False = no PHI). Used for HIPAA compliance reporting, data access controls, and breach risk assessment.']
  input_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of input records, reads, or variants consumed by this transformation event (e.g., number of FASTQ reads ingested, number of variants in input VCF). Used for data volume tracking, QC validation, and pipeline performance analysis.']
  memory_used_gb decimal(10,3) [note: 'Amount of RAM in gigabytes consumed by the compute job executing this transformation event. Used for HPC/cloud resource consumption tracking, cost attribution, and pipeline performance optimization.']
  operator_identity varchar [note: 'Username, service account name, or system identity (human user or automated pipeline agent) that initiated or executed this transformation event. Required for GxP audit trails, 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature traceability, and HIPAA access accountability.']
  operator_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes whether the transformation event was initiated by a human user, an automated bioinformatics pipeline, a system service account, or a scheduled job. Supports audit trail differentiation and compliance reporting under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  output_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of output records, reads, or variants produced by this transformation event (e.g., number of aligned reads in output BAM, number of variants in output VCF). Used for data volume tracking, QC validation, and yield analysis.']
  pipeline_stage varchar [note: 'High-level stage of the bioinformatics processing pipeline in which this transformation event occurred. Values: ingestion (raw FASTQ intake), preprocessing (trimming/QC), alignment (BAM/CRAM generation), variant_calling (VCF/gVCF), annotation (variant annotation), qc_filtering (QC-based filtering), reporting (clinical report generation), archival (long-term storage). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ingestion|preprocessing|alignment|variant_calling|annotation|qc_filtering|reporting|archival — promote to reference product]']
  qc_metric_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary Quality Control (QC) metric evaluated at this transformation step (e.g., mean_coverage_depth, percent_mapped_reads, phred_score_q30_percent, ts_tv_ratio, variant_call_rate). Provides context for the QC pass/fail determination.']
  qc_metric_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric value of the primary Quality Control (QC) metric evaluated at this transformation step (e.g., 45.7 for mean coverage depth, 98.3 for percent mapped reads). Used for QC trending, threshold monitoring, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  qc_pass_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the output artifact of this transformation event passed Quality Control (QC) criteria (True = QC passed, False = QC failed). Used for pipeline gating, clinical report eligibility, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this transformation event has been retried following a prior failure. Used for pipeline reliability monitoring, failure pattern analysis, and identifying systemic infrastructure or data quality issues.']
  source_artifact_checksum varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (MD5, SHA-256, or SHA-512) of the input genomic data artifact at the time of consumption. Used to verify data integrity, detect corruption, and confirm that the correct artifact version was used. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 and GxP data integrity compliance.']
  source_artifact_format varchar [note: 'File format of the input genomic data artifact consumed by this transformation event (e.g., FASTQ for raw reads, BAM/CRAM for aligned reads, VCF/gVCF for variant calls). Enables format-aware lineage queries and pipeline validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FASTQ|BAM|CRAM|VCF|gVCF|BED|FASTA|TSV|CSV|JSON|HTML|PDF — promote to reference product]']
  source_artifact_path varchar [note: 'Cloud or network storage path (e.g., S3 URI, ADLS path) of the input genomic data artifact consumed by this transformation event. Confidential as it may reveal internal infrastructure topology and data organization. Required for data recovery, re-processing, and audit traceability.']
  target_artifact_checksum varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (MD5, SHA-256, or SHA-512) of the output genomic data artifact produced by this transformation event. Used to verify data integrity, detect corruption, and confirm artifact authenticity for downstream steps. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 and GxP data integrity compliance.']
  target_artifact_format varchar [note: 'File format of the output genomic data artifact produced by this transformation event (e.g., BAM for alignment output, VCF for variant calling output, annotated VCF for annotation output). Enables format-aware lineage queries and downstream pipeline validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FASTQ|BAM|CRAM|VCF|gVCF|BED|FASTA|TSV|CSV|JSON|HTML|PDF — promote to reference product]']
  target_artifact_path varchar [note: 'Cloud or network storage path (e.g., S3 URI, ADLS path) of the output genomic data artifact produced by this transformation event. Confidential as it may reveal internal infrastructure topology. Required for downstream pipeline steps, data recovery, and audit traceability.']
  tool_container_image varchar [note: 'Docker or container image URI (including registry, repository, and digest/tag) used to execute the bioinformatics tool, ensuring exact computational environment reproducibility. Example: docker.io/broadinstitute/gatk:4.2.6.1. Supports GxP validation and 21 CFR Part 11 audit requirements.']
  tool_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bioinformatics software tool or algorithm used to perform the data transformation (e.g., BWA-MEM, GATK HaplotypeCaller, STAR, Trimmomatic, SnpEff, VEP). Essential for reproducibility and regulatory validation documentation.']
  tool_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version string of the bioinformatics tool used for the transformation (e.g., 4.2.6.1 for GATK, 0.7.17 for BWA). Required for reproducibility, pipeline validation, and regulatory submission documentation under GxP and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when this lineage event record was last modified in the data platform (e.g., status update from running to completed, error message appended). Required for GxP audit trails and 21 CFR Part 11 record integrity.']
  use_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory use classification of the genomic data processed in this lineage event. Values: RUO (Research Use Only), IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic), LDT (Laboratory Developed Test), clinical_research, agricultural, pharmaceutical. Determines applicable regulatory framework and data handling requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each data transformation event in the bioinformatics processing chain — from raw FASTQ ingestion through alignment (BAM/CRAM), variant calling (VCF/gVCF), annotation, QC filtering, and clinical report generation. Records source artifact(s), target artifact(s), transformation tool and version, pipeline run step reference, timestamp, lineage event type (ingest, transform, filter, merge, split, annotate, export, archive), operator/system identity, and error/failure context when transformations fail. Supports GxP audit trails, HIPAA data traceability, 21 CFR Part 11 compliance, reproducibility requirements, and failure root-cause analysis.'
}

Business justification: Pipeline validation studies are conducted to qualify a pipeline for a specific test catalog entry — a CAP/CLIA and FDA IVD requirement.   validation_study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.validation_study. Business justification: GxP traceability requires linking a bioinformatics pipeline validation study to the formal QMS validation study record.   accuracy_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Overall accuracy of the pipeline measured as concordance with a reference standard method, expressed as a percentage. Calculated as (TP + TN) / (TP + TN + FP + FN) × 100. Required for CLIA/CAP and FDA IVD qualification.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the validation study report was formally reviewed and approved by the designated approver. Marks the transition from completed execution to approved regulatory evidence.']
  assay_type varchar [note: 'The genomic assay type for which the pipeline is being validated (e.g., WGS — Whole Genome Sequencing, WES — Whole Exome Sequencing, targeted panel, RNA-seq, CNV array, methylation). Determines applicable regulatory standards and performance criteria. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|RNA_seq|CNV_array|methylation|amplicon|LRS — promote to reference product]']
  cap_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'CAP laboratory accreditation number for the facility conducting the validation. CAP accreditation is a recognized alternative to CLIA inspection and is required for many clinical genomics laboratory operations.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the validation study was formally completed and all results were finalized. Null if the study is still in-progress. Required for regulatory submission timelines and CLIA/CAP audit records.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pipeline validation study record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements for GxP-regulated data and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  data_privacy_classification varchar [note: 'Privacy classification of the genomic data used in the validation study. De-identified: PHI removed per HIPAA Safe Harbor. Pseudonymized: indirect identifiers replaced. Synthetic: computationally generated data. Identifiable: requires IRB approval and full PHI controls.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the root cause when validation_status is \'failed\'. Documents which acceptance criteria were not met and the suspected technical or procedural cause. Required for CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) initiation.']
  hpc_environment varchar [note: 'Compute environment in which the validation study pipeline runs were executed (e.g., AWS cloud HPC, on-premise HPC cluster, hybrid). Documents the infrastructure context for reproducibility and regulatory traceability.']
  irb_approval_number varchar [note: 'IRB protocol approval number when the validation study uses identifiable human genomic samples. Required for studies involving human subjects data to demonstrate ethical oversight compliance.']
  is_revalidation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this study is a revalidation triggered by a pipeline change, algorithm update, or prior validation failure (True), or an initial validation (False). Revalidations require documentation of the change that triggered them.']
  lod_established decimal(10,4) [note: 'The analytically established Limit of Detection (LOD) for the pipeline, expressed as variant allele frequency (VAF) or copies per milliliter depending on assay type. Represents the lowest detectable signal with 95% confidence. Core regulatory performance claim for IVD/LDT qualification.']
  lod_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the established LOD value (e.g., VAF — Variant Allele Frequency as a decimal, copies per mL for ctDNA, percent for mosaic variants). Required to correctly interpret the lod_established value.']
  minimum_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'Minimum sequencing coverage depth (read depth, expressed as ×) required and validated for the pipeline to meet performance specifications (e.g., 100× for somatic variant calling). Defines the analytical boundary conditions of the validated pipeline.']
  minimum_phred_score varchar [note: 'Minimum base quality Phred score threshold applied during the validation study (e.g., Q20, Q30). Defines the quality filtering boundary conditions under which the pipeline performance metrics were established.']
  pipeline_version_tag varchar [note: 'Semantic version label of the bioinformatics pipeline validated (e.g., v3.2.1, v4.0.0-rc1). Captured directly on this record to preserve the validated version string even if the pipeline registry is updated.']
  precision_cv_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Coefficient of Variation (CV%) measuring pipeline precision (reproducibility of quantitative outputs) across replicate runs. Lower CV indicates higher precision. Applicable for quantitative assays such as CNV ratio or VAF estimation.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the study protocol document used to execute this validation (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Captured to ensure the executed study is traceable to the exact approved protocol version, supporting GxP compliance.']
  reference_genome_build varchar [note: 'The reference genome assembly version used as the alignment and variant calling reference during the validation study (e.g., GRCh38, hg19). Critical for reproducibility and cross-study comparability of genomic results.']
  reference_standard varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the reference standard, truth set, or orthogonal method used to establish ground truth for the validation (e.g., NIST Genome in a Bottle NA12878, Sanger sequencing, ddPCR, Platinum Genomes). Required for accuracy and concordance claims.']
  regulatory_submission_ref varchar [note: 'External regulatory submission identifier associated with this validation study (e.g., FDA 510(k) number K231234, PMA number P200001, CE-IVD Technical File reference, EMA EPAR reference). Links the validation evidence to the formal regulatory dossier.']
  regulatory_submission_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory submission pathway for which this validation study provides supporting evidence. 510(k): FDA premarket notification. PMA: FDA premarket approval. De Novo: FDA novel device classification. CE-IVD: EU conformity assessment. EUA: Emergency Use Authorization. IVDR: EU In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation 2017/746. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 510k|PMA|De_Novo|CE-IVD|EUA|IVDR|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_use_classification varchar [note: 'Intended regulatory use classification of the pipeline being validated. IVD: In Vitro Diagnostic cleared/approved by FDA. LDT: Laboratory Developed Test under CLIA oversight. RUO: Research Use Only, not for clinical use. CE-IVD: EU conformity-marked IVD. EUA: Emergency Use Authorization.']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Total number of samples (or synthetic datasets) processed during the validation study. Determines statistical power of the validation and is reviewed by regulatory bodies to assess adequacy of the study design.']
  sensitivity_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Measured analytical sensitivity (true positive rate) of the pipeline expressed as a percentage (0–100). Calculated as TP / (TP + FN) × 100 across the validation sample set. Key regulatory performance metric for IVD/LDT claims.']
  specificity_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Measured analytical specificity (true negative rate) of the pipeline expressed as a percentage (0–100). Calculated as TN / (TN + FP) × 100 across the validation sample set. Key regulatory performance metric for IVD/LDT claims.']
  study_start_date date [note: 'The date on which formal execution of the validation study commenced (first sample or dataset processed per the study protocol). Distinct from the protocol authoring date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pipeline validation study record was most recently modified. Required for change tracking, audit trail compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the validation study. Draft: study protocol authored but not initiated. In-progress: active execution. Passed: all acceptance criteria met. Failed: one or more criteria not met. On-hold: execution paused. Cancelled: study terminated without completion.']
  validation_type varchar [note: 'Category of analytical validation being performed. Analytical sensitivity measures LOD; analytical specificity measures cross-reactivity; reproducibility measures inter-run/inter-site consistency; accuracy measures concordance with reference method; precision measures intra-run variability; interference measures matrix effects. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: analytical_sensitivity|analytical_specificity|reproducibility|accuracy|precision|interference|repeatability|comparability — promote to reference product]']
  variant_types_evaluated varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of genomic variant classes evaluated in this validation study (e.g., SNP, Indel, CNV, SV, fusion). Defines the analytical scope of the validation and maps to regulatory performance claims.']
  veeva_vault_study_reference varchar [note: 'System identifier for the validation study record as registered in Veeva Vault Quality Management System. Enables direct cross-system traceability between the analytics lakehouse record and the regulated document management system.']

  Note: 'Master record of formal analytical validation studies conducted on bioinformatics pipelines for IVD/LDT regulatory compliance — including study name, pipeline version validated, assay type, validation type (analytical sensitivity, specificity, reproducibility, accuracy), study protocol reference, validation status (in-progress, passed, failed), LOD established, regulatory submission reference (FDA, CE-IVD), and validation completion date. Required for CLIA/CAP and FDA IVD pipeline qualification.'
}

Links test catalog entries to their manufacturing process definitions for regulato']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Clinical tests originate from R&D projects. Required for 21 CFR Part 820 design history file traceability, validation documentation linkage, and regulatory submission (510(k)/PMA) evidence. Domain exp']
  reagent_qc_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.qc_specification. Business justification: A clinical test catalog entry mandates minimum reagent QC specifications (purity, concentration) acceptable for the assay. Required for regulatory submissions (FDA 510(k), LDT documentation) and test ']
  analytical_sensitivity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Analytical performance metric: percentage of true positive results correctly identified by the assay (true positive rate).']
  analytical_specificity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Analytical performance metric: percentage of true negative results correctly identified by the assay (true negative rate).']
  assay_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the genomic assay methodology. WGS=Whole Genome Sequencing, WES=Whole Exome Sequencing, targeted_panel=focused gene panel, SNP_array=Single Nucleotide Polymorphism array, CRISPR_based=CRISPR gene editing assay, qPCR=Quantitative Polymerase Chain Reaction, NGS_panel=Next-Generation Sequencing panel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WGS|WES|targeted_panel|SNP_array|CRISPR_based|qPCR|NGS_panel|cytogenetic|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assay_version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the assay configuration (e.g., v1.0, v2.1). Incremented when assay parameters, panel content, or pipeline change.']
  cap_accreditation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test is performed in a CAP-accredited laboratory. True=CAP accredited, False=not CAP accredited.']
  change_rationale varchar [note: 'Business and technical justification for the assay version change (e.g., improved sensitivity, updated gene panel, regulatory requirement, pipeline optimization).']
  clinical_validity_evidence varchar [note: 'Summary of evidence supporting the clinical validity of the test (e.g., peer-reviewed publications, clinical trial data, professional guidelines).']
  cpt_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit CPT billing code used for reimbursement and insurance claims for the clinical test.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this test catalog record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test catalog record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this assay version became active and available for clinical ordering.']
  intended_use_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the test. IVD=In Vitro Diagnostic (FDA-cleared or CE-marked for clinical use), RUO=Research Use Only (not for clinical decision-making), LDT=Laboratory Developed Test (CLIA-validated for clinical use).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this test catalog record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test catalog record was last updated.']
  limit_of_detection varchar [note: 'Analytical performance metric: the lowest quantity of analyte that can be reliably detected. Expressed with units (e.g., 5% variant allele frequency, 10 copies/mL).']
  ordering_physician_specialty varchar [note: 'Target physician specialties for which this test is intended (e.g., Medical Oncology, Genetic Counseling, Cardiology, Pediatrics). Multiple specialties may be listed.']
  panel_gene_list_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the gene panel content (applicable to targeted panels). Tracks which genes are included in this assay version.']
  phi_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test workflow and data handling are compliant with PHI protection requirements. True=compliant, False=not compliant.']
  price_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'List price of the clinical test in USD. Used for quotations, billing, and revenue recognition.']
  reflex_testing_rules varchar [note: 'Conditional logic for automatic follow-up testing based on initial results (e.g., if variant detected in gene X, reflex to confirmatory Sanger sequencing).']
  reimbursement_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test is eligible for insurance reimbursement. True=eligible, False=not eligible (patient pay only).']
  retired_date date [note: 'Date when this assay version was retired and no longer available for new orders. Nullable for currently active versions.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Accepted biological specimen types for the assay (e.g., whole blood, saliva, buccal swab, tissue biopsy, FFPE tissue, plasma, serum). Multiple types may be listed as comma-separated values.']
  test_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the clinical assay, including methodology, clinical indications, and intended use statement.']
  test_menu_category varchar [note: 'Organizational category for grouping tests in the clinical test menu (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Rare Disease, Pharmacogenomics, Infectious Disease).']
  test_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the clinical assay as displayed in test menus, requisition forms, and clinical reports.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test in the catalog. active=available for ordering, inactive=temporarily unavailable, pending_approval=awaiting regulatory or internal approval, discontinued=permanently retired, under_review=undergoing quality or compliance review.']
  turnaround_time_hours varchar [note: 'Committed turnaround time from specimen receipt to report delivery, measured in hours. Used for SLA tracking and customer expectations.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation state of the assay version. validated=analytical and clinical validation complete, in_validation=validation studies in progress, pending_validation=awaiting validation, failed_validation=did not meet acceptance criteria, not_required=validation not applicable.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master catalog of all IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) and LDT (Laboratory Developed Test) clinical assays offered by Genomics Biotech, including versioned assay configurations. Captures test code, test name, assay type (WGS, WES, targeted panel, SNP array, CRISPR-based), intended use (RUO vs IVD), regulatory clearance status (FDA 510(k)/PMA, CE-IVD), CLIA/CAP accreditation scope, specimen types accepted, TAT commitments, CPT billing codes, assay version history (version number, effective/retired dates, reference genome build, panel gene list version, pipeline version, validation status, change rationale), and reflex testing rules. SSOT for the clinical test menu and assay version traceability.'
}

  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory submission (FDA 510(k), PMA, or EU IVDR technical documentation) associated with this assay version. Links to regulatory approval documentation.']

Business justification: CAP/CLIA inspections require traceability from each deployed assay version to the validated research protocol specifying library prep, sequencing parameters, and bioinformatics pipeline. This ']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Assay versions require specific SKU linkage for lot traceability, regulatory compliance (21 CFR Part 820), inventory management, and configuration control. Critical for CLIA/CAP compliance and post-ma']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Each assay version is analytically validated against a specific product specification defining acceptance criteria (coverage depth, Q30, LOD). This FK is required for CLIA/CAP regulatory submissions a']
  test_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.test_catalog. Business justification: assay_version tracks versioned configurations of each clinical assay in the IVD test catalog. test_catalog is the authoritative master catalog entry. Each assay_version belongs to exactly one test_cat']
  validated_reagent_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Assay versions are validated against specific reagent batches; regulatory submissions (FDA 510k, LDT documentation) and change control records must cite the validated batch. Introducing a new batch re']
  analytical_validation_date date [note: 'Date when analytical validation study was completed and approved for this assay version. Required for regulatory submissions and laboratory accreditation.']
  analytical_validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the analytical validation study for this assay version. Completed and approved status required before version can be activated for clinical use. Tracks compliance with CLIA and CAP requirements.']
  annotation_database_version varchar [note: 'Version of the variant annotation database used for clinical interpretation (e.g., ClinVar version, dbSNP build, COSMIC version). Database version affects variant classification and clinical significance assignment.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this assay version was formally approved for clinical use. Part of the regulatory audit trail required for CLIA and CAP compliance.']
  change_rationale varchar [note: 'Business and technical justification for creating this new assay version. Documents reasons such as improved analytical performance, updated reference genome, regulatory requirement, or bug fix. Required for regulatory audit trail.']
  clia_complexity varchar [note: 'CLIA complexity categorization for this assay version. Waived tests have lowest complexity; high complexity tests require specialized personnel and quality systems. Determines laboratory accreditation requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this assay version record was first created in the system. Part of the data lineage and audit trail.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this assay version became or will become active for clinical use. All patient samples processed on or after this date use this version configuration.']
  intended_use_statement varchar [note: 'Regulatory-approved statement describing the clinical purpose and intended patient population for this assay version. Required for IVD labeling and marketing materials.']
  library_preparation_protocol varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the library preparation protocol used for sample processing in this assay version. Links to detailed SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) documentation.']
  lod_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the LOD value (e.g., percent allele fraction, copies per microliter, ng/mL). Provides context for interpreting the LOD value.']
  lod_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Analytical sensitivity expressed as the lowest quantity of analyte (e.g., variant allele fraction, copy number) that can be reliably detected. Critical performance characteristic for IVD assays.']
  minimum_coverage_depth varchar [note: 'Minimum sequencing read depth required for confident variant calling in this assay version. Expressed as number of reads covering a genomic position. Critical QC (Quality Control) parameter for NGS (Next-Generation Sequencing) assays.']
  minimum_phred_score varchar [note: 'Minimum base quality score (Phred score) threshold for variant calling. Higher scores indicate higher confidence in base calls. Typical clinical thresholds are Q30 or higher.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this assay version record was last updated. Tracks configuration changes and supports change control processes.']
  multiplexing_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy used for pooling multiple samples in a single sequencing run. Single index uses one barcode; dual index uses two barcodes for higher sample throughput and reduced index hopping.']
  panel_gene_list_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the curated gene list or panel used in this assay configuration. Tracks which genes are included in targeted sequencing or array-based assays. Essential for clinical validity documentation.']
  retired_date date [note: 'Date when this assay version was retired from active clinical use. Null if the version is still active or planned. Critical for traceability of which version was used for historical patient results.']
  sensitivity_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of true positive results correctly identified by the assay (true positives divided by true positives plus false negatives). Key analytical performance metric for clinical validity.']
  specificity_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of true negative results correctly identified by the assay (true negatives divided by true negatives plus false positives). Key analytical performance metric for clinical validity.']
  target_read_length varchar [note: 'Target length of sequencing reads in base pairs for this assay version. Affects ability to detect structural variants and map reads to repetitive regions.']
  test_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the test: IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) for FDA-cleared or CE-marked tests, LDT (Laboratory Developed Test) for CLIA-validated tests, or RUO (Research Use Only) for non-clinical tests.']
  turnaround_time_hours varchar [note: 'Target time from sample receipt to report delivery for this assay version, measured in hours. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) tracking and operational planning.']
  variant_calling_algorithm varchar [note: 'Name and version of the primary variant calling algorithm used in this assay version (e.g., GATK HaplotypeCaller, FreeBayes, VarScan). Algorithm choice affects sensitivity and specificity of variant detection.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the clinical assay configuration (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Follows semantic versioning convention to track major and minor changes.']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this assay version. Draft indicates under development; validation indicates undergoing analytical validation; active indicates approved for clinical use; deprecated indicates superseded but still in use; retired indicates no longer in use; withdrawn indicates removed from catalog due to regulatory or quality issues.']

  Note: 'Tracks versioned configurations of each clinical assay in the IVD test catalog — capturing assay version number, effective date, retired date, reference genome build (GRCh38/hg38, T2T), panel gene list version, bioinformatics pipeline version, analytical validation status, and change rationale. Enables traceability of which assay version was used for any given patient result. Supports regulatory audit and post-market surveillance requirements.'
}

  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: Clinical test orders must be performed under a valid regulatory approval (FDA clearance, CLIA waiver, LDT exemption). Required for billing, reimbursement, and regulatory audit trails. CAP/CLIA inspect']
  assay_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.assay_version. Business justification: test_order must reference the specific assay_version used to process the order. This is critical for clinical traceability — regulatory and CLIA requirements mandate knowing exactly which version of a']
  authorized_orderer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.authorized_orderer. Business justification: Test orders must link to the authorized orderer registry for credential verification, test menu access control, and regulatory compliance. Currently test_order only has ordering_physician_name and ord']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Test orders must reference catalog items for accurate billing, reimbursement code mapping (CPT/HCPCS), pricing, and revenue recognition. Essential for claims processing, revenue cycle management, and ']
  collection_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Test orders require a validated specimen collection/shipping address for cold-chain logistics and courier routing. Genomics labs must link orders to customer.address to enforce cold-chain eligibility ']
  complaint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.complaint. Business justification: Customer complaints about genomic test results, TAT failures, or specimen handling are always traceable to a specific test order in genomics lab operations. quality.complaint has account_id and sku_id']
  consumed_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material. Business justification: Test orders consume specific materials during execution; tracking material per order enables COGS calculation, inventory depletion, lot genealogy for regulatory traceability, and material usage varian']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the physician who ordered the clinical test. Links to physician master data.']

  ordering_accreditation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.accreditation. Real business need: LIMS workflow tracking, specimen consumption auditing, and regulatory compliance for clinical testing operations.']

It has catalog_item_id → product.catalog_item (cross-domain product catalog) but lacks a direct in-domain lin']
  accession_number varchar [note: 'Unique laboratory-assigned identifier for the specimen associated with this test order. Used for specimen tracking throughout the laboratory workflow.']
  accessioned_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the specimen was accessioned into the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS).']
  actual_tat_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual turnaround time in hours from specimen receipt to result reporting. Used for performance monitoring and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of billing and reimbursement for the clinical test order.']
