  research_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.protocol. Business justification: Production routings are industrialized versions of research protocols. Tech transfer process scales lab protocols to manufacturing SOPs. Essential for traceability in regulatory filings, process devia']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this routing is valid and executed. Routings are plant-specific in SAP PP.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Production routings for regulated IVD/genomics products are filed as part of regulatory submissions. Changes to routing require submission amendments. The existing plain-text `regulatory_submission_re']
  alternative_routing_number varchar [note: 'Reference to an alternative routing that can be used if this routing is unavailable. Enables flexible production planning and capacity management.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this routing was formally approved for production use. Part of the GMP document control audit trail.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the routing in the document control workflow. Only approved routings can be released for production use.']
  base_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Reference quantity for which the standard times in this routing are defined. Actual times are scaled proportionally based on order quantity.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the base quantity and lot sizes (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, L for liters). ISO standard unit codes.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the change control record that authorized the creation or last modification of this routing. Required for GMP traceability and audit compliance.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or context about this routing. Used for operational communication and knowledge transfer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this routing record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  gmp_classification varchar [note: 'GMP criticality classification of the routing. GMP-critical routings require enhanced documentation, validation, and electronic signatures per FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and EU GMP Annex 11.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this routing record was last updated. Tracks the currency of the routing data and supports change management.']
  last_validation_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent process validation study for this routing was completed and approved. Used to track validation currency.']
  lot_size_from decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Used in SAP PP to select the appropriate routing based on order quantity.']
  lot_size_to decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Enables lot-size-dependent routing selection in SAP PP.']
  mes_integration_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier used to link this routing to the shop-floor MES system for real-time production execution and data collection.']
  next_revalidation_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the routing must undergo revalidation to maintain its validated status. Periodic revalidation is required per GMP regulations.']
  operation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of manufacturing operations defined in this routing. Each operation represents a discrete production step with its own work center and times.']
  process_validation_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this routing requires formal process validation studies before use in commercial production. Required for IVD and RUO products per FDA and ISO 13485.']
  process_validation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of process validation for this routing. Validated status is required before the routing can be used for commercial GMP production.']
  requires_batch_record boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether production using this routing requires a formal batch manufacturing record (BMR) for traceability and compliance. Mandatory for GMP-regulated products.']
  requires_electronic_signature boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether operations in this routing require electronic signatures for GMP compliance. True for IVD and regulated product routings per FDA 21 CFR Part 11.']
  routing_group varchar [note: 'Group key that categorizes related routings together. Used in SAP PP to organize routings by product family or process type.']
  routing_group_counter varchar [note: 'Sequential counter within the routing group to distinguish multiple routings for the same material. Part of the composite routing key in SAP PP.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the routing. Externally-known unique code used in SAP PP for routing identification and work order generation.']
  routing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing. Active routings are available for work order generation, released routings are approved for production, locked routings are temporarily unavailable, and obsolete routings are retired.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the routing by its usage pattern. Standard routings are used for regular production, rate routings for repetitive manufacturing, reference routings as templates, and special routings for one-time or custom orders.']
  routing_usage varchar [note: 'SAP PP usage indicator defining the purpose of the routing (1=production, 2=maintenance, 3=quality inspection, etc.). Controls which business processes can reference this routing.']
  total_labor_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Sum of all labor times across all operations in the routing for the base quantity. Labor time represents manual work and scales with lot size.']
  total_machine_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Sum of all machine processing times across all operations in the routing for the base quantity. Machine time is equipment-dependent and scales with lot size.']
  total_setup_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Sum of all setup times across all operations in the routing. Setup time is independent of lot size and occurs once per production run.']
  total_standard_lead_time_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total manufacturing lead time in days required to complete all operations in this routing, including setup, processing, queue, and move times. Used for production planning and scheduling.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this routing becomes effective and can be used for work order generation. Part of the routing validity period.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this routing remains valid. Nullable for routings with indefinite validity. After this date, the routing cannot be used for new work orders.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this routing. Incremented with each approved change to maintain change history and support regulatory traceability.']

  Note: 'Master data defining the sequence of manufacturing operations (routing) required to produce a specific product, including standard times, work center assignments, and control keys. Captures routing number, routing group, material/product, plant, routing status (active, inactive), valid-from/valid-to dates, total standard lead time, operation sequence with operation numbers, work center per operation, standard setup/machine/labor times, GMP-critical operation flags, electronic signature requirement per operation, and MES integration reference. Serves as the manufacturing process template for work order generation and shop-floor execution. Integrates with SAP PP routing (CA01/CA02).'
}

The establishment registration defines the authorized manufacturing ']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the site record.']
  waste_disposal_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used for disposing of manufacturing waste.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for manufacturing_site. Referenced by manufacturing_site_id.'
}

Business justification: Manufacturing equipment is physically installed and operated within a specific work center (production line, assembly station, clean room). Linking equipment to work_center enables work-center-level e']

Table manufacturing.validation_run {
  validation_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the validation_run association']
  process_validation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the process validation study for which this batch serves as a validation run.']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the production batch designated as a validation run.']
  acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Whether this run met the acceptance criteria defined in the validation protocol. Values: ACCEPTED, REJECTED, PENDING_REVIEW. Belongs to the relationship because acceptance is evaluated against the specific study\'s protocol criteria, not the batch\'s general quality disposition.']
  deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of deviations recorded specifically during the execution of this batch as a validation run. Distinct from the production_batch.deviation_count which covers all batch deviations. Run-specific deviations are evaluated against the validation protocol\'s acceptance criteria.']
  run_date date [note: 'The date on which this production batch was executed as a validation run. May differ from the batch\'s manufacturing_start_date if the batch was produced for other purposes and subsequently designated as a validation run.']
  run_outcome varchar [note: 'The overall outcome of this specific validation run: PASS, FAIL, or INCONCLUSIVE. Distinct from batch disposition — a batch may be released (PASS disposition) but still FAIL a validation run criterion.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'The sequential order of this batch within the validation study (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Required by regulatory guidance to demonstrate consecutive run consistency. Belongs to the relationship, not to the batch or study alone.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal designation of a production batch as an executed run within a process validation study. It captures the operational and regulatory evidence generated when a specific production batch is used to demonstrate process consistency for a validation study. Each record links one process_validation study to one production_batch and carries run-specific attributes — sequence number, outcome, acceptance status, run date, and deviation count — that exist only in the context of this specific batch-within-study execution. Governed by ICH Q7, FDA Process Validation Guidance (2011), and EMA guidelines.. Existence Justification: In GMP biotech/genomics manufacturing, process validation studies formally designate specific production batches as \'validation runs\' — this is a regulated, actively managed business activity governed by ICH Q7 and FDA Process Validation Guidance (2011). One validation study requires multiple batches (typically 3 consecutive runs minimum per regulatory guidance), and one production batch can legitimately serve as a validation run for multiple concurrent studies (e.g., a batch validating both a new process parameter and a site transfer simultaneously). The \'Validation Run\' is a recognized regulatory and operational concept with its own data (run sequence, outcome, acceptance status) that belongs to neither the validation study nor the batch record alone.'
}

Table supply.supplier {
  supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier record. Primary key.']
  establishment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Suppliers of regulated materials must maintain FDA establishment registration and device listing. Required for supplier qualification audits, regulatory compliance verification, and supply chain trace']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the quality assurance manager or procurement director who approved the supplier.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier was formally approved for procurement following qualification and quality system assessment.']
  blocked_for_purchasing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether new purchase orders are blocked for this supplier due to quality issues, compliance violations, or financial concerns.']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for blocking purchasing activity with this supplier (e.g., quality hold, financial distress, regulatory non-compliance, contract dispute).']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification tier indicating the supplier\'s qualification status and procurement priority. GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice)-qualified suppliers meet regulatory standards for genomics manufacturing.']
  cold_chain_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has validated cold-chain logistics capabilities required for temperature-sensitive reagents and biological materials used in genomics workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supplier record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for transactions with this supplier.']
  diversity_classification varchar [note: 'Supplier diversity program classification (e.g., minority-owned, women-owned, veteran-owned, small business). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: minority_owned|women_owned|veteran_owned|small_business|disadvantaged_business|lgbtq_owned|disability_owned|none — promote to reference product]']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet identifier for the supplier entity, used for credit assessment and supplier risk management.']
  gmp_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds valid GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) certification required for supplying materials used in regulated genomics products.']

  headquarters_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the supplier\'s headquarters street address (suite, building, floor).']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City where the supplier\'s headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the supplier\'s headquarters location.']

  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal registered name of the supplier entity as it appears on contracts and regulatory filings.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the supplier relationship in the procurement system.']
  minimum_order_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum purchase order value required by the supplier, in the supplier\'s currency.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supplier record was last updated.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next supplier quality audit based on risk tier and audit frequency policy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional supplier information, special handling instructions, or procurement guidance.']
  on_time_delivery_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rolling 12-month percentage of purchase orders delivered on or before the requested delivery date. Key performance indicator (KPI) for supplier performance management.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred payment instrument for remitting payment to the supplier. ACH (Automated Clearing House) is the standard electronic payment method.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code defining the payment schedule and conditions for this supplier (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30).']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact at the supplier for procurement communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the supplier organization for procurement and supply chain coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the supplier business contact.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Current quality performance rating based on QC (Quality Control) results, COA (Certificate of Analysis) compliance, and nonconformance history.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Supply chain risk classification based on business continuity impact, single-source dependency, geographic risk, and financial stability. Critical tier indicates suppliers of materials with no qualified alternative source.']
  single_source_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supplier is the sole qualified source for one or more critical materials or components, representing elevated supply chain risk.']
  standard_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of calendar days from purchase order placement to delivery for standard items from this supplier. Used for demand planning and inventory management.']
  supplier_type varchar [note: 'Primary category of goods or services provided by the supplier. OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) indicates suppliers of sequencing instruments and genomics equipment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: raw_material|reagent|consumable|component|equipment|service|contract_manufacturer|distributor|oem — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the supplier entity (e.g., EIN, VAT number).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier relationship was formally terminated or the supplier status was changed to inactive.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Commercial or doing-business-as name used by the supplier in day-to-day operations.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the supplier as maintained in SAP MM vendor master. Used across procurement and supply chain operations.']

  Note: 'Master record for all suppliers and vendors providing raw materials, reagents, components, and services to Genomics Biotech. Captures supplier identity, classification (GMP-qualified, preferred, approved, conditional), regulatory certifications (ISO 13485, GMP), contact hierarchy, geographic footprint, payment terms, lead time profiles, and risk tier. SSOT for supplier identity across procurement and supply chain operations. Sourced from SAP MM vendor master.'
}

Table supply.material {
  material_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the material master record. Primary key.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: IVD reagents, kits, and devices require regulatory approval (510k, CE mark, etc.) before clinical use. Essential for material master compliance verification, QC release decisions, and ensuring only ap']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Materials that are also commercial products (finished goods, spare parts, consumables) should reference the product catalog. This links supply chain material master to commercial product catalog, enab']
  device_identifier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.device_identifier. Business justification: Medical devices and IVD products require UDI assignment for FDA GUDID submission and EU MDR compliance. Direct link needed for automated labeling generation, regulatory reporting, and traceability req']
  abc_indicator varchar [note: 'ABC classification for inventory management based on consumption value or criticality. A=High value/critical, B=Medium value/importance, C=Low value/routine. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARC-ABCIN).']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit in which stock of the material is managed (e.g., EA=Each, L=Liter, KG=Kilogram, ML=Milliliter). Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-MEINS).']
  batch_management_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material requires batch/lot number tracking for traceability and quality control. True for reagents, consumables, and materials subject to expiration or quality testing. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-XCHPF).']
  bom_relevance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this material is relevant for BOM (Bill of Materials) explosion and production planning. True if material is a component or assembly used in manufacturing.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique CAS Registry Number for chemical substances. Required for chemical inventory reporting and regulatory compliance (e.g., REACH, TSCA).']
  coa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Analysis must be provided by the supplier for each lot/batch of this material. True for reagents, chemicals, and biological materials.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the material master record was created. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-ERSDA).']
  deletion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is flagged for deletion. True if material is marked for archival and removal from active use. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-LVORM).']
  gmp_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the material is subject to GMP regulations. GMP=GMP-regulated material, NON_GMP=Not subject to GMP, GMP_CRITICAL=Critical GMP material requiring enhanced controls.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(13,3) [note: 'Gross weight of the material including packaging in kilograms. Used for logistics planning and freight calculation. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-BRGEW).']
  hazard_class varchar [note: 'Classification of material hazard level per GHS (Globally Harmonized System) for chemical safety and handling (e.g., Flammable Liquid Category 2, Acute Toxicity Category 3, Corrosive). Required for SDS (Safety Data Sheet) compliance.']
  industry_sector varchar [note: 'Industry sector classification for the material (e.g., P=Pharmaceuticals, C=Chemicals, M=Mechanical Engineering). Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-MBRSH).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the material master record. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-AENAM).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the material master record was last modified. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-LAEDA).']
  lot_size_procedure varchar [note: 'Procedure for calculating lot size in procurement and production (e.g., EX=Lot-for-lot, FX=Fixed lot size, HB=Replenishment to maximum stock level). Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARC-DISLS).']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Original manufacturer\'s part number or catalog number for the material. Used for cross-referencing and supplier communication.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short text description of the material for identification and reporting purposes. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MAKT-MAKTX).']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Grouping code for materials with similar characteristics for procurement and inventory management. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-MATKL).']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique material number used across procurement, manufacturing, and sales. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-MATNR).']
  material_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the material master record. ACTIVE=Available for use, INACTIVE=Temporarily unavailable, BLOCKED=Blocked for procurement/production, OBSOLETE=Phased out, PENDING_APPROVAL=Awaiting approval for release.']
  mrp_type varchar [note: 'MRP procedure used for planning this material (e.g., PD=MRP, VB=Manual reorder point, ND=No planning). Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARC-DISMM).']
  net_weight_kg decimal(13,3) [note: 'Net weight of the material excluding packaging in kilograms. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-NTGEW).']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Defines how the material is procured. E=External procurement (purchased), F=In-house production (manufactured), X=Both external and in-house. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARC-BESKZ).']
  product_hierarchy varchar [note: 'Hierarchical classification code for grouping materials into product families and categories for sales and marketing analysis. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-PRDHA).']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incoming goods of this material must undergo quality inspection before release to unrestricted stock. True for GMP-critical materials and reagents. Sourced from SAP QM Quality Management (MARC-QMATV).']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the material. RUO=Research Use Only, IVD=In Vitro Diagnostic (FDA-cleared), CE_IVD=CE-marked IVD (EU), LDT=Laboratory Developed Test, INVESTIGATIONAL=Under clinical investigation.']
  remaining_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Minimum remaining shelf life in days required at goods receipt. Materials with shorter remaining shelf life are rejected. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-MHDRZ).']
  serial_number_profile varchar [note: 'Profile defining serial number management requirements for the material. Used for instruments, flow cells, and high-value components requiring individual tracking. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-SERNP).']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Total shelf life of the material in days from manufacturing date. Used for expiration date calculation and inventory rotation (FEFO - First Expired First Out). Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-MHDHB).']
  storage_conditions varchar [note: 'Required storage conditions for the material including temperature range, humidity, and light exposure requirements (e.g., -20°C to -80°C for frozen reagents, 2°C to 8°C for refrigerated items, room temperature for stable materials).']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for cold-chain and temperature-sensitive materials. Critical for reagent stability and quality assurance.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for cold-chain and temperature-sensitive materials. Critical for reagent stability and quality assurance.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number for hazardous materials used in international transport and shipping documentation (e.g., UN1170 for ethanol).']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the material master record is valid and available for transactions. Used for new product introductions and material master effective dating.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which the material master record is valid. Used for material phase-out and obsolescence management. Null for materials with indefinite validity.']
  volume_liters decimal(13,3) [note: 'Volume of the material in liters. Used for storage space planning and logistics. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-VOLUM).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the material master record. Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA-ERNAM).']

  Note: 'Master record for all materials managed within the supply chain — including raw chemicals, biological reagents, flow cell components, array substrates, packaging materials, and finished goods SKUs. Captures material number, description, material type (ROH, HALB, FERT), base unit of measure, storage conditions (cold-chain temperature range, hazard class), shelf life, GMP classification, BOM relevance, and regulatory status (RUO, IVD). Sourced from SAP MM Material Master (MARA/MARC).'
}

Table supply.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase order record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Custom genomics projects (bespoke sequencing reagents, custom arrays) require customer-specific procurement where POs are raised against a specific customer account for cost allocation, project billin']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or service being procured on this purchase order line. Links to material master data.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plant. Business justification: purchase_order has plant_code as a STRING attribute, which is a denormalized reference to the supply.plant master. In SAP MM, every purchase order is issued for a specific plant. Replacing plant_code ']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: GMP procurement of regulated genomics/IVD materials requires POs to reference the regulatory submission authorizing the product for the target jurisdiction. Regulatory affairs and procurement teams ve']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_contract. Business justification: In SAP MM procurement, purchase orders are frequently released against a supplier contract or scheduling agreement. purchase_order currently has no FK to supplier_contract. Adding supplier_contract_id']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) who will fulfill this purchase order. Links to supplier master data.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was approved by authorized personnel. Marks transition from draft to committed procurement.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user ID of the person who approved the purchase order. Supports audit trail and Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) compliance.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was administratively closed after full receipt and invoice verification. Marks end of procurement lifecycle.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity confirmed by the supplier for delivery. May differ from ordered quantity based on supplier availability.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the purchase order expense will be charged. Enables Research and Development (R&D) vs. Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the purchase order value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Requested or confirmed delivery date for the purchase order line item. Critical for production planning and inventory management.']
  general_ledger_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account number for financial posting of the purchase order expense. Supports Profit and Loss (P&L) and balance sheet reporting.']
  gmp_relevant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order is subject to Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) regulations. True for materials used in In Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) or Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA) products.']
  goods_receipt_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether goods receipt posting is required for this purchase order line. True for physical materials, false for services.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and supplier (e.g., EXW, FOB, CIF, DDP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_receipt_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether invoice verification is required for this purchase order line. Controls accounts payable workflow.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the purchase order. Establishes ordering and uniqueness of line items within the PO.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates.']
  net_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total net value of the purchase order line item (ordered quantity × unit price), excluding tax and freight. Contributes to total PO value and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS).']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material or service units ordered from the supplier on this line item.']
  overdelivery_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Permitted percentage by which the supplier may exceed the ordered quantity without requiring approval. Accommodates supplier packaging constraints.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms code defining the payment schedule and discount conditions (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30).']
  po_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was created and issued to the supplier. Principal business event timestamp for procurement commitment.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique purchase order document number issued to supplier. Business identifier for procurement commitment.']
  po_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order in the procurement workflow. Tracks progression from creation through approval, fulfillment, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|released|partially_received|fully_received|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of purchase order by procurement model: standard (one-time), blanket (recurring), consignment (supplier-owned inventory), subcontracting (external processing), framework (long-term agreement), or service (non-material).']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Buyer or purchasing group responsible for this purchase order. Identifies the procurement specialist managing supplier relationships.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for procurement activities. Defines procurement authority and supplier relationships.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incoming goods receipt requires Quality Control (QC) inspection before release to inventory. Mandatory for critical reagents and consumables.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Cumulative quantity received to date via goods receipt transactions. Tracks fulfillment progress.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was released to the supplier for fulfillment. Starts the supplier Turnaround Time (TAT) clock.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Storage location code within the plant where received goods will be placed. Critical for cold-chain reagents and controlled materials.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to the purchase order line item. Calculated based on tax jurisdiction and material tax classification.']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of the purchase order line item including tax and other charges. Sum of net value and tax amount.']
  underdelivery_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Permitted percentage by which the supplier may deliver less than the ordered quantity without penalty. Manages supply risk for critical materials.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the ordered quantity. Common genomics units include EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), VIAL, PLATE, KIT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|BOX|VIAL|PLATE|KIT|SET|UNIT — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit of measure for the material or service. Basis for line item net value calculation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a formal procurement commitment issued to a supplier for materials or services. Header captures PO number, PO type (standard, blanket, consignment, subcontracting), supplier reference, plant/storage location, requested delivery date, incoterms, payment terms, total net value, currency, GMP-relevant flags, and approval workflow status. Line items (after po_line merge) capture material reference, ordered quantity, confirmed quantity, unit price, delivery schedule, storage location assignment, over/under-delivery tolerances, and goods receipt/invoice receipt indicators. The line item is the grain of this product. Each PO is the authoritative procurement document linking demand signals to supplier fulfillment. Sourced from SAP MM (EKKO/EKPO/EKET).'
}

Table supply.po_line {
  po_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase order line item. Primary key.']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.reagent_catalog. Business justification: Purchase order lines specify which reagent catalog item is procured. Enables procurement spend analytics by reagent type, supplier performance by product category, automated goods receipt validation—c']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record representing the specific reagent, consumable, component, or finished good being procured on this line.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plant. Business justification: po_line has plant_code as a STRING attribute, which is a denormalized reference to the supply.plant master. Each PO line item is destined for a specific plant in SAP MM. Replacing plant_code with a pr']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent purchase order header under which this line item is grouped.']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_contract. Business justification: Individual PO line items in SAP MM can reference specific contract line items (outline agreements). po_line currently has no FK to supplier_contract. Adding supplier_contract_id at the line level enab']
  account_assignment_category varchar [note: 'Defines how the procurement cost is assigned in financial accounting, such as cost center, internal order, project, or asset.']
  batch_management_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the material on this line requires batch or lot number tracking for traceability, quality control, and regulatory compliance.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the material requires temperature-controlled storage and transportation, essential for preserving the integrity of sensitive genomics reagents.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity confirmed by the supplier for delivery, which may differ from the ordered quantity due to availability or negotiation.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center to which the procurement expense for this line item is charged, enabling departmental cost tracking and analysis.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the individual who created this purchase order line item, supporting accountability and audit requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this purchase order line item record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the net price is denominated, such as USD, EUR, or GBP.']
  deletion_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this line item has been marked for deletion or cancellation, preventing further processing while preserving audit history.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'The scheduled or requested delivery date for the material on this purchase order line, critical for production planning and inventory management.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account number to which the procurement cost is posted, supporting financial reporting and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) visibility.']
  goods_receipt_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a goods receipt is required for this line item before invoice processing can occur, enforcing three-way match controls.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the material is classified as hazardous, requiring special handling, storage, and shipping procedures per safety regulations.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Standardized trade terms defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for shipping, insurance, and risk transfer, such as EXW, FOB, or DDP.']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'The specific geographic location or port associated with the Incoterms designation, clarifying the point of risk transfer.']
  invoice_receipt_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether an invoice is expected for this line item, controlling accounts payable processing workflows.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase order line item type, such as standard, consignment, subcontracting, or service, which determines procurement processing rules.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the purchase order, used for ordering and referencing individual line items.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order line item, tracking progression from creation through goods receipt, invoicing, and closure.']
  line_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value for this line item, calculated as ordered quantity multiplied by net price, before taxes and discounts.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'The original equipment manufacturer (OEM) part number for the material, used for precise identification and quality assurance in genomics reagent procurement.']
  material_group_code varchar [note: 'Classification code grouping similar materials together for procurement analysis, supplier negotiation, and spend visibility.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the individual who last modified this purchase order line item, enabling change accountability and compliance tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this purchase order line item record was last updated, enabling change tracking and audit compliance.']
  net_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The negotiated price per unit of measure for the material, excluding taxes and additional charges.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material ordered on this purchase order line, expressed in the unit of measure specified.']
  overdelivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable percentage by which the supplier may exceed the ordered quantity without requiring approval, supporting flexible receiving processes.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'The quantity unit to which the net price applies. For example, if price_unit is 100 and net_price is 50.00, the price is 50.00 per 100 units.']
  shelf_life_expiration_date date [note: 'The date by which the material must be used or disposed of, critical for managing reagent and consumable inventory in genomics laboratories.']
  short_text varchar [note: 'Additional free-form text providing supplementary information or special instructions for this purchase order line item.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'The specific storage location or warehouse area within the plant where the material will be received and stored upon delivery.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax classification code applied to this line item, determining the applicable tax rate and jurisdiction.']
  underdelivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable percentage by which the supplier may deliver less than the ordered quantity without triggering a shortage alert or requiring follow-up.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the material quantity is expressed, such as EA (each), L (liters), KG (kilograms), or BOX.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a purchase order representing a specific material, quantity, unit price, delivery schedule, and storage location assignment. Captures line number, material reference, ordered quantity, confirmed quantity, unit of measure, net price, delivery date, goods receipt indicator, invoice receipt indicator, and over/under-delivery tolerances. Enables granular tracking of multi-material procurement events. Sourced from SAP MM (EKPO/EKET).'
}

Table supply.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for goods_receipt']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the customer master for goods issues related to sales orders or customer returns.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Goods receipts create or reference batch records for batch-managed materials. The goods_receipt table has batch_number (STRING) but no FK to batch. Adding batch_id FK enables joining to batch master f']
  coa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.coa. Business justification: GMP goods receipts in genomics biotech are accompanied by Certificates of Analysis that must be validated before material release. A proper FK to reagent.coa enables automated CoA validation at receip']
  device_identifier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.device_identifier. Business justification: GMP receiving inspection for regulated IVD/genomics products requires UDI device identifier verification at goods receipt. FDA UDI rule and EU MDR mandate UDI scanning/verification during receiving. T']
  inbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_delivery. Business justification: Goods receipts are posted against inbound delivery documents in the logistics flow. The goods_receipt table has delivery_document_number (STRING) and delivery_line_item (INT) but no FK to inbound_deli']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Goods receipt creates or updates reagent lot in inventory. GMP operations require traceability from material document to specific lot for audit trails, deviation investigations, and batch record recon']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the material master. Identifies the specific reagent, consumable, instrument component, or finished good involved in the movement.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the plant (manufacturing site or distribution center) where the goods movement occurred.']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.po_line. Business justification: A goods receipt in SAP MM is posted against a specific PO line item, not just the PO header. goods_receipt already has purchase_order_id but lacks po_line_id, making it impossible to trace which speci']
  production_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_batch. Business justification: When a genomics manufacturing production batch is completed, a goods receipt posts it into inventory. GMP traceability requires linking the goods receipt to the production batch for batch disposition,']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Goods receipts are posted against purchase orders in SAP MM. The goods_receipt table currently has purchase_order_number (STRING) and purchase_order_line_item (INT) but no FK to purchase_order. Adding']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the storage location within the plant (e.g., warehouse zone, cold storage room, quarantine area).']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the supplier master for goods receipts from external vendors. Critical for supplier performance tracking and quality management.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.warehouse_location. Business justification: When materials are received (goods receipt posted), they are physically placed in a specific warehouse location (bin/rack). goods_receipt has storage_location_id (reagent domain) and plant_id but no F']
  cold_chain_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the cold chain temperature monitoring log for temperature-sensitive genomics reagents and biologics. Critical for ensuring material integrity and regulatory compliance.']
  company_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the company code currency used in consolidated reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the material document was created. Part of the immutable audit trail for GxP and SOX compliance.']
  document_date date [note: 'Date on the source document (e.g., delivery note, production order). May differ from posting date when documents are processed retrospectively.']
  entry_date date [note: 'Date when the material document was created in the system. Used for audit trail and document lifecycle tracking.']
  gmp_batch_record_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the GMP batch manufacturing record for materials issued to or received from production. Mandatory for FDA-regulated manufacturing operations.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the local plant currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  material_document_line_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the material document. Enables tracking of individual material movements within a single document.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP material document number (MBLNR) that uniquely identifies the goods movement transaction in the source ERP system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trails.']
  material_document_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the material document was posted. Part of the composite key in SAP MM along with material document number.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'Three-digit SAP movement type code that classifies the nature of the goods movement. Examples: 101 (GR against PO), 261 (goods issue to production), 301 (plant-to-plant transfer), 122 (return to supplier), 551 (scrapping).']
  movement_type_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the movement type for reporting and user interfaces.']
  movement_value_company_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of the goods movement in company code currency for consolidated financial reporting. Confidential for competitive pricing protection.']
  movement_value_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of the goods movement in local plant currency. Used for inventory valuation, COGS calculation, and financial reporting. Confidential for competitive pricing protection.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the goods movement was posted to inventory and financial ledgers. Critical for period-end closing, COGS calculation, and financial reporting.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material moved in base unit of measure. Positive for receipts/inbound movements, negative for issues/outbound movements.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the business reason for the goods movement (e.g., quality defect, expiration, process deviation, customer return). Critical for root cause analysis and quality investigations.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the movement reason for reporting and quality review.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this goods movement is a reversal of a previous transaction. True if this is a reversal document.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'Material document number of the original transaction that this document reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is true.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'SAP special stock type: E (sales order stock), K (consignment stock), O (project stock), Q (quality inspection stock), W (blocked stock). Critical for inventory segregation and financial reporting.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the quantity (e.g., EA for each, L for liters, KG for kilograms). Follows ISO standards for laboratory and manufacturing materials.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Split valuation type for materials with multiple valuation classes (e.g., RUO vs IVD grade reagents). Enables separate inventory valuation for materials with different quality grades or regulatory classifications.']
  vendor_number varchar [note: 'SAP vendor account number. Retained for cross-system reconciliation and legacy reporting.']

  Note: 'Transactional ledger of all material movements within the Genomics Biotech supply chain — including goods receipts against POs (mvt 101), goods issues to production (mvt 261), stock transfers between plants/locations (mvt 301/311), returns to supplier (mvt 122), and scrapping events (mvt 551). Captures movement type, posting date, material, plant, storage location, batch, quantity, movement value, reference document (PO, production order, delivery), cold-chain temperature log reference, COA reference, and movement reason code. Provides the single authoritative material ledger for GMP audit trails, COGS calculation, and full lot traceability. Sourced from SAP MM (MSEG/MKPF).'
}

Table supply.inventory_stock {
  inventory_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory stock record. Primary key for the inventory stock data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Genomics biotech companies place reagents and consumables on consignment at strategic customer accounts (hospitals, core labs). inventory_stock must identify which customer account holds consignment s']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.batch. Business justification: Inventory stock positions are tracked by batch for batch-managed materials. The inventory_stock table has batch_number (STRING) but no FK to batch. Adding batch_id FK enables joining to batch master f']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Links ERP inventory stock to quality-controlled reagent lot (CoA-released unit). Enables real-time expiry monitoring, FEFO execution, quality hold propagation across systems—essential for genomics bio']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record representing the genomics product, reagent, consumable, or instrument component being stocked.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing or distribution plant where the material is stocked.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Inventory stock positions may reference the originating purchase order for procurement traceability and COGS analysis. The inventory_stock table has purchase_order_number (STRING) but no FK to purchas']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific storage location within the plant where the material is physically stored.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier from whom this batch was procured. Used for supplier performance tracking and traceability.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.warehouse_location. Business justification: inventory_stock tracks stock positions at plant and storage location level but lacks a direct FK to warehouse_location. In a genomics biotech supply chain with cold-chain and GMP zone requirements, kn']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_order. Business justification: Inventory stock reservations in genomics biotech are made against specific work orders for material staging. MRP reservation management requires linking reserved stock to the work order that triggered']
  abc_indicator varchar [note: 'ABC classification for inventory management. A = high-value/critical, B = moderate, C = low-value/non-critical. Used for prioritization and cycle counting.']
  available_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Calculated available quantity: unrestricted stock minus reserved stock. Represents stock available for new commitments.']
  blocked_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material blocked for use due to quality issues, regulatory hold, or other restrictions. Requires disposition decision.']
  certificate_of_analysis_number varchar [note: 'Certificate of Analysis document number provided by the vendor for this batch. Contains quality test results and specifications.']
  cold_chain_storage_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature-controlled storage zone assignment for materials requiring cold-chain handling. Critical for reagent stability and GMP compliance.']
  consignment_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material held on consignment from a vendor. Ownership transfers only upon consumption.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory stock record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the stock value amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CNY|CHF|AUD|CAD|SGD — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical cycle count for this stock record. Used to ensure inventory accuracy.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Shelf-life expiration date for the batch. Critical for reagents, consumables, and biologics with limited stability. Must be monitored to prevent use of expired materials.']
  gmp_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this material is subject to GMP controls requiring batch-level traceability, quality documentation, and regulatory compliance.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this material is classified as hazardous, requiring special handling, storage, and Safety Data Sheet (SDS) compliance.']
  in_transit_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material currently in transit between plants or storage locations. Not yet available at the destination.']
  last_goods_movement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent goods movement (receipt, issue, transfer) for this stock record. Used to identify slow-moving or obsolete inventory.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory stock record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trails.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the batch was manufactured or produced. Used for shelf-life calculation and traceability.']
  maximum_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum stock level allowed at this location. Used to prevent overstocking and optimize working capital.']
  mrp_controller_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the MRP controller or planner responsible for managing replenishment of this material.']
  quality_inspection_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material currently under quality inspection or Quality Control (QC) testing. Cannot be used until QC release.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the material was received into the storage location. Used for FIFO/FEFO inventory management.']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum stock level that triggers a replenishment order. Used in automated procurement planning to ensure uninterrupted supply.']
  reserved_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of unrestricted stock reserved for specific production orders, sales orders, or projects. Committed but not yet issued.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Buffer stock maintained to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Critical for high-priority genomics inputs.']
  serialized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this material is managed at the individual serial number level (e.g., instruments, high-value equipment).']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stock position snapshot was captured. Supports historical stock analysis and trend reporting.']
  stock_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the stock record. Indicates availability, alerts, or restrictions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AVAILABLE|LOW_STOCK|OUT_OF_STOCK|EXPIRING_SOON|EXPIRED|BLOCKED|QUARANTINE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'Classification of stock ownership and purpose. Distinguishes between own stock, consignment, project-specific stock, and other special stock types.']
  stock_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the stock at this location, calculated using the material valuation price. Critical for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) visibility.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for stock quantities. Common genomics units include EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), ML (milliliter), UL (microliter), VIAL, KIT, PLATE. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|ML|UL|G|MG|BOX|KIT|VIAL|PLATE|TUBE|ROLL — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unrestricted_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material available for unrestricted use. This stock can be issued for production, sales orders, or other consumption without restrictions.']
  valuation_price_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or moving average price per unit used for inventory valuation. Basis for COGS calculation.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Method used for inventory valuation. Standard valuation uses a single price; split valuation allows different prices for different stock segments.']

  Note: 'Current and historical stock position for each material at each plant and storage location. Captures unrestricted stock, quality inspection stock, blocked stock, consignment stock, and in-transit stock quantities. Includes batch-level granularity for GMP-controlled materials, expiry date tracking, cold-chain storage zone assignment, and reorder point/safety stock parameters. Supports COGS visibility and uninterrupted supply assurance for critical genomics inputs. Sourced from SAP MM (MARD/MCHB/MBEW).'
}

Table supply.stock_movement {
  stock_movement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stock movement transaction. Primary key for the stock movement log.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer if this movement is a goods issue for sales order fulfillment or customer return.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the batch or lot number for batch-managed materials. Critical for GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) traceability and Certificate of Analysis (COA) linkage.']
  inbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_delivery. Business justification: stock_movement currently stores delivery_number as a denormalized STRING reference to the inbound delivery document. Replacing this with a proper FK inbound_delivery_id to supply.inbound_delivery.inbo']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reagent.lot. Business justification: Stock movements (issues, transfers, adjustments) must trace to specific reagent lots for GMP compliance. Lot-level traceability required for batch records, experiment reproducibility, regulatory inspe']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material (raw material, reagent, consumable, or finished good) involved in this stock movement.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing or distribution plant where the stock movement occurred.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Stock movements (goods receipts, transfers, issues) often reference the originating purchase order for procurement-related movements. The stock_movement table has purchase_order_number (STRING) and pu']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific storage location (warehouse zone, cold storage, quarantine area) within the plant.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor if this movement is a goods receipt from external procurement.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_order. Business justification: Material consumption stock movements (goods issues) in genomics biotech are posted against work orders for cost accounting and GMP material traceability. production_order_number on stock_movement is a']
  amount_in_company_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total value of the stock movement converted to the company code\'s reporting currency. Used for consolidated financial reporting and Profit and Loss (P&L) analysis.']
  amount_in_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total value of the stock movement in the plant\'s local currency. Used for inventory valuation and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation. Derived from material price and quantity.']
  company_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the company\'s reporting currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this stock movement record was first created in the data warehouse. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date printed on the source document (e.g., delivery note date, production order completion date). May differ from posting date.']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the material document was created in the system. Used for audit trail and Turnaround Time (TAT) analysis.']
  entry_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used during transaction entry. Supports multi-unit scenarios (e.g., ordering in cases but stocking in units).']
  general_ledger_account varchar [note: 'The General Ledger account number to which the inventory value change is posted. Links stock movement to financial accounting.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the local currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  material_document_line_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the material document. Uniquely identifies this movement within the document header.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP material document number (MBLNR) that groups one or more movement line items into a single posting transaction. Serves as the business identifier for audit and reconciliation.']
  movement_indicator varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the movement direction: receipt (inbound), issue (outbound), or transfer (location-to-location).']
  movement_type_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit SAP movement type code that classifies the nature of the stock movement (e.g., 101=Goods Receipt from PO, 261=Goods Issue to Production Order, 311=Transfer Posting, 551=Scrapping). Determines accounting impact and inventory valuation logic.']
  movement_type_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the movement type (e.g., Goods Receipt, Goods Issue, Stock Transfer, Return, Scrapping).']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the stock movement was posted to the material ledger and financial accounting. Used for period-end closing and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material moved, expressed in the base unit of measure. Positive for receipts, negative for issues in some reporting contexts.']
  quantity_in_entry_unit decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity in the unit of measure used during data entry (e.g., purchase order unit). May differ from base unit for conversion tracking.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the business reason for the stock movement (e.g., quality defect, expiration, customer return, process scrap). Used for root cause analysis and Quality Assurance (QA) investigations.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the movement reason code.']
  reservation_line_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the reservation document.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the material reservation document if this movement fulfills a reservation (e.g., for production or project consumption).']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this movement is a reversal (cancellation) of a previous material document. True if reversal, False otherwise.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'The material document number that this movement reverses. Populated only if reversal_indicator is True.']
  sales_order_line_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the sales order. Identifies the specific material line being shipped.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales order if this movement is a goods issue for customer delivery. Links to Order Management (SD) process.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'Single-character code indicating special stock scenarios (e.g., consignment stock, project stock, sales order stock). Blank for standard stock.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stock status: unrestricted (available for use), quality_inspection (under QC testing), blocked (quarantined), or restricted (conditionally available). Critical for GMP compliance and Quality Control (QC) workflows.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the quantity is expressed (e.g., EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, L=Liter, BOX=Box). Aligns with ISO unit codes where applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this stock movement record was last updated in the data warehouse. Used for change tracking and incremental load processing.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Sub-classification of material for split valuation purposes (e.g., different pricing for domestic vs. imported batches). Blank if not used.']

  Note: 'Transactional log of every material movement within the supply chain — including goods receipts, goods issues, stock transfers, returns, and scrapping events. Captures movement type, posting date, material, plant, storage location, batch, quantity, value, reference document (PO, production order, delivery), and movement reason. Provides full material ledger traceability required for GMP audit trails and COGS calculation. Sourced from SAP MM (MSEG/MKPF).'
}

Table supply.batch {
  batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production or procurement batch. Primary key for the batch master record.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_approval. Business justification: GMP batch release requires verification that batch specifications conform to approved regulatory dossier. Critical for QC disposition decisions, certificate of analysis generation, and ensuring batche']
  device_identifier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.device_identifier. Business justification: FDA UDI rule and EU MDR require lot/batch-level UDI-PI traceability linking each batch to its UDI device identifier. material has device_identifier_id for product-level UDI, but batch-level UDI tracea']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for which this batch was produced or procured. Links to the material product in the supply domain.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where the batch was produced. Links to the facility master in the supply or manufacturing domain.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order under which this batch was procured. Null for internally manufactured batches.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier master record if the batch was externally procured. Null for internally manufactured batches.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary warehouse location where the batch is stored. Links to warehouse master in the supply domain.']
  available_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Current unrestricted quantity available for use or shipment from this batch. Updated in real-time as inventory is consumed or reserved.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing batch or lot number assigned to the production or procurement batch. Used for traceability and quality control. Also known as lot number.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the batch indicating its current state in inventory and usability.']
  blocked_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity from this batch that is blocked for quality or compliance reasons and cannot be used or shipped until released.']
  coa_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the Certificate of Analysis document that certifies the batch meets quality specifications. Required for GMP compliance and customer shipments.']
  cold_chain_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch has been maintained within required cold-chain temperature ranges throughout its lifecycle. False indicates a temperature excursion event occurred.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the batch was manufactured or procured. Required for customs, trade compliance, and regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the batch should not be used due to potential degradation or loss of efficacy. Mandatory for reagents, consumables, and IVD products.']
  gmp_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the batch was manufactured under GMP, non-GMP, or GMP-equivalent conditions. Critical for regulatory compliance and product classification.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, storage, and shipping procedures.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified the batch record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch record was last updated in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Date on which the batch was manufactured or produced. Critical for shelf-life calculation and expiry determination.']
  origin varchar [note: 'Source of the batch indicating whether it was produced internally, procured from an external supplier, manufactured by a contract manufacturer, reworked from a previous batch, or is a sample batch.']
  qc_release_date date [note: 'Date on which the batch was officially released by Quality Assurance for use or distribution. Null if batch is not yet released.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Current quality control release status of the batch. Only released batches are available for use or shipment. Managed by Quality Management module.']
  quarantine_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity from this batch that is in quality inspection and not yet released for use. Managed by Quality Management.']
  recall_date date [note: 'Date on which the recall was initiated for this batch. Null if no recall has been issued.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch is subject to a product recall. True triggers immediate inventory blocking and customer notification workflows.']
  recall_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the batch was recalled. Includes quality defect description, safety concern, or regulatory non-compliance issue.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory category of the batch indicating its intended use and compliance requirements. IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic), RUO (Research Use Only), GMP API (Good Manufacturing Practice Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient).']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date by which the batch must be retested to confirm continued quality and stability. Applicable to raw materials and intermediates.']
  sds_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the Safety Data Sheet associated with this batch. Required for hazardous materials and chemicals.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Total shelf life of the batch in days from manufacturing date to expiry date. Used for inventory planning and FEFO (First Expired First Out) logic.']
  size_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity produced or procured in this batch. Expressed in the base unit of measure for the material.']
  size_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the batch size quantity. Examples: EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), ML (milliliter).']
  sterilization_date date [note: 'Date on which the batch was sterilized. Null if sterilization is not applicable.']
  sterilization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to sterilize the batch if applicable. Critical for consumables and reagents used in clinical or diagnostic applications.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for the batch to maintain quality and stability. Defines temperature and environmental requirements.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the batch. Exceeding this threshold may compromise batch quality.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the batch. Used for cold-chain monitoring and compliance.']
  supplier_batch_number varchar [note: 'Original batch or lot number assigned by the supplier for externally procured materials. Used for supplier traceability and quality investigations.']

  Note: 'Master record for a specific production or procurement batch of a material, enabling full lot traceability from supplier through manufacturing to customer delivery. Captures batch number, material reference, manufacturing date, expiry date, COA number, QC release status, storage conditions, cold-chain compliance flag, GMP classification, and batch origin (internal production vs. external procurement). Critical for GxP compliance, recall management, and reagent/consumable quality assurance. Sourced from SAP MM/QM (MCH1/MCHA).'
}

Table supply.inbound_delivery {
  inbound_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inbound delivery document. Primary key for the inbound delivery entity.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order that triggered this inbound delivery. Links delivery to procurement document.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Import clearance for regulated genomics/IVD products (sequencing reagents, diagnostic kits) requires referencing the import license or regulatory submission. inbound_delivery already tracks customs_cl']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor who is shipping the materials. Primary counterparty for this inbound delivery.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Genomics Biotech facility, warehouse, or laboratory receiving the inbound shipment.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the shipment physically arrived at the receiving dock. Critical for TAT (Turnaround Time) measurement and SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-issued document serving as receipt of goods and contract of carriage. Required for international shipments and freight claims.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier or freight forwarder responsible for transporting the shipment.']
  cold_chain_device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the temperature monitoring device attached to the shipment. Essential for reagent and consumable shipments requiring temperature control per GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice).']
  commercial_invoice_number varchar [note: 'Supplier invoice number associated with this delivery. Used for three-way match in accounts payable (invoice, PO, goods receipt).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inbound delivery record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  customs_clearance_date date [note: 'Date when customs clearance was completed for international shipments. Impacts delivery lead time and TAT (Turnaround Time).']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of customs clearance process for international shipments. Critical for cross-border deliveries of reagents, instruments, and consumables.']
  delivery_note_text varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions related to the inbound delivery. Captures handling requirements, supplier communications, or receiving observations.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the inbound delivery document. Used for tracking and communication with suppliers.']
